Benchmark Results for gpt-5.4-mini-HighReasoning

Test 1: Travelling Salesman Problem Solver (C++) Score: 0.00 / 21
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP) in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY number of cities from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 10-40 cities (brute-force feasible for smallest cases)
- **Medium**: 100-1000 cities (requires heuristics)
- **Large**: 5000-100000 cities (requires efficient heuristics)
- **Extreme**: 500000-5000000 cities (requires very fast approximations)
- **Epic**: 10000000+ cities (requires highly optimized approximations)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with city counts ranging from 10 to 10000000. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N (number of cities)
Lines 2..N+1: x y coordinates (floats)

**Output format (stdout):**
One line with N integers: the visit order of city indices (0-indexed), starting at city 0.
Do NOT include returning to city 0 (return is implicit).

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on problem size
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 300 seconds even for 1000,000,000 cities
3. **Quality**: Should produce reasonable routes at all scales
4. **Correctness**: Output must be a valid permutation of 0..N-1 starting with 0

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function that reads stdin and writes stdout.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on N.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: 10 Cities
View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a list of cities, find the shortest round-trip route that visits every city exactly once and returns to the start. This is one of the most famous hard problems in computer science — checking every possible route is only feasible for a handful of cities, so the AI must use clever shortcuts to find a good route quickly.

Subpasses scale from 10 cities up to 1 000, so brute-force is impossible for the larger cases. The AI's route length is compared against a simple baseline; beating it earns full marks.

 0.0000
[10 cities]

TSP Solver - 10 Cities

View Code (8809 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <array>
#include <bit>
#include <charconv>
#include <cmath>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstdio>
#include <cstdlib>
#include <cstring>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <numeric>
#include <system_error>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

struct Point {
    float x;
    float y;
};

static inline double dist2(const Point& a, const Point& b) {
    double dx = double(a.x) - double(b.x);
    double dy = double(a.y) - double(b.y);
    return dx * dx + dy * dy;
}

static inline uint64_t splitBy1(uint32_t x) {
    uint64_t v = x;
    v = (v | (v << 16)) & 0x0000FFFF0000FFFFULL;
    v = (v | (v << 8)) & 0x00FF00FF00FF00FFULL;
    v = (v | (v << 4)) & 0x0F0F0F0F0F0F0F0FULL;
    v = (v | (v << 2)) & 0x3333333333333333ULL;
    v = (v | (v << 1)) & 0x5555555555555555ULL;
    return v;
}

static inline uint64_t mortonKey(float x, float y, float minX, float maxX, float minY, float maxY) {
    constexpr uint32_t scale = (1u << 21) - 1u;
    double nx = 0.5;
    double ny = 0.5;
    double dx = double(maxX) - double(minX);
    double dy = double(maxY) - double(minY);
    if (dx > 0.0) nx = (double(x) - double(minX)) / dx;
    if (dy > 0.0) ny = (double(y) - double(minY)) / dy;
    if (nx < 0.0) nx = 0.0;
    if (nx > 1.0) nx = 1.0;
    if (ny < 0.0) ny = 0.0;
    if (ny > 1.0) ny = 1.0;
    uint32_t ix = uint32_t(nx * scale + 0.5);
    uint32_t iy = uint32_t(ny * scale + 0.5);
    return splitBy1(ix) | (splitBy1(iy) << 1);
}

struct FastInput {
    static constexpr size_t BUFSIZE = 1 << 20;
    char buf[BUFSIZE];
    size_t idx = 0, size = 0;

    inline char getChar() {
        if (idx >= size) {
            size = fread(buf, 1, BUFSIZE, stdin);
            idx = 0;
            if (size == 0) return 0;
        }
        return buf[idx++];
    }

    template <class T>
    bool readInt(T& out) {
        char c = getChar();
        if (!c) return false;
        while (c != '-' && (c < '0' || c > '9')) {
            c = getChar();
            if (!c) return false;
        }
        T sign = 1;
        if (c == '-') {
            sign = -1;
            c = getChar();
        }
        T val = 0;
        for (; c >= '0' && c <= '9'; c = getChar()) val = val * 10 + (c - '0');
        out = val * sign;
        return true;
    }

    bool readDouble(double& out) {
        char c = getChar();
        if (!c) return false;
        while ((c < '0' || c > '9') && c != '-' && c != '.') {
            c = getChar();
            if (!c) return false;
        }
        char tmp[128];
        size_t p = 0;
        while (c && c != ' ' && c != '\n' && c != '\r' && c != '\t') {
            if (p + 1 < sizeof(tmp)) tmp[p++] = c;
            c = getChar();
        }
        auto res = from_chars(tmp, tmp + p, out);
        if (res.ec != errc()) out = 0.0;
        return true;
    }
};

static vector<int> exactSmallTSP(const vector<Point>& pts) {
    int n = (int)pts.size();
    if (n == 1) return {0};

    int m = n - 1;
    int total = 1 << m;
    const double INF = numeric_limits<double>::infinity();

    vector<double> dp((size_t)total * m, INF);
    vector<int> parent((size_t)total * m, -1);
    auto at = [&](int mask, int i) -> double& { return dp[(size_t)mask * m + i]; };
    auto par = [&](int mask, int i) -> int& { return parent[(size_t)mask * m + i]; };

    for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) at(1 << i, i) = dist2(pts[0], pts[i + 1]);

    for (int mask = 1; mask < total; ++mask) {
        for (int last = 0; last < m; ++last) {
            if (!(mask & (1 << last))) continue;
            double cur = at(mask, last);
            if (!isfinite(cur)) continue;
            int rem = ((total - 1) ^ mask);
            while (rem) {
                int bit = rem & -rem;
                int nxt = (int)countr_zero((unsigned)bit);
                int nmask = mask | bit;
                double cand = cur + dist2(pts[last + 1], pts[nxt + 1]);
                double& dst = at(nmask, nxt);
                if (cand < dst) {
                    dst = cand;
                    par(nmask, nxt) = last;
                }
                rem ^= bit;
            }
        }
    }

    double best = INF;
    int bestLast = -1;
    int full = total - 1;
    for (int last = 0; last < m; ++last) {
        double cand = at(full, last) + dist2(pts[last + 1], pts[0]);
        if (cand < best) {
            best = cand;
            bestLast = last;
        }
    }

    vector<int> route;
    route.reserve(n);
    route.push_back(0);
    int mask = full;
    int last = bestLast;
    vector<int> rev;
    rev.reserve(m);
    while (mask) {
        rev.push_back(last + 1);
        int pl = par(mask, last);
        mask ^= 1 << last;
        last = pl;
    }
    reverse(rev.begin(), rev.end());
    route.insert(route.end(), rev.begin(), rev.end());
    return route;
}

static vector<uint64_t> makeKeys(const vector<Point>& pts) {
    float minX = numeric_limits<float>::max();
    float maxX = -numeric_limits<float>::max();
    float minY = numeric_limits<float>::max();
    float maxY = -numeric_limits<float>::max();
    for (const auto& p : pts) {
        minX = min(minX, p.x);
        maxX = max(maxX, p.x);
        minY = min(minY, p.y);
        maxY = max(maxY, p.y);
    }
    vector<uint64_t> keys(pts.size());
    for (size_t i = 0; i < pts.size(); ++i) keys[i] = mortonKey(pts[i].x, pts[i].y, minX, maxX, minY, maxY);
    return keys;
}

static vector<int> radixSortOrder(const vector<uint64_t>& keys) {
    int n = (int)keys.size();
    vector<int> order(n), tmp(n);
    iota(order.begin(), order.end(), 0);
    constexpr int BITS = 16;
    constexpr int BUCKETS = 1 << BITS;
    vector<int> cnt(BUCKETS);

    for (int pass = 0; pass < 4; ++pass) {
        fill(cnt.begin(), cnt.end(), 0);
        int shift = pass * BITS;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) ++cnt[(keys[order[i]] >> shift) & (BUCKETS - 1)];
        int sum = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < BUCKETS; ++i) {
            int c = cnt[i];
            cnt[i] = sum;
            sum += c;
        }
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            int id = order[i];
            int b = (keys[id] >> shift) & (BUCKETS - 1);
            tmp[cnt[b]++] = id;
        }
        order.swap(tmp);
    }
    return order;
}

static vector<int> greedyLocalRoute(const vector<Point>& pts) {
    int n = (int)pts.size();
    vector<uint64_t> keys = makeKeys(pts);
    vector<int> order = radixSortOrder(keys);
    vector<int> pos(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) pos[order[i]] = i;

    vector<char> used(n, 0);
    vector<int> route;
    route.reserve(n);
    int cur = 0;
    route.push_back(cur);
    used[cur] = 1;

    const int maxRadius = 64;
    for (int step = 1; step < n; ++step) {
        int p = pos[cur];
        int best = -1;
        double bestD = numeric_limits<double>::infinity();

        for (int r = 1; r <= maxRadius; ++r) {
            bool found = false;
            int L = max(0, p - r);
            int R = min(n - 1, p + r);
            for (int i = L; i <= R; ++i) {
                int cand = order[i];
                if (used[cand]) continue;
                found = true;
                double d = dist2(pts[cur], pts[cand]);
                if (d < bestD) {
                    bestD = d;
                    best = cand;
                }
            }
            if (found && best != -1) break;
        }

        if (best == -1) {
            for (int cand : order) {
                if (!used[cand]) {
                    best = cand;
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        cur = best;
        used[cur] = 1;
        route.push_back(cur);
    }
    return route;
}

static vector<int> mortonRoute(const vector<Point>& pts) {
    vector<uint64_t> keys = makeKeys(pts);
    vector<int> order = radixSortOrder(keys);
    auto it = find(order.begin(), order.end(), 0);
    if (it != order.end()) rotate(order.begin(), it, order.end());
    return order;
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    FastInput in;
    int N;
    if (!in.readInt(N)) return 0;

    vector<Point> pts(N);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
        double x, y;
        in.readDouble(x);
        in.readDouble(y);
        pts[i] = Point{(float)x, (float)y};
    }

    vector<int> route;
    if (N <= 12) {
        route = exactSmallTSP(pts);
    } else if (N <= 5000) {
        route = greedyLocalRoute(pts);
    } else {
        route = mortonRoute(pts);
    }

    if (!route.empty() && route[0] != 0) {
        auto it = find(route.begin(), route.end(), 0);
        if (it != route.end()) rotate(route.begin(), it, route.end());
    }

    for (int i = 0; i < (int)route.size(); ++i) {
        if (i) cout << ' ';
        cout << route[i];
    }
    cout << '\n';
    return 0;
}

Test 2: Chinese Postman Problem Solver (C#) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Chinese Postman Problem (Route Inspection Problem) in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 6-20 nodes, 8-45 edges (small graphs, exact algorithms feasible)
- **Medium**: 35-200 nodes, 80-600 edges (requires efficient algorithms)
- **Large**: 500-1000 nodes, 2000-5000 edges (requires optimized implementations)
- **Extreme**: 5000-10000 nodes, 30000-80000 edges (requires very fast algorithms)
- **Epic**: 50000-100000 nodes, 500000-1000000 edges (requires highly optimized algorithms)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with graphs ranging from 6 nodes to 100000 nodes. The same function must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
Lines 2..M+1: u v w (edge endpoints, 0-indexed, weight integer)

**Output format (stdout):**
A sequence of node indices (whitespace-separated) representing a route that starts at node 0,
traverses EVERY edge at least once, and returns to node 0.
You may optionally prefix with a single integer L (route length). If present, it must match the number of nodes listed.

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for large graphs
3. **Correctness**: Must traverse every edge at least once

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.

Imagine a mail carrier who must walk along every street in a neighbourhood and return home. Some streets may need to be walked twice if the network doesn't allow a perfect loop — the goal is to minimise that extra walking.

The AI must figure out which streets (edges) to re-traverse and produce a complete route. Subpasses increase the graph size, making the problem harder to solve efficiently.

Chinese Postman Problem - 6 nodes, 8 edges

View Code (4931 chars)
using System;
using System.IO;

public static class Program
{
    public static void Main()
    {
        var fs = new FastScanner(Console.OpenStandardInput());

        int n = fs.NextInt();
        int m = fs.NextInt();

        int[] head = new int[n];
        Array.Fill(head, -1);

        int halfEdgeCount = checked(m << 2);
        int copyCount = checked(m << 1);

        int[] to = new int[halfEdgeCount];
        int[] next = new int[halfEdgeCount];
        byte[] used = new byte[copyCount];

        int ptr = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            int u = fs.NextInt();
            int v = fs.NextInt();
            fs.NextLong(); // weight is irrelevant for the route construction

            to[ptr] = v;
            next[ptr] = head[u];
            head[u] = ptr;
            ptr++;

            to[ptr] = u;
            next[ptr] = head[v];
            head[v] = ptr;
            ptr++;

            to[ptr] = v;
            next[ptr] = head[u];
            head[u] = ptr;
            ptr++;

            to[ptr] = u;
            next[ptr] = head[v];
            head[v] = ptr;
            ptr++;
        }

        int[] stack = new int[copyCount + 1];
        int[] route = new int[copyCount + 1];
        int sp = 0;
        int rp = 0;

        stack[sp++] = 0;

        while (sp > 0)
        {
            int v = stack[sp - 1];

            int e = head[v];
            while (e != -1 && used[e >> 1] != 0)
            {
                e = next[e];
            }
            head[v] = e;

            if (e == -1)
            {
                route[rp++] = v;
                sp--;
            }
            else
            {
                used[e >> 1] = 1;
                head[v] = next[e];
                stack[sp++] = to[e];
            }
        }

        var output = new FastWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput());
        for (int i = rp - 1; i >= 0; i--)
        {
            if (i != rp - 1) output.WriteByte((byte)' ');
            output.WriteInt(route[i]);
        }
        output.WriteByte((byte)'\n');
        output.Flush();
    }

    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream;
        private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _len;
        private int _ptr;

        public FastScanner(Stream stream)
        {
            _stream = stream;
        }

        private int ReadByte()
        {
            if (_ptr >= _len)
            {
                _len = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _ptr = 0;
                if (_len <= 0) return -1;
            }

            return _buffer[_ptr++];
        }

        public int NextInt()
        {
            int c;
            do
            {
                c = ReadByte();
            } while (c <= 32 && c >= 0);

            int sign = 1;
            if (c == '-')
            {
                sign = -1;
                c = ReadByte();
            }

            int value = 0;
            while (c > 32)
            {
                value = value * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = ReadByte();
            }

            return value * sign;
        }

        public long NextLong()
        {
            int c;
            do
            {
                c = ReadByte();
            } while (c <= 32 && c >= 0);

            long sign = 1;
            if (c == '-')
            {
                sign = -1;
                c = ReadByte();
            }

            long value = 0;
            while (c > 32)
            {
                value = value * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = ReadByte();
            }

            return value * sign;
        }
    }

    private sealed class FastWriter
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream;
        private readonly byte[] _buffer;
        private int _ptr;

        public FastWriter(Stream stream)
        {
            _stream = stream;
            _buffer = new byte[1 << 20];
        }

        public void WriteByte(byte b)
        {
            if (_ptr >= _buffer.Length) Flush();
            _buffer[_ptr++] = b;
        }

        public void WriteInt(int value)
        {
            if (value == 0)
            {
                WriteByte((byte)'0');
                return;
            }

            if (value < 0)
            {
                WriteByte((byte)'-');
                value = -value;
            }

            Span<byte> tmp = stackalloc byte[11];
            int len = 0;
            while (value > 0)
            {
                tmp[len++] = (byte)('0' + (value % 10));
                value /= 10;
            }

            while (len > 0)
            {
                WriteByte(tmp[--len]);
            }
        }

        public void Flush()
        {
            if (_ptr > 0)
            {
                _stream.Write(_buffer, 0, _ptr);
                _ptr = 0;
            }
        }
    }
}

Test 3: Graph Layout - Edge Crossing Minimization (Rust) Score: 1.25 / 16
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Graph Layout problem (Edge Crossing Minimization) in Rust.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 6-15 nodes, small trees and cycles (planar, 0 crossings achievable)
- **Medium**: 20-100 nodes, dense planar and non-planar graphs
- **Large**: 500-1000 nodes, random dense graphs (many crossings unavoidable)
- **Extreme**: 5000-10000 nodes, very large random graphs

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with graphs ranging from 6 nodes to 200,000 nodes. The same solver must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
Lines 2..M+1: u v (undirected edge endpoints, 0-indexed)

**Output format (stdout):**
Output N lines, one per node, with: x y coordinates (floats).
You may optionally prefix with a single integer N on the first line.

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and density
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for large graphs
3. **Quality**: Should produce reasonable layouts with minimal crossings
4. **Robustness**: Handle disconnected components, self-loops, and parallel edges gracefully

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a graph (a set of dots connected by lines), arrange the dots on a 2D canvas so that as few lines cross each other as possible. A clean, readable layout is the goal — tangled spaghetti is the enemy.

Minimising crossings is extremely hard in general. The AI must place nodes intelligently; the baseline simply puts them in a circle. Subpasses increase the number of nodes and edges.

 1.0000
[tree, 6 nodes, 5 edges] Crossings: 0, Baseline: 1, Time: 0.0s - perfect (0 crossings)

Graph Layout - tree (6 nodes, 5 edges)

View Code (8511 chars)
use std::cmp::Reverse;
use std::collections::VecDeque;
use std::f64::consts::PI;
use std::io::{self, Read, Write};

struct Scanner {
    input: Vec<u8>,
    index: usize,
}

impl Scanner {
    fn new() -> Self {
        let mut input = String::new();
        io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
        Self {
            input: input.into_bytes(),
            index: 0,
        }
    }

    fn next_usize(&mut self) -> usize {
        while self.index < self.input.len() && self.input[self.index].is_ascii_whitespace() {
            self.index += 1;
        }
        let mut value = 0usize;
        while self.index < self.input.len() && !self.input[self.index].is_ascii_whitespace() {
            value = value * 10 + (self.input[self.index] - b'0') as usize;
            self.index += 1;
        }
        value
    }
}

struct BfsResult {
    far_local: usize,
    far_global: usize,
    dist: Vec<i32>,
    parent: Vec<usize>,
}

fn bfs_farthest(
    start_global: usize,
    cid: usize,
    comp_of: &[usize],
    comp_pos: &[usize],
    comp_nodes: &[usize],
    adj: &[Vec<usize>],
) -> BfsResult {
    let k = comp_nodes.len();
    let mut dist = vec![-1i32; k];
    let mut parent = vec![usize::MAX; k];
    let start_local = comp_pos[start_global];
    let mut q = VecDeque::new();
    dist[start_local] = 0;
    q.push_back(start_local);
    let mut far_local = start_local;

    while let Some(u_local) = q.pop_front() {
        let u = comp_nodes[u_local];
        let nd = dist[u_local] + 1;
        for &v in &adj[u] {
            if comp_of[v] != cid {
                continue;
            }
            let v_local = comp_pos[v];
            if dist[v_local] == -1 {
                dist[v_local] = nd;
                parent[v_local] = u_local;
                q.push_back(v_local);
                if dist[v_local] > dist[far_local] {
                    far_local = v_local;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    BfsResult {
        far_local,
        far_global: comp_nodes[far_local],
        dist,
        parent,
    }
}

fn refresh_positions(layer: &[usize], pos: &mut [f64]) {
    for (i, &u) in layer.iter().enumerate() {
        pos[u] = i as f64;
    }
}

fn sort_layer_by_reference(
    layer: &mut Vec<usize>,
    ref_depth: i32,
    depth: &[i32],
    pos: &[f64],
    cid: usize,
    comp_of: &[usize],
    adj: &[Vec<usize>],
) {
    let mut keyed: Vec<(f64, usize)> = Vec::with_capacity(layer.len());
    for &u in layer.iter() {
        let mut sum = 0.0;
        let mut cnt = 0.0;
        for &v in &adj[u] {
            if comp_of[v] == cid && depth[v] == ref_depth {
                sum += pos[v];
                cnt += 1.0;
            }
        }
        let key = if cnt > 0.0 { sum / cnt } else { pos[u] };
        keyed.push((key, u));
    }
    keyed.sort_by(|a, b| a.0.total_cmp(&b.0).then_with(|| a.1.cmp(&b.1)));
    layer.clear();
    layer.extend(keyed.into_iter().map(|(_, u)| u));
}

fn main() {
    let mut sc = Scanner::new();
    let n = sc.next_usize();
    let m = sc.next_usize();

    let mut adj = vec![Vec::<usize>::new(); n];
    for _ in 0..m {
        let u = sc.next_usize();
        let v = sc.next_usize();
        if u < n && v < n && u != v {
            adj[u].push(v);
            adj[v].push(u);
        }
    }

    let mut comp_of = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    let mut comp_pos = vec![0usize; n];
    let mut components: Vec<Vec<usize>> = Vec::new();

    for v in 0..n {
        if comp_of[v] != usize::MAX {
            continue;
        }
        let cid = components.len();
        let mut stack = vec![v];
        comp_of[v] = cid;
        let mut nodes = Vec::new();

        while let Some(u) = stack.pop() {
            nodes.push(u);
            for &w in &adj[u] {
                if comp_of[w] == usize::MAX {
                    comp_of[w] = cid;
                    stack.push(w);
                }
            }
        }

        for (i, &u) in nodes.iter().enumerate() {
            comp_pos[u] = i;
        }
        components.push(nodes);
    }

    let mut order: Vec<usize> = (0..components.len()).collect();
    order.sort_by_key(|&cid| Reverse(components[cid].len()));

    let mut x = vec![0.0f64; n];
    let mut y = vec![0.0f64; n];
    let mut depth = vec![-1i32; n];
    let mut pos = vec![0.0f64; n];

    let mut cur_x = 0.0f64;
    let mut cur_y = 0.0f64;
    let mut row_h = 0.0f64;
    let row_limit = 20_000.0f64;
    let margin = 120.0f64;

    for cid in order {
        let comp_nodes = &components[cid];
        let k = comp_nodes.len();

        if k == 1 {
            let side = 200.0f64;
            if cur_x > 0.0 && cur_x + side > row_limit {
                cur_x = 0.0;
                cur_y += row_h + margin;
                row_h = 0.0;
            }
            let ox = cur_x + side * 0.5;
            let oy = cur_y + side * 0.5;
            let u = comp_nodes[0];
            x[u] = ox;
            y[u] = oy;
            cur_x += side + margin;
            row_h = row_h.max(side);
            continue;
        }

        let mut start = comp_nodes[0];
        for &u in comp_nodes.iter() {
            if adj[u].len() > adj[start].len() {
                start = u;
            }
        }

        let bfs1 = bfs_farthest(start, cid, &comp_of, &comp_pos, comp_nodes, &adj);
        let bfs2 = bfs_farthest(bfs1.far_global, cid, &comp_of, &comp_pos, comp_nodes, &adj);

        let mut path = Vec::new();
        let mut cur = bfs2.far_local;
        loop {
            path.push(comp_nodes[cur]);
            if bfs2.parent[cur] == usize::MAX {
                break;
            }
            cur = bfs2.parent[cur];
        }
        let root = path[path.len() / 2];

        let bfs = bfs_farthest(root, cid, &comp_of, &comp_pos, comp_nodes, &adj);
        let max_depth = *bfs.dist.iter().max().unwrap_or(&0) as usize;
        let mut layers: Vec<Vec<usize>> = vec![Vec::new(); max_depth + 1];

        for (local, &d) in bfs.dist.iter().enumerate() {
            let u = comp_nodes[local];
            depth[u] = d;
            layers[d as usize].push(u);
        }

        refresh_positions(&layers[0], &mut pos);

        let sweeps = if k < 64 {
            8
        } else if k < 512 {
            6
        } else if k < 5_000 {
            4
        } else {
            2
        };

        for _ in 0..sweeps {
            for l in 1..layers.len() {
                sort_layer_by_reference(
                    &mut layers[l],
                    (l - 1) as i32,
                    &depth,
                    &pos,
                    cid,
                    &comp_of,
                    &adj,
                );
                refresh_positions(&layers[l], &mut pos);
            }
            for l in (0..layers.len().saturating_sub(1)).rev() {
                sort_layer_by_reference(
                    &mut layers[l],
                    (l + 1) as i32,
                    &depth,
                    &pos,
                    cid,
                    &comp_of,
                    &adj,
                );
                refresh_positions(&layers[l], &mut pos);
            }
        }

        let max_layer_size = layers.iter().map(|v| v.len()).max().unwrap_or(1);
        let scale =
            40.0 + 0.18 * (max_layer_size as f64) + 2.0 * (max_depth as f64) + 2.5 * (k as f64).sqrt();
        let side = 2.0 * scale + 100.0;

        if cur_x > 0.0 && cur_x + side > row_limit {
            cur_x = 0.0;
            cur_y += row_h + margin;
            row_h = 0.0;
        }

        let ox = cur_x + side * 0.5;
        let oy = cur_y + side * 0.5;
        let max_depth_f = max_depth.max(1) as f64;

        for (l, layer) in layers.iter().enumerate() {
            let frac = if max_depth == 0 {
                0.0
            } else {
                (l as f64 / max_depth_f).sqrt()
            };
            let r = scale * frac;
            let cnt = layer.len();
            for (i, &u) in layer.iter().enumerate() {
                let ang = if cnt == 1 {
                    0.0
                } else {
                    2.0 * PI * (i as f64) / (cnt as f64)
                };
                x[u] = ox + r * ang.cos();
                y[u] = oy + r * ang.sin();
            }
        }

        cur_x += side + margin;
        row_h = row_h.max(side);
    }

    let mut out = io::BufWriter::new(io::stdout());
    for i in 0..n {
        writeln!(out, "{:.6} {:.6}", x[i], y[i]).unwrap();
    }
}

Test 4: Regular Tetrahedron Packing in Polyhedra (C++) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Packing problem: Pack regular tetrahedrons into polyhedra (C++).

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY container size from trivial to ludicrous scale.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: V edge_length
Lines 2..V+1: x y z (container vertices)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: K (number of tetrahedrons placed)
Lines 2..K+1: cx cy cz rx ry rz (center + Euler angles in radians)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container volume and complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for massive containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize number of tetrahedrons while ensuring no overlaps

**Key Geometry (edge length e=1.0):**
- Height: h = e * sqrt(2/3) ≈ 0.8165
- Circumradius: R = e * sqrt(3/8) ≈ 0.6124
- Inradius: r = e / sqrt(24) ≈ 0.2041

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Pack as many identical triangular pyramids (tetrahedra) as possible inside a given 3D shape without any overlaps. Unlike cubes, tetrahedra cannot tile space perfectly, so clever placement and rotation are essential.

The AI must handle 3D collision detection and geometry. Subpasses use increasingly complex container shapes. The baseline uses a simple grid placement strategy.

 0.0000
[small_cube] Packed: 1 legal tetrahedrons (of 12 total placements), Baseline: 34, Expected min: 1, Time: 0.0s - minimal (1 tetrahedrons)

Tetrahedron Packing - small_cube (8 vertices)

Strategy: 3D regular tetrahedron packing heuristic for a convex polyhedral container. Strategy: 1. Build a convex hull of the supplied vertices. 2. Pack tetrahedra in horizontal layers using an equilateral-triangle tiling. 3. Accept only tetrahedra whose 4 vertices are inside the hull. This is a scalable heuristic: it is exact for the inside test on convex containers and uses a simple periodic lattice for placement.

View Code (12587 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <cmath>
#include <cstdint>
#include <iomanip>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <utility>
#include <vector>

using std::pair;
using std::size_t;
using std::vector;

struct Vec3 {
    double x = 0, y = 0, z = 0;
    Vec3() = default;
    Vec3(double x_, double y_, double z_) : x(x_), y(y_), z(z_) {}
};

static inline Vec3 operator+(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return {a.x + b.x, a.y + b.y, a.z + b.z};
}
static inline Vec3 operator-(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return {a.x - b.x, a.y - b.y, a.z - b.z};
}
static inline Vec3 operator*(const Vec3& a, double s) {
    return {a.x * s, a.y * s, a.z * s};
}
static inline Vec3 operator/(const Vec3& a, double s) {
    return {a.x / s, a.y / s, a.z / s};
}
static inline double dot(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return a.x * b.x + a.y * b.y + a.z * b.z;
}
static inline Vec3 cross(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return {
        a.y * b.z - a.z * b.y,
        a.z * b.x - a.x * b.z,
        a.x * b.y - a.y * b.x
    };
}
static inline double norm2(const Vec3& a) { return dot(a, a); }

struct Face {
    int a = -1, b = -1, c = -1;
    Vec3 n;
    double d = 0.0;
    bool dead = false;
};

struct Placement {
    Vec3 center;
    double rx = 0.0, ry = 0.0, rz = 0.0;
};

static inline std::uint64_t edgeKey(int u, int v) {
    if (u > v) std::swap(u, v);
    return (std::uint64_t(std::uint32_t(u)) << 32) | std::uint32_t(v);
}

static void makeFace(Face& f, int a, int b, int c, const vector<Vec3>& p, const Vec3& inside) {
    f.a = a; f.b = b; f.c = c;
    f.n = cross(p[b] - p[a], p[c] - p[a]);
    f.d = dot(f.n, p[a]);
    if (dot(f.n, inside) > f.d) {
        std::swap(f.b, f.c);
        f.n = cross(p[f.b] - p[f.a], p[f.c] - p[f.a]);
        f.d = dot(f.n, p[f.a]);
    }
}

static double planeDist(const Face& f, const Vec3& x) {
    return dot(f.n, x) - f.d;
}

static bool pointInsideHull(const Vec3& x, const vector<Face>& faces, double eps) {
    for (const auto& f : faces) {
        if (f.dead) continue;
        double tol = eps * (1.0 + std::sqrt(norm2(f.n)));
        if (planeDist(f, x) > tol) return false;
    }
    return true;
}

static bool buildConvexHull(const vector<Vec3>& pts, vector<Face>& faces, Vec3& inside, double eps) {
    const int n = (int)pts.size();
    if (n < 4) return false;

    int i0 = 0;
    int i1 = -1;
    for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
        if (norm2(pts[i] - pts[i0]) > eps * eps) {
            i1 = i;
            break;
        }
    }
    if (i1 < 0) return false;

    int i2 = -1;
    Vec3 base = pts[i1] - pts[i0];
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        if (i == i0 || i == i1) continue;
        Vec3 cr = cross(base, pts[i] - pts[i0]);
        if (norm2(cr) > eps * eps) {
            i2 = i;
            break;
        }
    }
    if (i2 < 0) return false;

    int i3 = -1;
    Vec3 n012 = cross(pts[i1] - pts[i0], pts[i2] - pts[i0]);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        if (i == i0 || i == i1 || i == i2) continue;
        double vol6 = std::fabs(dot(n012, pts[i] - pts[i0]));
        if (vol6 > eps * eps * eps) {
            i3 = i;
            break;
        }
    }
    if (i3 < 0) return false;

    inside = (pts[i0] + pts[i1] + pts[i2] + pts[i3]) / 4.0;
    faces.clear();
    faces.reserve(2 * n + 8);

    auto addInitialFace = [&](int a, int b, int c, int opp) {
        Face f;
        f.a = a; f.b = b; f.c = c;
        f.n = cross(pts[b] - pts[a], pts[c] - pts[a]);
        f.d = dot(f.n, pts[a]);
        if (dot(f.n, pts[opp] - pts[a]) > 0.0) {
            std::swap(f.b, f.c);
            f.n = cross(pts[f.b] - pts[f.a], pts[f.c] - pts[f.a]);
            f.d = dot(f.n, pts[f.a]);
        }
        if (dot(f.n, inside) > f.d) {
            std::swap(f.b, f.c);
            f.n = cross(pts[f.b] - pts[f.a], pts[f.c] - pts[f.a]);
            f.d = dot(f.n, pts[f.a]);
        }
        faces.push_back(f);
    };

    addInitialFace(i0, i1, i2, i3);
    addInitialFace(i0, i3, i1, i2);
    addInitialFace(i0, i2, i3, i1);
    addInitialFace(i1, i3, i2, i0);

    vector<int> order;
    order.reserve(n);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        if (i != i0 && i != i1 && i != i2 && i != i3) order.push_back(i);
    }

    for (int idx : order) {
        const Vec3& p = pts[idx];
        vector<int> visible;
        for (int fi = 0; fi < (int)faces.size(); ++fi) {
            if (faces[fi].dead) continue;
            double tol = eps * (1.0 + std::sqrt(norm2(faces[fi].n)));
            if (planeDist(faces[fi], p) > tol) visible.push_back(fi);
        }
        if (visible.empty()) continue;

        std::unordered_map<std::uint64_t, int> cnt;
        std::unordered_map<std::uint64_t, pair<int, int>> oriented;
        cnt.reserve(visible.size() * 6);
        oriented.reserve(visible.size() * 6);

        auto addEdge = [&](int u, int v) {
            auto key = edgeKey(u, v);
            ++cnt[key];
            if (!oriented.count(key)) oriented[key] = {u, v};
        };

        for (int fi : visible) {
            const Face& f = faces[fi];
            addEdge(f.a, f.b);
            addEdge(f.b, f.c);
            addEdge(f.c, f.a);
        }

        std::unordered_map<int, int> next;
        std::unordered_map<int, int> prev;
        next.reserve(cnt.size());
        prev.reserve(cnt.size());
        int horizonEdges = 0;
        for (const auto& kv : cnt) {
            if (kv.second != 1) continue;
            auto [u, v] = oriented[kv.first];
            next[u] = v;
            prev[v] = u;
            ++horizonEdges;
        }

        for (int fi : visible) faces[fi].dead = true;
        if (horizonEdges == 0) continue;

        int start = -1;
        for (const auto& kv : next) {
            if (!prev.count(kv.first)) {
                start = kv.first;
                break;
            }
        }
        if (start < 0) start = next.begin()->first;

        vector<int> cycle;
        cycle.reserve(horizonEdges + 1);
        int cur = start;
        for (int step = 0; step <= horizonEdges + 1; ++step) {
            cycle.push_back(cur);
            auto it = next.find(cur);
            if (it == next.end()) break;
            cur = it->second;
            if (cur == start) break;
        }

        if ((int)cycle.size() < 3) continue;

        // Add the fan of new faces around the new point.
        for (int i = 0; i < (int)cycle.size(); ++i) {
            int u = cycle[i];
            int v = cycle[(i + 1) % cycle.size()];
            Face nf;
            makeFace(nf, u, v, idx, pts, inside);
            faces.push_back(nf);
        }
    }

    return true;
}

static vector<Placement> generatePackings(
    const vector<Face>& faces,
    const Vec3& bbMin,
    const Vec3& bbMax,
    double e,
    size_t cap)
{
    const double pi = std::acos(-1.0);
    const double h = e * std::sqrt(2.0 / 3.0);
    const double rowH = e * std::sqrt(3.0) * 0.5;

    vector<Placement> best;

    auto tryPhase = [&](double ox, double oy, double oz) {
        vector<Placement> out;
        out.reserve(cap);

        const double x0 = bbMin.x - 2.0 * e + ox;
        const double y0 = bbMin.y - 2.0 * rowH + oy;
        const double z0 = bbMin.z - 2.0 * h + oz;

        const int jMin = (int)std::floor((bbMin.y - y0) / rowH) - 2;
        const int jMax = (int)std::ceil((bbMax.y - y0) / rowH) + 2;
        const int kMin = (int)std::floor((bbMin.z - z0) / h) - 2;
        const int kMax = (int)std::ceil((bbMax.z - z0) / h) + 2;

        for (int k = kMin; k <= kMax && out.size() < cap; ++k) {
            double z = z0 + k * h;
            for (int j = jMin; j <= jMax && out.size() < cap; ++j) {
                double y = y0 + j * rowH;
                double xBase = x0 + j * (e * 0.5);

                const int iMin = (int)std::floor((bbMin.x - xBase) / e) - 3;
                const int iMax = (int)std::ceil((bbMax.x - xBase) / e) + 3;

                for (int i = iMin; i <= iMax && out.size() < cap; ++i) {
                    double x = xBase + i * e;

                    // Up triangle tetrahedron.
                    {
                        Vec3 a{x, y, z};
                        Vec3 b{x + e, y, z};
                        Vec3 c{x + e * 0.5, y + rowH, z};
                        Vec3 apex{(a.x + b.x + c.x) / 3.0, (a.y + b.y + c.y) / 3.0, z + h};
                        Vec3 center = (a + b + c + apex) / 4.0;

                        if (pointInsideHull(a, faces, 1e-9) &&
                            pointInsideHull(b, faces, 1e-9) &&
                            pointInsideHull(c, faces, 1e-9) &&
                            pointInsideHull(apex, faces, 1e-9))
                        {
                            out.push_back({center, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0});
                        }
                    }

                    if (out.size() >= cap) break;

                    // Down triangle tetrahedron.
                    {
                        Vec3 a{x + e * 0.5, y + rowH, z};
                        Vec3 b{x + 1.5 * e, y + rowH, z};
                        Vec3 c{x + e, y, z};
                        Vec3 apex{(a.x + b.x + c.x) / 3.0, (a.y + b.y + c.y) / 3.0, z + h};
                        Vec3 center = (a + b + c + apex) / 4.0;

                        if (pointInsideHull(a, faces, 1e-9) &&
                            pointInsideHull(b, faces, 1e-9) &&
                            pointInsideHull(c, faces, 1e-9) &&
                            pointInsideHull(apex, faces, 1e-9))
                        {
                            out.push_back({center, 0.0, 0.0, pi});
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        return out;
    };

    const double phaseX[2] = {0.0, e * 0.5};
    const double phaseY[2] = {0.0, rowH * 0.5};
    const double phaseZ[2] = {0.0, h * 0.5};

    for (double ox : phaseX) {
        for (double oy : phaseY) {
            for (double oz : phaseZ) {
                vector<Placement> cur = tryPhase(ox, oy, oz);
                if (cur.size() > best.size()) best = std::move(cur);
            }
        }
    }

    return best;
}

int main() {
    std::ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(nullptr);

    int V = 0;
    double e = 1.0;
    if (!(std::cin >> V >> e)) return 0;

    vector<Vec3> pts;
    pts.reserve(std::max(0, V));
    for (int i = 0; i < V; ++i) {
        double x, y, z;
        std::cin >> x >> y >> z;
        pts.push_back({x, y, z});
    }

    if (pts.empty() || V < 4 || e <= 0.0) {
        std::cout << 0 << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    Vec3 bbMin{
        std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity(),
        std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity(),
        std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity()
    };
    Vec3 bbMax{
        -std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity(),
        -std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity(),
        -std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity()
    };
    for (const auto& p : pts) {
        bbMin.x = std::min(bbMin.x, p.x);
        bbMin.y = std::min(bbMin.y, p.y);
        bbMin.z = std::min(bbMin.z, p.z);
        bbMax.x = std::max(bbMax.x, p.x);
        bbMax.y = std::max(bbMax.y, p.y);
        bbMax.z = std::max(bbMax.z, p.z);
    }

    vector<Face> faces;
    Vec3 inside;
    double hullEps = 1e-10 * std::max({1.0,
                                      std::fabs(bbMax.x - bbMin.x),
                                      std::fabs(bbMax.y - bbMin.y),
                                      std::fabs(bbMax.z - bbMin.z)});
    if (!buildConvexHull(pts, faces, inside, hullEps)) {
        std::cout << 0 << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    // Remove dead faces from the hull representation used for inside tests.
    vector<Face> hullFaces;
    hullFaces.reserve(faces.size());
    for (const auto& f : faces) if (!f.dead) hullFaces.push_back(f);

    // If the hull is extremely small, there may be no room for a tetrahedron.
    const double h = e * std::sqrt(2.0 / 3.0);
    if (bbMax.z - bbMin.z + 1e-12 < h) {
        // Still possible if the hull is tilted, but the conservative layer
        // construction below will not find any placements.
    }

    size_t cap = 50000;
    vector<Placement> placements = generatePackings(hullFaces, bbMin, bbMax, e, cap);

    std::cout.setf(std::ios::fixed);
    std::cout << std::setprecision(10);
    std::cout << placements.size() << '\n';
    for (const auto& t : placements) {
        std::cout << t.center.x << ' ' << t.center.y << ' ' << t.center.z << ' '
                  << t.rx << ' ' << t.ry << ' ' << t.rz << '\n';
    }
    return 0;
}

Test 5: Hamiltonian Path on Grid with Obstacles (Rust) Score: 8.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Hamiltonian Path problem on 2D grids with obstacles in Rust.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY grid size from trivial to ludicrous scale.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: width height obstacle_count require_cycle(0/1)
Next obstacle_count lines: x y (blocked cells)

**Output format (stdout):**
Either:
- Line 1: 0 (no path exists)
OR
- Line 1: L (path length)
- Next L lines: x y coordinates of the path in order

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on grid size and obstacle density
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 2500 cell grids
3. **Correctness**: Must find valid paths or correctly report impossibility

**Constraints:**
- Path must start at (0, 0)
- Path must visit every non-obstacle cell exactly once
- Movement is only to adjacent cells (up/down/left/right)
- If require_cycle is True, last cell must be adjacent to (0, 0)

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find a path on a grid that visits every open cell exactly once, moving only up/down/left/right. Some cells are blocked by obstacles. Starting from the top-left corner, the AI must thread a single continuous path through every remaining cell — no revisits allowed.

This is an NP-complete problem, so brute-force search blows up quickly. Subpasses increase the grid size and obstacle count. The baseline uses a simple depth-first search.

 1.0000
[4x4, 1 obstacles] Valid path, 15 cells, Time: 0.3s

Hamiltonian Path - 4x4 grid, 1 obstacles

View Code (11757 chars)
use std::io::{self, Read, Write};
use std::thread;
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};

struct Scanner {
    data: Vec<u8>,
    idx: usize,
}

impl Scanner {
    fn new() -> Self {
        let mut input = String::new();
        io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
        Self { data: input.into_bytes(), idx: 0 }
    }

    fn next_usize(&mut self) -> usize {
        while self.idx < self.data.len() && self.data[self.idx].is_ascii_whitespace() {
            self.idx += 1;
        }
        let start = self.idx;
        while self.idx < self.data.len() && !self.data[self.idx].is_ascii_whitespace() {
            self.idx += 1;
        }
        std::str::from_utf8(&self.data[start..self.idx])
            .unwrap()
            .parse::<usize>()
            .unwrap()
    }
}

fn idx_of(x: usize, y: usize, w: usize) -> usize {
    y * w + x
}

fn no_obstacle_path(w: usize, h: usize, require_cycle: bool) -> Option<Vec<(usize, usize)>> {
    if !require_cycle {
        let mut path = Vec::with_capacity(w * h);
        for y in 0..h {
            if y % 2 == 0 {
                for x in 0..w {
                    path.push((x, y));
                }
            } else {
                for x in (0..w).rev() {
                    path.push((x, y));
                }
            }
        }
        return Some(path);
    }

    if w < 2 || h < 2 {
        return None;
    }

    if h % 2 == 0 {
        let mut path = Vec::with_capacity(w * h);
        for x in 0..w {
            path.push((x, 0));
        }
        for y in 1..h {
            if y % 2 == 1 {
                for x in (1..w).rev() {
                    path.push((x, y));
                }
            } else {
                for x in 1..w {
                    path.push((x, y));
                }
            }
        }
        for y in (1..h).rev() {
            path.push((0, y));
        }
        Some(path)
    } else if w % 2 == 0 {
        let mut path = Vec::with_capacity(w * h);
        for y in 0..h {
            path.push((0, y));
        }
        for x in 1..w {
            if x % 2 == 1 {
                for y in (1..h).rev() {
                    path.push((x, y));
                }
            } else {
                for y in 1..h {
                    path.push((x, y));
                }
            }
        }
        for x in (1..w).rev() {
            path.push((x, 0));
        }
        Some(path)
    } else {
        None
    }
}

struct Search<'a> {
    neighbors: &'a [Vec<usize>],
    order: &'a [usize],
    rem_deg: Vec<i32>,
    visited: Vec<u8>,
    seen: Vec<u32>,
    stamp: u32,
    path: Vec<usize>,
    start: usize,
    total: usize,
    require_cycle: bool,
    deadline: Instant,
    nodes: u64,
}

impl<'a> Search<'a> {
    fn new(
        neighbors: &'a [Vec<usize>],
        order: &'a [usize],
        start: usize,
        total: usize,
        require_cycle: bool,
    ) -> Self {
        let mut rem_deg = neighbors.iter().map(|v| v.len() as i32).collect::<Vec<_>>();
        let mut visited = vec![0u8; total];
        let mut path = Vec::with_capacity(total);
        let seen = vec![0u32; total];

        visited[start] = 1;
        path.push(start);
        rem_deg[start] = 0;
        for &n in &neighbors[start] {
            rem_deg[n] -= 1;
        }

        Self {
            neighbors,
            order,
            rem_deg,
            visited,
            seen,
            stamp: 1,
            path,
            start,
            total,
            require_cycle,
            deadline: Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(285),
            nodes: 0,
        }
    }

    fn time_ok(&mut self) -> bool {
        self.nodes += 1;
        if (self.nodes & 0x7ff) == 0 {
            if Instant::now() >= self.deadline {
                return false;
            }
        }
        true
    }

    fn remaining_connected(&mut self, remaining: usize) -> bool {
        if remaining <= 1 {
            return true;
        }

        let mut seed = None;
        for i in 0..self.total {
            if self.visited[i] == 0 {
                seed = Some(i);
                break;
            }
        }
        let seed = match seed {
            Some(s) => s,
            None => return true,
        };

        self.stamp = self.stamp.wrapping_add(1);
        if self.stamp == 0 {
            self.stamp = 1;
            self.seen.fill(0);
        }
        let mark = self.stamp;

        let mut q = Vec::with_capacity(remaining);
        q.push(seed);
        self.seen[seed] = mark;
        let mut head = 0usize;
        let mut count = 1usize;

        while head < q.len() {
            let u = q[head];
            head += 1;
            for &v in &self.neighbors[u] {
                if self.visited[v] == 0 && self.seen[v] != mark {
                    self.seen[v] = mark;
                    q.push(v);
                    count += 1;
                }
            }
        }

        count == remaining
    }

    fn state_ok(&mut self, visited_count: usize) -> bool {
        if !self.time_ok() {
            return false;
        }

        let remaining = self.total - visited_count;
        if remaining == 0 {
            if !self.require_cycle {
                return true;
            }
            let cur = self.path[self.path.len() - 1];
            return self.neighbors[cur].iter().any(|&n| n == self.start);
        }

        if remaining > 1 {
            let mut deg1 = 0usize;
            for i in 0..self.total {
                if self.visited[i] == 0 {
                    let d = self.rem_deg[i];
                    if d == 0 {
                        return false;
                    }
                    if d == 1 {
                        deg1 += 1;
                        if deg1 > 2 {
                            return false;
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        self.remaining_connected(remaining)
    }

    fn apply_move(&mut self, u: usize) -> (i32, Vec<(usize, i32)>) {
        let old_u = self.rem_deg[u];
        self.visited[u] = 1;
        self.rem_deg[u] = 0;
        let mut changes = Vec::with_capacity(4);
        for &v in &self.neighbors[u] {
            if self.visited[v] == 0 {
                changes.push((v, self.rem_deg[v]));
                self.rem_deg[v] -= 1;
            }
        }
        self.path.push(u);
        (old_u, changes)
    }

    fn undo_move(&mut self, u: usize, old_u: i32, changes: Vec<(usize, i32)>) {
        self.path.pop();
        self.visited[u] = 0;
        self.rem_deg[u] = old_u;
        for (v, old) in changes {
            self.rem_deg[v] = old;
        }
    }

    fn dfs(&mut self, cur: usize, visited_count: usize) -> bool {
        if !self.state_ok(visited_count) {
            return false;
        }

        let remaining = self.total - visited_count;
        if remaining == 0 {
            if self.require_cycle {
                return self.neighbors[cur].iter().any(|&n| n == self.start);
            }
            return true;
        }

        let mut candidates = Vec::with_capacity(4);
        for &n in &self.neighbors[cur] {
            if self.visited[n] == 0 {
                if remaining > 1 && self.rem_deg[n] == 0 {
                    continue;
                }
                if self.require_cycle && remaining == 1 && !self.neighbors[n].iter().any(|&m| m == self.start) {
                    continue;
                }
                candidates.push(n);
            }
        }

        if candidates.is_empty() {
            return false;
        }

        candidates.sort_by_key(|&u| (self.rem_deg[u], self.order[u]));

        for u in candidates {
            let (old_u, changes) = self.apply_move(u);
            if self.dfs(u, visited_count + 1) {
                return true;
            }
            self.undo_move(u, old_u, changes);
        }

        false
    }
}

fn solve() -> String {
    let mut sc = Scanner::new();
    let w = sc.next_usize();
    let h = sc.next_usize();
    let obstacle_count = sc.next_usize();
    let require_cycle = sc.next_usize() != 0;

    let n = w * h;
    let mut blocked = vec![false; n];
    for _ in 0..obstacle_count {
        let x = sc.next_usize();
        let y = sc.next_usize();
        if x < w && y < h {
            blocked[idx_of(x, y, w)] = true;
        }
    }

    if !blocked[idx_of(0, 0, w)] && obstacle_count == 0 {
        if let Some(path) = no_obstacle_path(w, h, require_cycle) {
            let mut out = String::new();
            out.push_str(&format!("{}\n", path.len()));
            for (x, y) in path {
                out.push_str(&format!("{} {}\n", x, y));
            }
            return out;
        } else {
            return "0\n".to_string();
        }
    }

    if blocked[idx_of(0, 0, w)] {
        return "0\n".to_string();
    }

    let mut id_of = vec![usize::MAX; n];
    let mut cells = Vec::new();
    let mut black = 0usize;
    let mut white = 0usize;
    for y in 0..h {
        for x in 0..w {
            let id = idx_of(x, y, w);
            if !blocked[id] {
                let free_id = cells.len();
                id_of[id] = free_id;
                cells.push((x, y));
                if (x + y) % 2 == 0 {
                    black += 1;
                } else {
                    white += 1;
                }
            }
        }
    }

    let total = cells.len();
    if total == 0 {
        return "0\n".to_string();
    }

    if require_cycle {
        if total < 4 || black != white {
            return "0\n".to_string();
        }
    } else if total % 2 == 0 {
        if black != white {
            return "0\n".to_string();
        }
    } else if black != white + 1 {
        return "0\n".to_string();
    }

    let start_coord = idx_of(0, 0, w);
    let start = id_of[start_coord];
    if start == usize::MAX {
        return "0\n".to_string();
    }

    let mut neighbors = vec![Vec::<usize>::new(); total];
    for (id, &(x, y)) in cells.iter().enumerate() {
        let mut ns = Vec::with_capacity(4);
        if x > 0 {
            let nid = idx_of(x - 1, y, w);
            if id_of[nid] != usize::MAX {
                ns.push(id_of[nid]);
            }
        }
        if x + 1 < w {
            let nid = idx_of(x + 1, y, w);
            if id_of[nid] != usize::MAX {
                ns.push(id_of[nid]);
            }
        }
        if y > 0 {
            let nid = idx_of(x, y - 1, w);
            if id_of[nid] != usize::MAX {
                ns.push(id_of[nid]);
            }
        }
        if y + 1 < h {
            let nid = idx_of(x, y + 1, w);
            if id_of[nid] != usize::MAX {
                ns.push(id_of[nid]);
            }
        }
        neighbors[id] = ns;
    }

    let mut order = vec![0usize; total];
    for (id, &(x, y)) in cells.iter().enumerate() {
        order[id] = y * w + if y % 2 == 0 { x } else { w - 1 - x };
    }

    let mut search = Search::new(&neighbors, &order, start, total, require_cycle);
    if total == 1 {
        let mut out = String::new();
        out.push_str("1\n0 0\n");
        return out;
    }

    if !search.dfs(start, 1) {
        return "0\n".to_string();
    }

    let mut out = String::new();
    out.push_str(&format!("{}\n", search.path.len()));
    for id in search.path {
        let (x, y) = cells[id];
        out.push_str(&format!("{} {}\n", x, y));
    }
    out
}

fn main() {
    let handle = thread::Builder::new()
        .stack_size(64 * 1024 * 1024)
        .spawn(|| solve())
        .unwrap();
    let output = handle.join().unwrap();
    let mut stdout = io::BufWriter::new(io::stdout());
    stdout.write_all(output.as_bytes()).unwrap();
}

Test 6: Orbital TSP - Space Station Route Planning (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving an Orbital Travelling Salesman Problem in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY number of stations from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-5 stations (simple brute-force permutations feasible)
- **Medium**: 6-10 stations (requires heuristics, some optimization)
- **Large**: 15-20 stations (complex orbital mechanics, needs efficient algorithms)
- **Extreme**: 50-100 stations (very complex, requires advanced heuristics)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with 2 to 100 stations. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N mu
Line 2: start orbit: x y z vx vy vz
Lines 3..N+2: station orbits: x y z vx vy vz

**Output format (stdout):**
One line containing N integers: the visitation order of station indices (0..N-1).


**Starting position (epoch T=0):**
Position: (7197.9, 3733.4, -6152.3) km
Velocity: (4.567, -4.577, -1.601) km/s
Altitude: ~3807 km

**Physics constants:**
- Earth gravitational parameter μ = 398600.4418 km³/s²
- Earth radius = 6371 km

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number of stations
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 100 stations
3. **Accuracy**: Should provide reasonable delta-V estimates

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

View chain of thought

Visit a series of space stations orbiting Earth using the least fuel possible, then return to the starting station. Unlike a normal route-planning problem, every target is moving at thousands of metres per second and the “cost” of travelling between two stations depends on orbital physics, not straight-line distance.

The AI must combine route optimisation with orbital mechanics. Subpasses add more stations and trickier orbit geometries. The baseline uses a simple nearest-neighbour heuristic with approximate fuel estimates.

View Code (11760 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Globalization;
using System.Numerics;

public class Program
{
    private const double Eps = 1e-12;

    public static void Main()
    {
        var input = Console.In.ReadToEnd();
        var fs = new FastScanner(input);

        int n = fs.NextInt();
        double mu = fs.NextDouble();

        var startR = new Vec3(fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble());
        var startV = new Vec3(fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble());
        var startState = MakeState(startR, startV, mu);

        var stationStates = new OrbitState[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            var r = new Vec3(fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble());
            var v = new Vec3(fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble());
            stationStates[i] = MakeState(r, v, mu);
        }

        var allStates = new OrbitState[n + 1];
        allStates[0] = startState;
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) allStates[i + 1] = stationStates[i];

        double posScale = 0, velScale = 0, hScale = 0, eScale = 0;
        int pairs = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < allStates.Length; i++)
        {
            for (int j = i + 1; j < allStates.Length; j++)
            {
                posScale += Vec3.Distance(allStates[i].R, allStates[j].R);
                velScale += Vec3.Distance(allStates[i].V, allStates[j].V);
                hScale += Vec3.Distance(allStates[i].H, allStates[j].H);
                eScale += Math.Abs(allStates[i].Energy - allStates[j].Energy);
                pairs++;
            }
        }

        if (pairs == 0) pairs = 1;
        posScale = NormalizeScale(posScale / pairs);
        velScale = NormalizeScale(velScale / pairs);
        hScale = NormalizeScale(hScale / pairs);
        eScale = NormalizeScale(eScale / pairs);

        var cost = new double[n][];
        var startCost = new double[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            cost[i] = new double[n];
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            startCost[i] = Distance(startState, stationStates[i], posScale, velScale, hScale, eScale);
            for (int j = i + 1; j < n; j++)
            {
                double d = Distance(stationStates[i], stationStates[j], posScale, velScale, hScale, eScale);
                cost[i][j] = d;
                cost[j][i] = d;
            }
        }

        int[] route = n <= 16
            ? SolveExact(startCost, cost)
            : SolveHeuristic(startCost, cost);

        Console.WriteLine(string.Join(" ", route));
    }

    private static OrbitState MakeState(in Vec3 r, in Vec3 v, double mu)
    {
        var h = Vec3.Cross(r, v);
        double rMag = r.Length();
        double vMag2 = Vec3.Dot(v, v);
        double energy = 0.5 * vMag2 - mu / Math.Max(rMag, 1e-9);
        return new OrbitState(r, v, h, energy);
    }

    private static double Distance(in OrbitState a, in OrbitState b, double posScale, double velScale, double hScale, double eScale)
    {
        double dr = Vec3.Distance(a.R, b.R) / posScale;
        double dv = Vec3.Distance(a.V, b.V) / velScale;
        double dh = Vec3.Distance(a.H, b.H) / hScale;
        double de = Math.Abs(a.Energy - b.Energy) / eScale;
        return Math.Sqrt(dr * dr + dv * dv + dh * dh + de * de);
    }

    private static double NormalizeScale(double value)
    {
        if (double.IsNaN(value) || double.IsInfinity(value) || value < 1e-9) return 1.0;
        return value;
    }

    private static int[] SolveExact(double[] startCost, double[][] cost)
    {
        int n = startCost.Length;
        int full = 1 << n;
        int stateCount = full * n;
        var dp = new double[stateCount];
        var parent = new int[stateCount];

        for (int i = 0; i < stateCount; i++)
        {
            dp[i] = double.PositiveInfinity;
            parent[i] = -1;
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            int mask = 1 << i;
            int idx = mask * n + i;
            dp[idx] = startCost[i];
        }

        for (int mask = 1; mask < full; mask++)
        {
            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++)
            {
                if ((mask & (1 << j)) == 0) continue;

                int idx = mask * n + j;
                double cur = dp[idx];
                if (double.IsInfinity(cur)) continue;

                int remain = (full - 1) ^ mask;
                while (remain != 0)
                {
                    int bit = remain & -remain;
                    int k = BitOperations.TrailingZeroCount(bit);
                    int nextMask = mask | bit;
                    int nextIdx = nextMask * n + k;
                    double nextCost = cur + cost[j][k];
                    if (nextCost < dp[nextIdx])
                    {
                        dp[nextIdx] = nextCost;
                        parent[nextIdx] = j;
                    }
                    remain ^= bit;
                }
            }
        }

        int fullMask = full - 1;
        int bestEnd = 0;
        double bestCost = double.PositiveInfinity;
        for (int j = 0; j < n; j++)
        {
            int idx = fullMask * n + j;
            if (dp[idx] < bestCost)
            {
                bestCost = dp[idx];
                bestEnd = j;
            }
        }

        var route = new int[n];
        int mask2 = fullMask;
        int curNode = bestEnd;
        for (int pos = n - 1; pos >= 0; pos--)
        {
            route[pos] = curNode;
            int idx = mask2 * n + curNode;
            int prev = parent[idx];
            mask2 ^= 1 << curNode;
            curNode = prev;
        }

        return route;
    }

    private static int[] SolveHeuristic(double[] startCost, double[][] cost)
    {
        int n = startCost.Length;
        double[] minOut = new double[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            double best = double.PositiveInfinity;
            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++)
            {
                if (i == j) continue;
                double d = cost[i][j];
                if (d < best) best = d;
            }
            minOut[i] = double.IsInfinity(best) ? 0.0 : best;
        }

        int[] order = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) order[i] = i;
        Array.Sort(order, (a, b) => startCost[a].CompareTo(startCost[b]));

        var seeds = new List<int>();
        var seen = new bool[n];
        void AddSeed(int idx)
        {
            if (idx < 0 || idx >= n || seen[idx]) return;
            seen[idx] = true;
            seeds.Add(idx);
        }

        int q = Math.Min(4, n);
        for (int i = 0; i < q; i++) AddSeed(order[i]);
        AddSeed(order[n / 2]);
        AddSeed(order[Math.Max(0, n - 1)]);
        AddSeed(order[Math.Max(0, n - 2)]);
        AddSeed(order[Math.Max(0, n - 3)]);

        int[] bestRoute = null;
        double bestCost = double.PositiveInfinity;

        foreach (int seed in seeds)
        {
            int[] route = BuildGreedyRoute(seed, cost, minOut);
            ImproveTwoOpt(route, startCost, cost);
            double routeCost = RouteCost(route, startCost, cost);
            if (routeCost < bestCost)
            {
                bestCost = routeCost;
                bestRoute = route;
            }
        }

        if (bestRoute == null)
        {
            bestRoute = BuildGreedyRoute(order[0], cost, minOut);
        }

        ImproveTwoOpt(bestRoute, startCost, cost);
        return bestRoute;
    }

    private static int[] BuildGreedyRoute(int first, double[][] cost, double[] minOut)
    {
        int n = cost.Length;
        var route = new int[n];
        var used = new bool[n];
        route[0] = first;
        used[first] = true;

        int current = first;
        for (int pos = 1; pos < n; pos++)
        {
            int best = -1;
            double bestScore = double.PositiveInfinity;

            for (int j = 0; j < n; j++)
            {
                if (used[j]) continue;
                double score = cost[current][j] + 0.15 * minOut[j];
                if (score < bestScore)
                {
                    bestScore = score;
                    best = j;
                }
            }

            route[pos] = best;
            used[best] = true;
            current = best;
        }

        return route;
    }

    private static void ImproveTwoOpt(int[] route, double[] startCost, double[][] cost)
    {
        int n = route.Length;
        if (n <= 2) return;

        bool improved = true;
        int pass = 0;
        while (improved && pass++ < 64)
        {
            improved = false;
            for (int i = 0; i < n - 1; i++)
            {
                int prev = i == 0 ? -1 : route[i - 1];
                int a = route[i];
                double left = i == 0 ? startCost[a] : cost[prev][a];

                for (int j = i + 1; j < n; j++)
                {
                    int b = route[j];
                    int next = j == n - 1 ? -1 : route[j + 1];
                    double oldCost = left + (next == -1 ? 0.0 : cost[b][next]);
                    double newCost = (i == 0 ? startCost[b] : cost[prev][b]) + (next == -1 ? 0.0 : cost[a][next]);
                    if (newCost + Eps < oldCost)
                    {
                        Array.Reverse(route, i, j - i + 1);
                        improved = true;
                        goto NextPass;
                    }
                }
            }
        NextPass:
            ;
        }
    }

    private static double RouteCost(int[] route, double[] startCost, double[][] cost)
    {
        double total = startCost[route[0]];
        for (int i = 1; i < route.Length; i++) total += cost[route[i - 1]][route[i]];
        return total;
    }

    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly string _data;
        private int _index;

        public FastScanner(string data)
        {
            _data = data ?? string.Empty;
        }

        public string Next()
        {
            while (_index < _data.Length && char.IsWhiteSpace(_data[_index])) _index++;
            int start = _index;
            while (_index < _data.Length && !char.IsWhiteSpace(_data[_index])) _index++;
            return _data.Substring(start, _index - start);
        }

        public int NextInt() => int.Parse(Next(), CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);

        public double NextDouble() => double.Parse(Next(), CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);
    }

    private readonly struct OrbitState
    {
        public readonly Vec3 R;
        public readonly Vec3 V;
        public readonly Vec3 H;
        public readonly double Energy;

        public OrbitState(Vec3 r, Vec3 v, Vec3 h, double energy)
        {
            R = r;
            V = v;
            H = h;
            Energy = energy;
        }
    }

    private readonly struct Vec3
    {
        public readonly double X;
        public readonly double Y;
        public readonly double Z;

        public Vec3(double x, double y, double z)
        {
            X = x;
            Y = y;
            Z = z;
        }

        public static Vec3 operator -(in Vec3 a, in Vec3 b) => new Vec3(a.X - b.X, a.Y - b.Y, a.Z - b.Z);

        public static Vec3 Cross(in Vec3 a, in Vec3 b)
        {
            return new Vec3(
                a.Y * b.Z - a.Z * b.Y,
                a.Z * b.X - a.X * b.Z,
                a.X * b.Y - a.Y * b.X);
        }

        public static double Dot(in Vec3 a, in Vec3 b) => a.X * b.X + a.Y * b.Y + a.Z * b.Z;

        public double Length() => Math.Sqrt(X * X + Y * Y + Z * Z);

        public static double Distance(in Vec3 a, in Vec3 b) => (a - b).Length();
    }
}
