Test 7: CSG Union of Two Polyhedra (C++) Score: 3.10 / 12
Typical Prompt:
You are implementing a CSG (Constructive Solid Geometry) Union operation in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY mesh complexity from trivial (6 vertex unit cubes) to
ludicrous scale (millions of vertices). The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: VA FA VB FB (vertex/face counts for mesh A and B)
Next VA lines: x y z (mesh A vertices)
Next FA lines: a b c (mesh A faces, 0-indexed)
Next VB lines: x y z (mesh B vertices)
Next FB lines: a b c (mesh B faces)

You can assume that the input geometry is clean, manifold, watertight, and free of self-intersections.

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: V F (vertex and face counts for union mesh)
Next V lines: x y z
Next F lines: a b c (0-indexed triangle indices)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on mesh complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for very complex meshes
3. **Correctness**: Must output correct union geometry. Must be watertight, connected,
manifold, free of holes and self intersections..

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given two overlapping 3D shapes (triangle meshes), merge them into a single combined shape — like gluing two lumps of clay together and keeping only the outer surface. The output mesh must be watertight with no self-intersections.

The AI must correctly handle the tricky geometry where the two shapes overlap: splitting triangles, discarding interior faces, and stitching everything back together. Subpasses use increasingly complex shape pairs.

 0.0000
[overlapping_boxes] TOPOLOGY ISSUES: 3 boundary edges (mesh has holes, not watertight). Result vol: 15.00, Expected (MC): ~15.04, Inputs: 8.00 + 8.00, Time: 0.1s

CSG Union - overlapping_boxes

Approach: CSG Union solver Approach: - Build BVHs for both meshes. - For each triangle, find intersections with the other mesh. - Split intersected triangles into planar fragments using a local segment graph. - Classify fragments with a ray-casting inside test against the other mesh. - Emit the kept fragments, deduplicating vertices and exact duplicate triangles.

View Code (28455 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <array>
#include <cmath>
#include <cstdint>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <numeric>
#include <queue>
#include <stack>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <unordered_set>
#include <vector>

using std::array;
using std::int64_t;
using std::size_t;
using std::vector;

static constexpr double INF_D = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();

struct Vec2 {
    double x = 0, y = 0;
    Vec2() = default;
    Vec2(double xx, double yy) : x(xx), y(yy) {}
    Vec2 operator+(const Vec2& o) const { return {x + o.x, y + o.y}; }
    Vec2 operator-(const Vec2& o) const { return {x - o.x, y - o.y}; }
    Vec2 operator*(double s) const { return {x * s, y * s}; }
};

struct Vec3 {
    double x = 0, y = 0, z = 0;
    Vec3() = default;
    Vec3(double xx, double yy, double zz) : x(xx), y(yy), z(zz) {}
    Vec3 operator+(const Vec3& o) const { return {x + o.x, y + o.y, z + o.z}; }
    Vec3 operator-(const Vec3& o) const { return {x - o.x, y - o.y, z - o.z}; }
    Vec3 operator*(double s) const { return {x * s, y * s, z * s}; }
    Vec3 operator/(double s) const { return {x / s, y / s, z / s}; }
};

static inline double dot(const Vec2& a, const Vec2& b) { return a.x * b.x + a.y * b.y; }
static inline double cross(const Vec2& a, const Vec2& b) { return a.x * b.y - a.y * b.x; }
static inline double dot(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) { return a.x * b.x + a.y * b.y + a.z * b.z; }
static inline Vec3 cross(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return {
        a.y * b.z - a.z * b.y,
        a.z * b.x - a.x * b.z,
        a.x * b.y - a.y * b.x
    };
}
static inline double norm2(const Vec2& a) { return dot(a, a); }
static inline double norm2(const Vec3& a) { return dot(a, a); }
static inline double norm(const Vec2& a) { return std::sqrt(norm2(a)); }
static inline double norm(const Vec3& a) { return std::sqrt(norm2(a)); }
static inline Vec3 normalize(const Vec3& a) {
    double n = norm(a);
    if (n == 0.0) return a;
    return a / n;
}

struct AABB {
    Vec3 mn, mx;
    AABB() {
        mn = {INF_D, INF_D, INF_D};
        mx = {-INF_D, -INF_D, -INF_D};
    }
    void expand(const Vec3& p) {
        mn.x = std::min(mn.x, p.x); mn.y = std::min(mn.y, p.y); mn.z = std::min(mn.z, p.z);
        mx.x = std::max(mx.x, p.x); mx.y = std::max(mx.y, p.y); mx.z = std::max(mx.z, p.z);
    }
    void expand(const AABB& o) {
        expand(o.mn); expand(o.mx);
    }
};

static inline bool overlap(const AABB& a, const AABB& b, double eps) {
    return !(a.mx.x + eps < b.mn.x || b.mx.x + eps < a.mn.x ||
             a.mx.y + eps < b.mn.y || b.mx.y + eps < a.mn.y ||
             a.mx.z + eps < b.mn.z || b.mx.z + eps < a.mn.z);
}

struct TriData {
    array<int, 3> id{};
    AABB box;
    Vec3 n;
    double d = 0.0;
};

struct BVHNode {
    AABB box;
    int left = -1, right = -1;
    int start = 0, count = 0;
    bool leaf = false;
};

struct QuantKey3 {
    long long x, y, z;
    bool operator==(const QuantKey3& o) const {
        return x == o.x && y == o.y && z == o.z;
    }
};

struct QuantKey3Hash {
    size_t operator()(const QuantKey3& k) const noexcept {
        auto mix = [](uint64_t v) {
            v ^= v >> 33;
            v *= 0xff51afd7ed558ccdULL;
            v ^= v >> 33;
            v *= 0xc4ceb9fe1a85ec53ULL;
            v ^= v >> 33;
            return v;
        };
        uint64_t h1 = mix((uint64_t)k.x);
        uint64_t h2 = mix((uint64_t)k.y);
        uint64_t h3 = mix((uint64_t)k.z);
        return (size_t)(h1 ^ (h2 << 1) ^ (h3 << 2));
    }
};

static inline long long qround(double v, double scale) {
    return (long long)std::llround(v / scale);
}

static inline QuantKey3 quantKey3(const Vec3& p, double scale) {
    return {qround(p.x, scale), qround(p.y, scale), qround(p.z, scale)};
}

struct FaceKey {
    int a, b, c;
    bool operator==(const FaceKey& o) const { return a == o.a && b == o.b && c == o.c; }
};

struct FaceKeyHash {
    size_t operator()(const FaceKey& k) const noexcept {
        uint64_t x = (uint64_t)k.a * 1315423911u ^ (uint64_t)k.b * 2654435761u ^ (uint64_t)k.c * 97531u;
        x ^= x >> 33;
        x *= 0xff51afd7ed558ccdULL;
        x ^= x >> 33;
        x *= 0xc4ceb9fe1a85ec53ULL;
        x ^= x >> 33;
        return (size_t)x;
    }
};

struct Mesh {
    vector<Vec3> v;
    vector<array<int, 3>> f;
    vector<TriData> tris;
    vector<BVHNode> bvh;
    vector<int> triOrder;
    Vec3 bbMin, bbMax;
    double scale = 1.0;
    Vec3 rayDir;
    double eps = 1e-9;

    void build();
    bool pointInside(const Vec3& p) const;
    void queryTriCandidates(int node, const AABB& box, vector<int>& out) const;
    void queryRayCandidates(int node, const Vec3& orig, const Vec3& dir, vector<int>& out) const;
};

static AABB triBox(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b, const Vec3& c) {
    AABB box;
    box.expand(a); box.expand(b); box.expand(c);
    return box;
}

static bool pointInTri3D(const Vec3& p, const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b, const Vec3& c, const Vec3& n, double eps) {
    Vec3 c0 = cross(b - a, p - a);
    Vec3 c1 = cross(c - b, p - b);
    Vec3 c2 = cross(a - c, p - c);
    return dot(n, c0) >= -eps && dot(n, c1) >= -eps && dot(n, c2) >= -eps;
}

static bool rayTriIntersect(const Vec3& orig, const Vec3& dir,
                            const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b, const Vec3& c,
                            double eps) {
    Vec3 e1 = b - a;
    Vec3 e2 = c - a;
    Vec3 p = cross(dir, e2);
    double det = dot(e1, p);
    if (std::fabs(det) < eps) return false;
    double invDet = 1.0 / det;
    Vec3 t = orig - a;
    double u = dot(t, p) * invDet;
    if (u < -eps || u > 1.0 + eps) return false;
    Vec3 q = cross(t, e1);
    double v = dot(dir, q) * invDet;
    if (v < -eps || u + v > 1.0 + eps) return false;
    double tt = dot(e2, q) * invDet;
    return tt > eps;
}

static bool triTriIntersectSegment(const TriData& A, const TriData& B,
                                   const vector<Vec3>& av, const vector<Vec3>& bv,
                                   Vec3& s0, Vec3& s1, double eps) {
    double da0 = dot(B.n, av[A.id[0]]) + B.d;
    double da1 = dot(B.n, av[A.id[1]]) + B.d;
    double da2 = dot(B.n, av[A.id[2]]) + B.d;
    double db0 = dot(A.n, bv[B.id[0]]) + A.d;
    double db1 = dot(A.n, bv[B.id[1]]) + A.d;
    double db2 = dot(A.n, bv[B.id[2]]) + A.d;

    bool aAllPos = (da0 > eps && da1 > eps && da2 > eps);
    bool aAllNeg = (da0 < -eps && da1 < -eps && da2 < -eps);
    bool bAllPos = (db0 > eps && db1 > eps && db2 > eps);
    bool bAllNeg = (db0 < -eps && db1 < -eps && db2 < -eps);
    if ((aAllPos || aAllNeg) || (bAllPos || bAllNeg)) return false;

    Vec3 dir = cross(A.n, B.n);
    if (norm2(dir) < eps * eps) {
        // Parallel or coplanar: skipped by this routine.
        return false;
    }
    dir = normalize(dir);

    vector<Vec3> pts;
    auto addPt = [&](const Vec3& p) {
        for (const Vec3& q : pts) {
            if (norm2(p - q) <= eps * eps * 16.0) return;
        }
        pts.push_back(p);
    };

    auto edgeTest = [&](const Vec3& p, const Vec3& q, double dp, double dq, const Vec3& oppA0, const Vec3& oppA1, const Vec3& oppA2, const Vec3& onN, double onD) {
        if (std::fabs(dp) <= eps && pointInTri3D(p, oppA0, oppA1, oppA2, onN, eps * 4.0)) {
            addPt(p);
        }
        if ((dp > eps && dq < -eps) || (dp < -eps && dq > eps)) {
            double t = dp / (dp - dq);
            Vec3 x = p + (q - p) * t;
            if (pointInTri3D(x, oppA0, oppA1, oppA2, onN, eps * 4.0)) addPt(x);
        } else if (std::fabs(dp) <= eps && std::fabs(dq) <= eps) {
            // Edge lies in opposite plane. Add endpoints if they lie in the opposite triangle.
            if (pointInTri3D(p, oppA0, oppA1, oppA2, onN, eps * 4.0)) addPt(p);
            if (pointInTri3D(q, oppA0, oppA1, oppA2, onN, eps * 4.0)) addPt(q);
        }
    };

    array<Vec3, 3> apts{av[A.id[0]], av[A.id[1]], av[A.id[2]]};
    array<Vec3, 3> bpts{bv[B.id[0]], bv[B.id[1]], bv[B.id[2]]};
    array<double, 3> da{da0, da1, da2};
    array<double, 3> db{db0, db1, db2};

    for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i) {
        int j = (i + 1) % 3;
        edgeTest(apts[i], apts[j], da[i], da[j], bpts[0], bpts[1], bpts[2], B.n, B.d);
    }
    for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i) {
        int j = (i + 1) % 3;
        edgeTest(bpts[i], bpts[j], db[i], db[j], apts[0], apts[1], apts[2], A.n, A.d);
    }

    if (pts.size() < 2) return false;

    double best = -1.0;
    Vec3 p0{}, p1{};
    for (size_t i = 0; i < pts.size(); ++i) {
        for (size_t j = i + 1; j < pts.size(); ++j) {
            double d = norm2(pts[i] - pts[j]);
            if (d > best) {
                best = d;
                p0 = pts[i];
                p1 = pts[j];
            }
        }
    }
    if (best <= eps * eps * 16.0) return false;
    s0 = p0;
    s1 = p1;
    return true;
}

static bool bboxRayHit(const AABB& box, const Vec3& orig, const Vec3& dir, double eps, double& tmin, double& tmax) {
    tmin = 0.0;
    tmax = INF_D;
    for (int k = 0; k < 3; ++k) {
        double o = (k == 0 ? orig.x : (k == 1 ? orig.y : orig.z));
        double d = (k == 0 ? dir.x : (k == 1 ? dir.y : dir.z));
        double mn = (k == 0 ? box.mn.x : (k == 1 ? box.mn.y : box.mn.z));
        double mx = (k == 0 ? box.mx.x : (k == 1 ? box.mx.y : box.mx.z));
        if (std::fabs(d) < eps) {
            if (o < mn - eps || o > mx + eps) return false;
            continue;
        }
        double inv = 1.0 / d;
        double t1 = (mn - o) * inv;
        double t2 = (mx - o) * inv;
        if (t1 > t2) std::swap(t1, t2);
        tmin = std::max(tmin, t1);
        tmax = std::min(tmax, t2);
        if (tmin > tmax) return false;
    }
    return true;
}

void Mesh::queryTriCandidates(int node, const AABB& box, vector<int>& out) const {
    if (node < 0 || bvh.empty()) return;
    const BVHNode& nd = bvh[node];
    if (!overlap(nd.box, box, eps)) return;
    if (nd.leaf) {
        for (int i = nd.start; i < nd.start + nd.count; ++i) out.push_back(triOrder[i]);
        return;
    }
    queryTriCandidates(nd.left, box, out);
    queryTriCandidates(nd.right, box, out);
}

void Mesh::queryRayCandidates(int node, const Vec3& orig, const Vec3& dir, vector<int>& out) const {
    if (node < 0 || bvh.empty()) return;
    const BVHNode& nd = bvh[node];
    double t0, t1;
    if (!bboxRayHit(nd.box, orig, dir, eps, t0, t1)) return;
    if (nd.leaf) {
        for (int i = nd.start; i < nd.start + nd.count; ++i) out.push_back(triOrder[i]);
        return;
    }
    queryRayCandidates(nd.left, orig, dir, out);
    queryRayCandidates(nd.right, orig, dir, out);
}

static int buildBVHRec(vector<BVHNode>& nodes, const vector<TriData>& tris, vector<int>& order, int l, int r) {
    int nodeId = (int)nodes.size();
    nodes.push_back({});
    AABB box;
    for (int i = l; i < r; ++i) box.expand(tris[order[i]].box);
    nodes[nodeId].box = box;
    int cnt = r - l;
    if (cnt <= 8) {
        nodes[nodeId].leaf = true;
        nodes[nodeId].start = l;
        nodes[nodeId].count = cnt;
        return nodeId;
    }
    AABB cbox;
    for (int i = l; i < r; ++i) {
        const TriData& t = tris[order[i]];
        Vec3 c{(t.box.mn.x + t.box.mx.x) * 0.5, (t.box.mn.y + t.box.mx.y) * 0.5, (t.box.mn.z + t.box.mx.z) * 0.5};
        cbox.expand(c);
    }
    Vec3 ext = cbox.mx - cbox.mn;
    int axis = 0;
    if (ext.y >= ext.x && ext.y >= ext.z) axis = 1;
    else if (ext.z >= ext.x && ext.z >= ext.y) axis = 2;
    int m = (l + r) / 2;
    std::nth_element(order.begin() + l, order.begin() + m, order.begin() + r, [&](int a, int b) {
        Vec3 ca{(tris[a].box.mn.x + tris[a].box.mx.x) * 0.5, (tris[a].box.mn.y + tris[a].box.mx.y) * 0.5, (tris[a].box.mn.z + tris[a].box.mx.z) * 0.5};
        Vec3 cb{(tris[b].box.mn.x + tris[b].box.mx.x) * 0.5, (tris[b].box.mn.y + tris[b].box.mx.y) * 0.5, (tris[b].box.mn.z + tris[b].box.mx.z) * 0.5};
        if (axis == 0) return ca.x < cb.x;
        if (axis == 1) return ca.y < cb.y;
        return ca.z < cb.z;
    });
    nodes[nodeId].left = buildBVHRec(nodes, tris, order, l, m);
    nodes[nodeId].right = buildBVHRec(nodes, tris, order, m, r);
    nodes[nodeId].leaf = false;
    return nodeId;
}

void Mesh::build() {
    tris.clear();
    tris.reserve(f.size());
    bbMin = {INF_D, INF_D, INF_D};
    bbMax = {-INF_D, -INF_D, -INF_D};
    for (const auto& p : v) {
        bbMin.x = std::min(bbMin.x, p.x); bbMin.y = std::min(bbMin.y, p.y); bbMin.z = std::min(bbMin.z, p.z);
        bbMax.x = std::max(bbMax.x, p.x); bbMax.y = std::max(bbMax.y, p.y); bbMax.z = std::max(bbMax.z, p.z);
    }
    scale = std::max({bbMax.x - bbMin.x, bbMax.y - bbMin.y, bbMax.z - bbMin.z, 1.0});
    eps = scale * 1e-9;
    rayDir = normalize(Vec3{1.0, 0.234567890123, 0.543210987654});

    double signedVol = 0.0;
    for (auto tri : f) {
        const Vec3& a = v[tri[0]];
        const Vec3& b = v[tri[1]];
        const Vec3& c = v[tri[2]];
        signedVol += dot(a, cross(b, c));
    }
    if (signedVol < 0.0) {
        for (auto& tri : f) std::swap(tri[1], tri[2]);
    }

    for (const auto& tri : f) {
        TriData t;
        t.id = tri;
        const Vec3& a = v[tri[0]];
        const Vec3& b = v[tri[1]];
        const Vec3& c = v[tri[2]];
        t.box = triBox(a, b, c);
        t.n = normalize(cross(b - a, c - a));
        t.d = -dot(t.n, a);
        tris.push_back(t);
    }

    triOrder.resize(tris.size());
    std::iota(triOrder.begin(), triOrder.end(), 0);
    bvh.clear();
    if (!tris.empty()) {
        buildBVHRec(bvh, tris, triOrder, 0, (int)triOrder.size());
    }
}

bool Mesh::pointInside(const Vec3& p) const {
    vector<int> cand;
    cand.reserve(64);
    queryRayCandidates(0, p, rayDir, cand);
    int hits = 0;
    for (int idx : cand) {
        const TriData& t = tris[idx];
        const Vec3& a = v[t.id[0]];
        const Vec3& b = v[t.id[1]];
        const Vec3& c = v[t.id[2]];
        if (rayTriIntersect(p, rayDir, a, b, c, eps * 8.0)) ++hits;
    }
    return (hits & 1) != 0;
}

struct LocalPoint {
    Vec2 p2;
    Vec3 p3;
};

struct LocalPointKey {
    long long x, y;
    bool operator==(const LocalPointKey& o) const { return x == o.x && y == o.y; }
};

struct LocalPointKeyHash {
    size_t operator()(const LocalPointKey& k) const noexcept {
        uint64_t h = (uint64_t)k.x * 1315423911u ^ (uint64_t)k.y * 2654435761u;
        h ^= h >> 33;
        h *= 0xff51afd7ed558ccdULL;
        h ^= h >> 33;
        h *= 0xc4ceb9fe1a85ec53ULL;
        h ^= h >> 33;
        return (size_t)h;
    }
};

static inline LocalPointKey quantKey2(const Vec2& p, double s) {
    return {qround(p.x, s), qround(p.y, s)};
}

static bool pointOnSeg2(const Vec2& p, const Vec2& a, const Vec2& b, double eps) {
    Vec2 ap = p - a, bp = p - b, ab = b - a;
    if (std::fabs(cross(ab, ap)) > eps * 8.0) return false;
    double d1 = dot(ap, ab);
    double d2 = dot(bp, ab);
    return d1 >= -eps * 8.0 && d2 <= eps * 8.0;
}

static double segParam(const Vec2& p, const Vec2& a, const Vec2& b) {
    Vec2 ab = b - a;
    double l2 = norm2(ab);
    if (l2 == 0.0) return 0.0;
    return dot(p - a, ab) / l2;
}

static double polygonArea2(const vector<int>& poly, const vector<LocalPoint>& pts) {
    double a = 0.0;
    int n = (int)poly.size();
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        const Vec2& p = pts[poly[i]].p2;
        const Vec2& q = pts[poly[(i + 1) % n]].p2;
        a += cross(p, q);
    }
    return 0.5 * a;
}

static bool pointInTriangle2(const Vec2& p, const Vec2& a, const Vec2& b, const Vec2& c, double eps) {
    double c1 = cross(b - a, p - a);
    double c2 = cross(c - b, p - b);
    double c3 = cross(a - c, p - c);
    return c1 >= -eps && c2 >= -eps && c3 >= -eps;
}

static bool earClipTriangulate(const vector<int>& polyIn, const vector<LocalPoint>& pts, vector<array<int, 3>>& outTris) {
    outTris.clear();
    if (polyIn.size() < 3) return false;
    vector<int> poly = polyIn;
    double area = polygonArea2(poly, pts);
    if (std::fabs(area) < 1e-18) return false;
    if (area < 0.0) std::reverse(poly.begin(), poly.end());

    vector<int> idx = poly;
    int guard = 0;
    while (idx.size() > 3 && guard < 10000) {
        ++guard;
        bool cut = false;
        int n = (int)idx.size();
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            int ip = idx[(i - 1 + n) % n];
            int ic = idx[i];
            int in = idx[(i + 1) % n];
            const Vec2& a = pts[ip].p2;
            const Vec2& b = pts[ic].p2;
            const Vec2& c = pts[in].p2;
            double cr = cross(b - a, c - b);
            if (cr <= 1e-18) continue;
            bool anyInside = false;
            for (int j = 0; j < n; ++j) {
                int id = idx[j];
                if (id == ip || id == ic || id == in) continue;
                if (pointInTriangle2(pts[id].p2, a, b, c, 1e-12)) {
                    anyInside = true;
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (anyInside) continue;
            outTris.push_back({ip, ic, in});
            idx.erase(idx.begin() + i);
            cut = true;
            break;
        }
        if (!cut) break;
    }
    if (idx.size() == 3) outTris.push_back({idx[0], idx[1], idx[2]});
    if (outTris.empty()) {
        // Fallback fan triangulation.
        for (size_t i = 1; i + 1 < poly.size(); ++i) {
            outTris.push_back({poly[0], poly[(int)i], poly[(int)i + 1]});
        }
    }
    return !outTris.empty();
}

struct UnionBuilder {
    const Mesh& A;
    const Mesh& B;
    vector<Vec3> outV;
    vector<array<int, 3>> outF;
    std::unordered_map<QuantKey3, int, QuantKey3Hash> vmap;
    std::unordered_set<FaceKey, FaceKeyHash> fmap;
    double gScale;
    double keyScale;

    UnionBuilder(const Mesh& a, const Mesh& b) : A(a), B(b) {
        Vec3 mn{
            std::min(A.bbMin.x, B.bbMin.x),
            std::min(A.bbMin.y, B.bbMin.y),
            std::min(A.bbMin.z, B.bbMin.z)
        };
        Vec3 mx{
            std::max(A.bbMax.x, B.bbMax.x),
            std::max(A.bbMax.y, B.bbMax.y),
            std::max(A.bbMax.z, B.bbMax.z)
        };
        gScale = std::max({mx.x - mn.x, mx.y - mn.y, mx.z - mn.z, 1.0});
        keyScale = gScale * 1e-8;
    }

    int addVertex(const Vec3& p) {
        QuantKey3 k = quantKey3(p, keyScale);
        auto it = vmap.find(k);
        if (it != vmap.end()) return it->second;
        int id = (int)outV.size();
        outV.push_back(p);
        vmap.emplace(k, id);
        return id;
    }

    void addTri(const Vec3& p0, const Vec3& p1, const Vec3& p2) {
        int a = addVertex(p0);
        int b = addVertex(p1);
        int c = addVertex(p2);
        if (a == b || b == c || a == c) return;
        FaceKey k{a, b, c};
        int x = k.a, y = k.b, z = k.c;
        if (x > y) std::swap(x, y);
        if (y > z) std::swap(y, z);
        if (x > y) std::swap(x, y);
        FaceKey ck{x, y, z};
        if (!fmap.insert(ck).second) return;
        outF.push_back({a, b, c});
    }

    Vec3 localToWorld(const Vec3& o, const Vec3& e1, const Vec3& e2, const Vec2& p) const {
        return o + e1 * p.x + e2 * p.y;
    }

    void processTriangle(const Mesh& me, const Mesh& other, int triIdx) {
        const TriData& td = me.tris[triIdx];
        const Vec3& p0 = me.v[td.id[0]];
        const Vec3& p1 = me.v[td.id[1]];
        const Vec3& p2 = me.v[td.id[2]];

        Vec3 e1 = normalize(p1 - p0);
        Vec3 n = normalize(cross(p1 - p0, p2 - p0));
        Vec3 e2 = cross(n, e1);

        vector<LocalPoint> pts;
        pts.reserve(64);
        std::unordered_map<LocalPointKey, int, LocalPointKeyHash> pmap;
        double localScale = me.scale * 1e-8;

        auto project = [&](const Vec3& p) -> Vec2 {
            Vec3 d = p - p0;
            return {dot(d, e1), dot(d, e2)};
        };

        auto addPoint = [&](const Vec3& p) -> int {
            Vec2 q = project(p);
            LocalPointKey k = quantKey2(q, localScale);
            auto it = pmap.find(k);
            if (it != pmap.end()) {
                int id = it->second;
                // Keep the first geometry sample.
                return id;
            }
            int id = (int)pts.size();
            pts.push_back({q, p});
            pmap.emplace(k, id);
            return id;
        };

        int v0 = addPoint(p0);
        int v1 = addPoint(p1);
        int v2 = addPoint(p2);

        struct Seg { int a, b; };
        vector<Seg> segs;
        vector<int> cand;
        cand.reserve(64);
        other.queryTriCandidates(0, td.box, cand);
        std::sort(cand.begin(), cand.end());
        cand.erase(std::unique(cand.begin(), cand.end()), cand.end());

        for (int j : cand) {
            const TriData& od = other.tris[j];
            Vec3 s0, s1;
            if (!triTriIntersectSegment(td, od, me.v, other.v, s0, s1, std::max(me.eps, other.eps) * 8.0)) continue;
            int a = addPoint(s0);
            int b = addPoint(s1);
            if (a == b) continue;
            segs.push_back({a, b});
        }

        // If no split segments exist, classify the whole triangle directly.
        if (segs.empty()) {
            Vec3 sample = (p0 + p1 + p2) / 3.0 + n * (me.scale * 1e-8);
            if (!other.pointInside(sample)) {
                addTri(p0, p1, p2);
            }
            return;
        }

        // Build boundary point lists per triangle edge.
        array<vector<std::pair<double, int>>, 3> edgePts;
        auto addEdgePoint = [&](int ei, int pid) {
            const Vec2& P = pts[pid].p2;
            const Vec2& A = pts[ei == 0 ? v0 : (ei == 1 ? v1 : v2)].p2;
            const Vec2& B = pts[ei == 0 ? v1 : (ei == 1 ? v2 : v0)].p2;
            if (!pointOnSeg2(P, A, B, localScale * 8.0)) return;
            double t = segParam(P, A, B);
            edgePts[ei].push_back({t, pid});
        };

        for (int i = 0; i < (int)pts.size(); ++i) {
            addEdgePoint(0, i);
            addEdgePoint(1, i);
            addEdgePoint(2, i);
        }

        vector<std::pair<int, int>> edges;
        auto addEdge = [&](int a, int b) {
            if (a == b) return;
            if (a > b) std::swap(a, b);
            edges.push_back({a, b});
        };

        auto addBoundaryEdges = [&](int ei) {
            auto& lst = edgePts[ei];
            if (lst.empty()) return;
            std::sort(lst.begin(), lst.end(), [](const auto& A, const auto& B) {
                if (std::fabs(A.first - B.first) > 1e-12) return A.first < B.first;
                return A.second < B.second;
            });
            vector<int> ids;
            ids.reserve(lst.size());
            for (auto& pr : lst) {
                if (ids.empty() || ids.back() != pr.second) ids.push_back(pr.second);
            }
            for (size_t i = 1; i < ids.size(); ++i) addEdge(ids[i - 1], ids[i]);
        };
        addBoundaryEdges(0);
        addBoundaryEdges(1);
        addBoundaryEdges(2);
        for (const auto& s : segs) addEdge(s.a, s.b);

        if (edges.empty()) {
            Vec3 sample = (p0 + p1 + p2) / 3.0 + n * (me.scale * 1e-8);
            if (!other.pointInside(sample)) addTri(p0, p1, p2);
            return;
        }

        struct HE {
            int from = -1, to = -1, twin = -1, next = -1;
            double ang = 0.0;
        };

        vector<HE> hes;
        hes.reserve(edges.size() * 2 + 8);
        vector<vector<int>> out((int)pts.size());
        auto addUndirected = [&](int a, int b) {
            int h = (int)hes.size();
            hes.push_back({a, b, h + 1, -1, 0.0});
            hes.push_back({b, a, h, -1, 0.0});
            out[a].push_back(h);
            out[b].push_back(h + 1);
        };
        std::sort(edges.begin(), edges.end());
        edges.erase(std::unique(edges.begin(), edges.end()), edges.end());
        for (auto [a, b] : edges) addUndirected(a, b);

        vector<vector<int>> ord((int)pts.size());
        for (int i = 0; i < (int)pts.size(); ++i) {
            ord[i] = out[i];
            const Vec2& O = pts[i].p2;
            std::sort(ord[i].begin(), ord[i].end(), [&](int ha, int hb) {
                Vec2 da{pts[hes[ha].to].p2.x - O.x, pts[hes[ha].to].p2.y - O.y};
                Vec2 db{pts[hes[hb].to].p2.x - O.x, pts[hes[hb].to].p2.y - O.y};
                double aa = std::atan2(da.y, da.x);
                double ab = std::atan2(db.y, db.x);
                if (std::fabs(aa - ab) > 1e-15) return aa < ab;
                return hes[ha].to < hes[hb].to;
            });
            for (size_t j = 0; j < ord[i].size(); ++j) {
                int h = ord[i][j];
                hes[h].ang = std::atan2(pts[hes[h].to].p2.y - O.y, pts[hes[h].to].p2.x - O.x);
            }
        }

        for (int h = 0; h < (int)hes.size(); ++h) {
            int v = hes[h].to;
            const auto& lst = ord[v];
            if (lst.empty()) continue;
            int twin = hes[h].twin;
            int pos = -1;
            for (int i = 0; i < (int)lst.size(); ++i) {
                if (lst[i] == twin) { pos = i; break; }
            }
            if (pos < 0) continue;
            int prev = (pos - 1 + (int)lst.size()) % (int)lst.size();
            hes[h].next = lst[prev];
        }

        vector<char> vis(hes.size(), 0);
        vector<vector<int>> faces;
        for (int h = 0; h < (int)hes.size(); ++h) {
            if (vis[h] || hes[h].next < 0) continue;
            vector<int> cyc;
            int cur = h;
            int limit = 0;
            while (!vis[cur] && limit++ < 10000) {
                vis[cur] = 1;
                cyc.push_back(hes[cur].from);
                cur = hes[cur].next;
                if (cur < 0) break;
                if (cur == h) {
                    cyc.push_back(hes[cur].from);
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (cyc.size() < 3) continue;
            if (cyc.front() == cyc.back()) cyc.pop_back();
            // Remove consecutive duplicates.
            vector<int> cleaned;
            for (int id : cyc) {
                if (cleaned.empty() || cleaned.back() != id) cleaned.push_back(id);
            }
            if (cleaned.size() >= 2 && cleaned.front() == cleaned.back()) cleaned.pop_back();
            if (cleaned.size() < 3) continue;
            double a = polygonArea2(cleaned, pts);
            if (a > 1e-18) faces.push_back(std::move(cleaned));
        }

        if (faces.empty()) {
            Vec3 sample = (p0 + p1 + p2) / 3.0 + n * (me.scale * 1e-8);
            if (!other.pointInside(sample)) addTri(p0, p1, p2);
            return;
        }

        for (const auto& face : faces) {
            vector<array<int, 3>> triList;
            if (!earClipTriangulate(face, pts, triList)) continue;
            if (triList.empty()) continue;
            const auto& t0 = triList[0];
            Vec3 sample = (pts[t0[0]].p3 + pts[t0[1]].p3 + pts[t0[2]].p3) / 3.0 + n * (me.scale * 1e-8);
            bool keep = !other.pointInside(sample);
            if (!keep) continue;
            for (const auto& tri : triList) {
                addTri(pts[tri[0]].p3, pts[tri[1]].p3, pts[tri[2]].p3);
            }
        }
    }

    void run() {
        for (int i = 0; i < (int)A.tris.size(); ++i) processTriangle(A, B, i);
        for (int i = 0; i < (int)B.tris.size(); ++i) processTriangle(B, A, i);
    }
};

int main() {
    std::ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(nullptr);

    int VA, FA, VB, FB;
    if (!(std::cin >> VA >> FA >> VB >> FB)) return 0;

    Mesh A, B;
    A.v.resize(VA);
    A.f.resize(FA);
    B.v.resize(VB);
    B.f.resize(FB);

    for (int i = 0; i < VA; ++i) {
        std::cin >> A.v[i].x >> A.v[i].y >> A.v[i].z;
    }
    for (int i = 0; i < FA; ++i) {
        std::cin >> A.f[i][0] >> A.f[i][1] >> A.f[i][2];
    }
    for (int i = 0; i < VB; ++i) {
        std::cin >> B.v[i].x >> B.v[i].y >> B.v[i].z;
    }
    for (int i = 0; i < FB; ++i) {
        std::cin >> B.f[i][0] >> B.f[i][1] >> B.f[i][2];
    }

    A.build();
    B.build();

    UnionBuilder ub(A, B);
    ub.run();

    std::cout << ub.outV.size() << ' ' << ub.outF.size() << '\n';
    std::cout.setf(std::ios::fixed);
    std::cout.precision(10);
    for (const auto& p : ub.outV) {
        std::cout << p.x << ' ' << p.y << ' ' << p.z << '\n';
    }
    for (const auto& t : ub.outF) {
        std::cout << t[0] << ' ' << t[1] << ' ' << t[2] << '\n';
    }
    return 0;
}

Test 8: Maze Solver (Rust) Score: 1.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a maze solver in Rust that must handle ANY maze complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale.

Your program will be tested with mazes ranging from 5x5 to 50000x50000.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Maze format:**
- 'A' = Start position
- 'B' = Goal/End position
- '#' = Wall (cannot pass through)
- ' ' (space) = Open path (can walk through)

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: H W (height and width)
Next H lines: the maze row strings (each W characters)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: N (number of steps in path)
Next N lines: x y (0-indexed coordinates from top-left)
Path should include both start (A) and end (B) positions.
Movement is only up/down/left/right (no diagonals).
Output nothing (or 0) if no path exists.

**Constraints:**
- Path must be continuous (each step adjacent to previous)
- Path must not go through walls ('#')
- Path must start at 'A' and end at 'B'
- Path must not loop even if the maze allows it.
- If multiple solutions exist, return any one.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Find the shortest path through a maze from a start cell to an end cell. The maze is a grid of walls and open passages, and the AI must navigate from one corner to the other using the fewest steps possible.

Subpasses increase the maze size dramatically, testing whether the AI's approach scales to very large mazes. The baseline finds the true shortest path using breadth-first search.

 1.0000
[5x5] Path found, Time: 0.1s - path length 5

Maze Solver - 5x5

View Code (4080 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead, BufReader, Write};

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut reader = BufReader::new(stdin.lock());

    let mut first_line = Vec::new();
    if reader.read_until(b'\n', &mut first_line).unwrap() == 0 {
        return;
    }
    while matches!(first_line.last(), Some(b'\n' | b'\r')) {
        first_line.pop();
    }

    let first = String::from_utf8(first_line).unwrap();
    let mut it = first.split_whitespace();
    let h: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let w: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let total = h.checked_mul(w).expect("grid too large");

    // Cell encoding:
    // 254 = wall
    // 255 = unvisited open cell
    // 252 = start cell
    // 0..3 = parent direction to reach the previous cell
    let mut cells = vec![255u8; total];
    let mut start = None::<usize>;
    let mut goal = None::<usize>;

    for r in 0..h {
        let mut line = Vec::new();
        reader.read_until(b'\n', &mut line).unwrap();
        while matches!(line.last(), Some(b'\n' | b'\r')) {
            line.pop();
        }

        let base = r * w;
        for c in 0..w {
            let idx = base + c;
            let ch = *line.get(c).unwrap_or(&b' ');
            cells[idx] = match ch {
                b'#' => 254,
                b'A' => {
                    start = Some(idx);
                    252
                }
                b'B' => {
                    goal = Some(idx);
                    255
                }
                b' ' => 255,
                _ => 254,
            };
        }
    }

    let start = match start {
        Some(v) => v,
        None => {
            let mut out = io::BufWriter::new(io::stdout().lock());
            writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
            return;
        }
    };
    let goal = match goal {
        Some(v) => v,
        None => {
            let mut out = io::BufWriter::new(io::stdout().lock());
            writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
            return;
        }
    };

    if start == goal {
        let mut out = io::BufWriter::new(io::stdout().lock());
        writeln!(out, "1").unwrap();
        writeln!(out, "{} {}", start / w, start % w).unwrap();
        return;
    }

    let mut queue = Vec::<u32>::new();
    queue.push(start as u32);
    let mut head = 0usize;
    let mut found = false;

    while head < queue.len() {
        let cur = queue[head] as usize;
        head += 1;
        if cur == goal {
            found = true;
            break;
        }

        let r = cur / w;
        let c = cur % w;

        if r > 0 {
            let ni = cur - w;
            if cells[ni] == 255 {
                cells[ni] = 1; // parent is below
                queue.push(ni as u32);
            }
        }
        if r + 1 < h {
            let ni = cur + w;
            if cells[ni] == 255 {
                cells[ni] = 0; // parent is above
                queue.push(ni as u32);
            }
        }
        if c > 0 {
            let ni = cur - 1;
            if cells[ni] == 255 {
                cells[ni] = 3; // parent is right
                queue.push(ni as u32);
            }
        }
        if c + 1 < w {
            let ni = cur + 1;
            if cells[ni] == 255 {
                cells[ni] = 2; // parent is left
                queue.push(ni as u32);
            }
        }
    }

    let mut out = io::BufWriter::new(io::stdout().lock());
    if !found {
        writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
        return;
    }

    let mut path = Vec::<usize>::new();
    let mut cur = goal;
    loop {
        path.push(cur);
        if cur == start {
            break;
        }
        cur = match cells[cur] {
            0 => cur - w,
            1 => cur + w,
            2 => cur - 1,
            3 => cur + 1,
            _ => {
                writeln!(out, "0").unwrap();
                return;
            }
        };
    }

    path.reverse();
    writeln!(out, "{}", path.len()).unwrap();
    for idx in path {
        writeln!(out, "{} {}", idx / w, idx % w).unwrap();
    }
}

Test 9: 1D Cutting Stock - Minimum Waste (C#) Score: 0.00 / 15
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the 1D Cutting Stock Problem in C#.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N stock_length
Line 2: N cut lengths separated by spaces

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_stocks
Next num_stocks lines: space-separated cut indices assigned to that stock (0-indexed)

**Example:**
For cuts=[30, 40, 50] and stock_length=100:
Input:
3 100
30 40 50

Output:
2
0 1
2

**Constraints:**
- Each cut must come from a single stock piece (no gluing)
- Multiple cuts can come from the same stock piece if they fit
- No cut can exceed stock_length
- Must handle varying numbers of cuts efficiently. You can use
multiple threads, you can micro-optimise code, you can write clever algorithms,
whatever it takes to handle the problem and solve it within 5 minutes even if
there are millions of cuts.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given fixed-length stock material and a shopping list of smaller pieces to cut, use as few stock pieces as possible. Every requested piece must be cut from somewhere, but any leftover material is wasted — so packing the cuts tightly matters.

This is essentially a bin-packing problem and is NP-hard. Subpasses scale from a handful of cuts up to millions, forcing the AI to write code that is both correct and fast. The baseline uses a simple first-fit-decreasing heuristic.

Cutting Stock - 5 cuts, stock=100

View Code (8158 chars)
using System;
using System.IO;
using System.Text;

public static class Program
{
    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream = Console.OpenStandardInput();
        private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _len;
        private int _ptr;

        private int ReadByte()
        {
            if (_ptr >= _len)
            {
                _len = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _ptr = 0;
                if (_len <= 0)
                    return -1;
            }
            return _buffer[_ptr++];
        }

        public int ReadInt()
        {
            int c;
            do
            {
                c = ReadByte();
                if (c == -1)
                    return int.MinValue;
            } while (c <= 32);

            int sign = 1;
            if (c == '-')
            {
                sign = -1;
                c = ReadByte();
            }

            int value = 0;
            while (c > 32 && c != -1)
            {
                value = value * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = ReadByte();
            }
            return value * sign;
        }
    }

    private static int[] _left = Array.Empty<int>();
    private static int[] _right = Array.Empty<int>();
    private static int[] _priority = Array.Empty<int>();
    private static int[] _key = Array.Empty<int>();
    private static int[] _bucketHead = Array.Empty<int>();
    private static int[] _stockNext = Array.Empty<int>();
    private static int[] _stockHead = Array.Empty<int>();
    private static int[] _stockTail = Array.Empty<int>();
    private static int _root = -1;
    private static int _nodeCount;
    private static int _stockCount;
    private static int _stockLength;
    private static uint _rng = 2463534242u;

    private static int NextPriority()
    {
        uint x = _rng;
        x ^= x << 13;
        x ^= x >> 17;
        x ^= x << 5;
        _rng = x;
        return (int)(x & 0x7fffffff);
    }

    private static int NewNode(int key, int stockId)
    {
        int id = _nodeCount++;
        _key[id] = key;
        _priority[id] = NextPriority();
        _left[id] = -1;
        _right[id] = -1;
        _bucketHead[id] = stockId;
        _stockNext[stockId] = -1;
        return id;
    }

    private static int RotateRight(int p)
    {
        int q = _left[p];
        _left[p] = _right[q];
        _right[q] = p;
        return q;
    }

    private static int RotateLeft(int p)
    {
        int q = _right[p];
        _right[p] = _left[q];
        _left[q] = p;
        return q;
    }

    private static int InsertNode(int node, int key, int stockId)
    {
        if (node == -1)
            return NewNode(key, stockId);

        if (key < _key[node])
        {
            _left[node] = InsertNode(_left[node], key, stockId);
            if (_priority[_left[node]] > _priority[node])
                node = RotateRight(node);
        }
        else
        {
            _right[node] = InsertNode(_right[node], key, stockId);
            if (_priority[_right[node]] > _priority[node])
                node = RotateLeft(node);
        }
        return node;
    }

    private static int Merge(int a, int b)
    {
        if (a == -1) return b;
        if (b == -1) return a;
        if (_priority[a] > _priority[b])
        {
            _right[a] = Merge(_right[a], b);
            return a;
        }
        else
        {
            _left[b] = Merge(a, _left[b]);
            return b;
        }
    }

    private static int DeleteNode(int node, int key)
    {
        if (node == -1)
            return -1;

        if (key < _key[node])
        {
            _left[node] = DeleteNode(_left[node], key);
            return node;
        }

        if (key > _key[node])
        {
            _right[node] = DeleteNode(_right[node], key);
            return node;
        }

        return Merge(_left[node], _right[node]);
    }

    private static int FindNode(int key)
    {
        int cur = _root;
        while (cur != -1)
        {
            if (key < _key[cur])
                cur = _left[cur];
            else if (key > _key[cur])
                cur = _right[cur];
            else
                return cur;
        }
        return -1;
    }

    private static int FindCeilingNode(int key)
    {
        int cur = _root;
        int best = -1;
        while (cur != -1)
        {
            if (_key[cur] >= key)
            {
                best = cur;
                cur = _left[cur];
            }
            else
            {
                cur = _right[cur];
            }
        }
        return best;
    }

    private static void AddStockToBucket(int stockId, int remaining)
    {
        if (remaining < 0)
            return;

        int node = FindNode(remaining);
        if (node == -1)
        {
            _root = InsertNode(_root, remaining, stockId);
            return;
        }

        _stockNext[stockId] = _bucketHead[node];
        _bucketHead[node] = stockId;
    }

    private static int TakeStockForCut(int length, out int remaining)
    {
        int node = FindCeilingNode(length);
        if (node == -1)
        {
            remaining = 0;
            return -1;
        }

        int stockId = _bucketHead[node];
        remaining = _key[node] - length;
        _bucketHead[node] = _stockNext[stockId];
        _stockNext[stockId] = -1;
        if (_bucketHead[node] == -1)
            _root = DeleteNode(_root, _key[node]);
        return stockId;
    }

    private static int CreateStock()
    {
        int id = _stockCount++;
        _stockHead[id] = -1;
        _stockTail[id] = -1;
        return id;
    }

    private static void AppendCutToStock(int stockId, int cutIndex, int[] nextCut)
    {
        nextCut[cutIndex] = -1;
        if (_stockHead[stockId] == -1)
        {
            _stockHead[stockId] = cutIndex;
            _stockTail[stockId] = cutIndex;
            return;
        }

        nextCut[_stockTail[stockId]] = cutIndex;
        _stockTail[stockId] = cutIndex;
    }

    public static void Main()
    {
        var fs = new FastScanner();
        int n = fs.ReadInt();
        _stockLength = fs.ReadInt();

        if (n < 0 || _stockLength < 0)
            return;

        int[] lengths = new int[n];
        int[] order = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            lengths[i] = fs.ReadInt();
            order[i] = i;
        }

        Array.Sort(lengths, order);

        _left = new int[n + 5];
        _right = new int[n + 5];
        _priority = new int[n + 5];
        _key = new int[n + 5];
        _bucketHead = new int[n + 5];
        _stockNext = new int[n + 5];
        _stockHead = new int[n + 5];
        _stockTail = new int[n + 5];

        Array.Fill(_left, -1);
        Array.Fill(_right, -1);
        Array.Fill(_bucketHead, -1);
        Array.Fill(_stockNext, -1);
        Array.Fill(_stockHead, -1);
        Array.Fill(_stockTail, -1);

        int[] nextCut = new int[n];
        Array.Fill(nextCut, -1);

        for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--)
        {
            int length = lengths[i];
            int cutIndex = order[i];

            int remaining;
            int stockId = TakeStockForCut(length, out remaining);
            if (stockId == -1)
            {
                stockId = CreateStock();
                remaining = _stockLength - length;
            }

            AppendCutToStock(stockId, cutIndex, nextCut);
            AddStockToBucket(stockId, remaining);
        }

        var output = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput(), new UTF8Encoding(false), 1 << 20);
        output.WriteLine(_stockCount);
        for (int s = 0; s < _stockCount; s++)
        {
            int cur = _stockHead[s];
            bool first = true;
            while (cur != -1)
            {
                if (!first)
                    output.Write(' ');
                output.Write(cur);
                first = false;
                cur = nextCut[cur];
            }
            output.WriteLine();
        }
        output.Flush();
    }
}

Test 10: 2D Cutting Stock - Rectangle Packing (C++) Score: 11.40 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the 2D Cutting Stock Problem (Rectangle Bin Packing) in C++.

Given the option to purchase fixed size large 2D boards, and a list of rectangles needed,
minimize the number of boards purchased by cleverly planning your cuts and minimising waste.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 4-8 rectangles, 100x100-200x150 boards
- **Ludicrous**: 500-50000 rectangles, 2000x2000-10000x10000 boards

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N board_width board_height
Next N lines: w h (rectangle dimensions)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_boards
Next N lines: board_index x y rotated (one per rectangle, rotated is 0 or 1)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on problem size and board dimensions
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for 50000 rectangles
3. **Quality**: Minimize number of boards used while producing valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Rectangles may be rotated 90° if it helps
- No overlapping allowed
- Rectangles must not exceed board boundaries

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Pack a set of rectangles into as few fixed-size boards as possible, with no overlapping. Rectangles may be rotated 90° to fit better. Think of it like fitting as many labels as you can onto sheets of card stock.

This 2D packing problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number and variety of rectangles. The baseline stacks rectangles in simple horizontal rows.

 1.0000
[4 rectangles, 100x100 board] Boards: 1, Baseline: 1, Waste: 4100, Time: 0.3s - excellent (≤ baseline). (waste < 1 board, no repack check)

2D Cutting Stock - 4 rectangles, 100x100 board

View Code (7604 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <iostream>
#include <map>
#include <set>
#include <tuple>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

struct Rect {
    int w = 0;
    int h = 0;
    int idx = 0;
    int mx = 0;
    int mn = 0;
};

struct Board {
    int usedH = 0;
    int remH = 0;
};

struct Shelf {
    int h = 0;
    int rem = 0;
    int board = -1;
    int y = 0;
};

struct Placement {
    int board = 0;
    int x = 0;
    int y = 0;
    int rot = 0;
};

struct Candidate {
    bool valid = false;
    int stage = 3;     // 0 = existing shelf, 1 = existing board new shelf, 2 = new board
    int level = 0;     // shelf height
    int extra = 0;     // leftover width or leftover board height
    int boardId = -1;
    int shelfId = -1;
    int w = 0;
    int h = 0;
    int rot = 0;
};

struct Packer {
    int W = 0;
    int H = 0;
    vector<set<pair<int, int>>> shelvesByH;
    vector<int> seg;
    vector<Board> boards;
    map<int, set<int>> boardsByRem;
    vector<Shelf> shelves;

    Packer(int boardW, int boardH) : W(boardW), H(boardH), shelvesByH(boardH + 1), seg(4 * (boardH + 5), 0) {}

    void updateSeg(int node, int l, int r, int pos) {
        if (l == r) {
            seg[node] = shelvesByH[pos].empty() ? 0 : shelvesByH[pos].rbegin()->first;
            return;
        }
        int mid = (l + r) >> 1;
        if (pos <= mid) {
            updateSeg(node << 1, l, mid, pos);
        } else {
            updateSeg(node << 1 | 1, mid + 1, r, pos);
        }
        seg[node] = max(seg[node << 1], seg[node << 1 | 1]);
    }

    int findHeight(int node, int l, int r, int ql, int needW) const {
        if (r < ql || seg[node] < needW) {
            return -1;
        }
        if (l == r) {
            return l;
        }
        int mid = (l + r) >> 1;
        int left = findHeight(node << 1, l, mid, ql, needW);
        if (left != -1) {
            return left;
        }
        return findHeight(node << 1 | 1, mid + 1, r, ql, needW);
    }

    void removeBoardFromMap(int id) {
        int rem = boards[id].remH;
        auto it = boardsByRem.find(rem);
        if (it == boardsByRem.end()) {
            return;
        }
        it->second.erase(id);
        if (it->second.empty()) {
            boardsByRem.erase(it);
        }
    }

    int createBoard() {
        int id = static_cast<int>(boards.size());
        boards.push_back(Board{0, H});
        return id;
    }

    pair<int, int> bestBoardForShelf(int h) const {
        auto it = boardsByRem.lower_bound(h);
        if (it == boardsByRem.end()) {
            return {-1, H - h};
        }
        return {*it->second.begin(), it->first - h};
    }

    Candidate makeCandidate(int a, int b, int rot) const {
        Candidate cand;
        cand.w = a;
        cand.h = b;
        cand.rot = rot;

        if (a > W || b > H) {
            return cand;
        }

        int bucket = findHeight(1, 1, H, b, a);
        if (bucket != -1) {
            const auto& bucketSet = shelvesByH[bucket];
            auto it = bucketSet.lower_bound({a, -1});
            if (it != bucketSet.end()) {
                cand.valid = true;
                cand.stage = 0;
                cand.level = bucket;
                cand.extra = it->first - a;
                cand.shelfId = it->second;
                return cand;
            }
        }

        auto [boardId, boardAfter] = bestBoardForShelf(b);
        cand.valid = true;
        cand.level = b;
        cand.extra = boardAfter;
        if (boardId >= 0) {
            cand.stage = 1;
            cand.boardId = boardId;
        } else {
            cand.stage = 2;
        }
        return cand;
    }

    static bool better(const Candidate& a, const Candidate& b) {
        if (!a.valid) {
            return false;
        }
        if (!b.valid) {
            return true;
        }
        if (a.stage != b.stage) {
            return a.stage < b.stage;
        }
        if (a.level != b.level) {
            return a.level < b.level;
        }
        if (a.extra != b.extra) {
            return a.extra < b.extra;
        }
        if (a.rot != b.rot) {
            return a.rot < b.rot;
        }
        return a.w > b.w;
    }

    void placeExistingShelf(const Candidate& cand, vector<Placement>& out, int rectIdx) {
        Shelf& sh = shelves[cand.shelfId];
        Placement p;
        p.board = sh.board;
        p.x = W - sh.rem;
        p.y = sh.y;
        p.rot = cand.rot;
        out[rectIdx] = p;

        auto& bucketSet = shelvesByH[sh.h];
        auto it = bucketSet.find({sh.rem, cand.shelfId});
        if (it != bucketSet.end()) {
            bucketSet.erase(it);
        }
        sh.rem -= cand.w;
        if (sh.rem > 0) {
            bucketSet.insert({sh.rem, cand.shelfId});
        }
        updateSeg(1, 1, H, sh.h);
    }

    void placeNewShelf(const Candidate& cand, vector<Placement>& out, int rectIdx) {
        int boardId = cand.boardId;
        if (cand.stage == 2) {
            boardId = createBoard();
        } else {
            removeBoardFromMap(boardId);
        }

        Board& board = boards[boardId];
        int y = board.usedH;
        board.usedH += cand.h;
        board.remH -= cand.h;

        if (board.remH > 0) {
            boardsByRem[board.remH].insert(boardId);
        }

        int shelfId = static_cast<int>(shelves.size());
        shelves.push_back(Shelf{cand.h, W - cand.w, boardId, y});

        if (cand.h >= 1 && cand.h <= H && shelves.back().rem > 0) {
            shelvesByH[cand.h].insert({shelves.back().rem, shelfId});
            updateSeg(1, 1, H, cand.h);
        }

        Placement p;
        p.board = boardId;
        p.x = 0;
        p.y = y;
        p.rot = cand.rot;
        out[rectIdx] = p;
    }
};

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int N = 0;
    int boardW = 0;
    int boardH = 0;
    if (!(cin >> N >> boardW >> boardH)) {
        return 0;
    }

    vector<Rect> rects(N);
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
        cin >> rects[i].w >> rects[i].h;
        rects[i].idx = i;
        rects[i].mx = max(rects[i].w, rects[i].h);
        rects[i].mn = min(rects[i].w, rects[i].h);
    }

    sort(rects.begin(), rects.end(), [](const Rect& a, const Rect& b) {
        if (a.mx != b.mx) {
            return a.mx > b.mx;
        }
        if (a.mn != b.mn) {
            return a.mn > b.mn;
        }
        return a.idx < b.idx;
    });

    Packer packer(boardW, boardH);
    vector<Placement> placements(N);

    for (const Rect& r : rects) {
        Candidate c0 = packer.makeCandidate(r.w, r.h, 0);
        Candidate c1 = packer.makeCandidate(r.h, r.w, 1);

        Candidate best;
        if (Packer::better(c0, c1)) {
            best = c0;
        } else {
            best = c1;
        }

        if (!best.valid) {
            // Fallback for malformed inputs: try the original orientation on a fresh board.
            best.valid = true;
            best.stage = 2;
            best.level = r.h;
            best.extra = boardH - r.h;
            best.boardId = -1;
            best.shelfId = -1;
            best.w = r.w;
            best.h = r.h;
            best.rot = 0;
        }

        if (best.stage == 0) {
            packer.placeExistingShelf(best, placements, r.idx);
        } else {
            packer.placeNewShelf(best, placements, r.idx);
        }
    }

    cout << packer.boards.size() << '\n';
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
        cout << placements[i].board << ' ' << placements[i].x << ' ' << placements[i].y << ' ' << placements[i].rot << '\n';
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 11: 2D Polygon Cutting Stock (Rust) Score: 0.50 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 2D Polygon Cutting Stock Problem in Rust.

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with problems ranging from 2 simple pieces to 5000+ complex polygons.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: SV NP (number of stock polygon vertices, number of pieces)
Next SV lines: x y (stock polygon vertices)
For each piece:
Line: PV (number of vertices in this piece)
Next PV lines: x y (piece polygon vertices)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_stocks
Next NP lines: stock_index x y rotation (one per piece)
stock_index: 0-indexed stock polygon
x y: translation to apply
rotation: angle in radians

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number and complexity of pieces
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 5000+ complex pieces
3. **Quality**: Minimize stock usage while ensuring valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Each placed piece must fit entirely within the stock polygon
- Pieces on same stock must not overlap
- Pieces may be rotated to any angle

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Cut a set of irregular polygon shapes out of stock sheets with as little waste as possible. Unlike rectangle packing, the pieces can be any shape — stars, L-shapes, curves — so fitting them together is much harder.

The AI must handle polygon geometry (containment, intersection, rotation) and plan efficient layouts. Subpasses increase the number and complexity of the pieces. The baseline uses a simple greedy placement strategy.

 0.5000
[4 rectangles from 100x100 square] Stocks: 4, Baseline: 1, Time: 0.0s - valid but inefficient (4.00x baseline).

Polygon Cutting - 4 rectangles from 100x100 square

View Code (15056 chars)
use std::cmp::Ordering;
use std::collections::BinaryHeap;
use std::f64::consts::{PI, SQRT_2};
use std::io::Read;

const EPS: f64 = 1e-9;

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default)]
struct Point {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
}

impl Point {
    #[inline]
    fn new(x: f64, y: f64) -> Self {
        Self { x, y }
    }
}

use std::ops::{Add, Div, Mul, Sub};

impl Add for Point {
    type Output = Self;
    fn add(self, rhs: Self) -> Self::Output {
        Self::new(self.x + rhs.x, self.y + rhs.y)
    }
}

impl Sub for Point {
    type Output = Self;
    fn sub(self, rhs: Self) -> Self::Output {
        Self::new(self.x - rhs.x, self.y - rhs.y)
    }
}

impl Mul<f64> for Point {
    type Output = Self;
    fn mul(self, rhs: f64) -> Self::Output {
        Self::new(self.x * rhs, self.y * rhs)
    }
}

impl Div<f64> for Point {
    type Output = Self;
    fn div(self, rhs: f64) -> Self::Output {
        Self::new(self.x / rhs, self.y / rhs)
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct Circle {
    c: Point,
    r: f64,
}

impl Circle {
    #[inline]
    fn contains(&self, p: Point) -> bool {
        dist2(self.c, p) <= (self.r + 1e-9) * (self.r + 1e-9)
    }
}

#[inline]
fn dist2(a: Point, b: Point) -> f64 {
    let dx = a.x - b.x;
    let dy = a.y - b.y;
    dx * dx + dy * dy
}

#[inline]
fn dist(a: Point, b: Point) -> f64 {
    dist2(a, b).sqrt()
}

#[inline]
fn cross(a: Point, b: Point, c: Point) -> f64 {
    (b.x - a.x) * (c.y - a.y) - (b.y - a.y) * (c.x - a.x)
}

#[inline]
fn dot(a: Point, b: Point, c: Point) -> f64 {
    (b.x - a.x) * (c.x - a.x) + (b.y - a.y) * (c.y - a.y)
}

#[inline]
fn point_on_segment(p: Point, a: Point, b: Point) -> bool {
    cross(a, b, p).abs() <= 1e-10 && dot(p, a, b) <= 1e-10
}

#[inline]
fn point_segment_distance(p: Point, a: Point, b: Point) -> f64 {
    let ab = b - a;
    let ap = p - a;
    let len2 = ab.x * ab.x + ab.y * ab.y;
    if len2 <= EPS {
        return dist(p, a);
    }
    let t = (ap.x * ab.x + ap.y * ab.y) / len2;
    let t = t.clamp(0.0, 1.0);
    let proj = Point::new(a.x + ab.x * t, a.y + ab.y * t);
    dist(p, proj)
}

#[inline]
fn polygon_bbox(poly: &[Point]) -> (f64, f64, f64, f64) {
    let mut minx = poly[0].x;
    let mut maxx = poly[0].x;
    let mut miny = poly[0].y;
    let mut maxy = poly[0].y;
    for &p in poly.iter().skip(1) {
        minx = minx.min(p.x);
        maxx = maxx.max(p.x);
        miny = miny.min(p.y);
        maxy = maxy.max(p.y);
    }
    (minx, maxx, miny, maxy)
}

fn polygon_area2(poly: &[Point]) -> f64 {
    let n = poly.len();
    let mut s = 0.0;
    for i in 0..n {
        let a = poly[i];
        let b = poly[(i + 1) % n];
        s += a.x * b.y - a.y * b.x;
    }
    s
}

fn polygon_centroid(poly: &[Point]) -> Point {
    let n = poly.len();
    let area2 = polygon_area2(poly);
    if area2.abs() <= EPS {
        let mut sx = 0.0;
        let mut sy = 0.0;
        for &p in poly {
            sx += p.x;
            sy += p.y;
        }
        return Point::new(sx / n as f64, sy / n as f64);
    }
    let mut cx = 0.0;
    let mut cy = 0.0;
    for i in 0..n {
        let a = poly[i];
        let b = poly[(i + 1) % n];
        let k = a.x * b.y - b.x * a.y;
        cx += (a.x + b.x) * k;
        cy += (a.y + b.y) * k;
    }
    let inv = 1.0 / (3.0 * area2);
    Point::new(cx * inv, cy * inv)
}

fn point_in_polygon(p: Point, poly: &[Point]) -> bool {
    let n = poly.len();
    let mut inside = false;
    for i in 0..n {
        let a = poly[i];
        let b = poly[(i + 1) % n];
        if point_on_segment(p, a, b) {
            return true;
        }
        let cond = (a.y > p.y) != (b.y > p.y);
        if cond {
            let x = a.x + (b.x - a.x) * (p.y - a.y) / (b.y - a.y);
            if x > p.x + EPS {
                inside = !inside;
            }
        }
    }
    inside
}

fn signed_distance_to_polygon(p: Point, poly: &[Point]) -> f64 {
    let n = poly.len();
    let mut d = f64::INFINITY;
    for i in 0..n {
        d = d.min(point_segment_distance(p, poly[i], poly[(i + 1) % n]));
    }
    if point_in_polygon(p, poly) {
        d
    } else {
        -d
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct Cell {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    h: f64,
    d: f64,
    max: f64,
}

impl Cell {
    fn new(x: f64, y: f64, h: f64, poly: &[Point]) -> Self {
        let d = signed_distance_to_polygon(Point::new(x, y), poly);
        let max = d + h * SQRT_2;
        Self { x, y, h, d, max }
    }
}

impl PartialEq for Cell {
    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
        self.max.to_bits() == other.max.to_bits()
    }
}
impl Eq for Cell {}

impl PartialOrd for Cell {
    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<Ordering> {
        self.max.partial_cmp(&other.max)
    }
}

impl Ord for Cell {
    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Ordering {
        self.partial_cmp(other).unwrap_or(Ordering::Equal)
    }
}

fn polylabel(poly: &[Point], precision: f64) -> (Point, f64) {
    let (minx, maxx, miny, maxy) = polygon_bbox(poly);
    let width = maxx - minx;
    let height = maxy - miny;
    if width.abs() <= EPS && height.abs() <= EPS {
        return (poly[0], 0.0);
    }

    let cell_size = width.min(height);
    let mut heap = BinaryHeap::new();

    let mut x = minx;
    while x <= maxx {
        let mut y = miny;
        while y <= maxy {
            heap.push(Cell::new(x + cell_size * 0.5, y + cell_size * 0.5, cell_size * 0.5, poly));
            y += cell_size;
        }
        x += cell_size;
    }

    let centroid = polygon_centroid(poly);
    let mut best = Cell::new(centroid.x, centroid.y, 0.0, poly);
    if best.d < 0.0 {
        best = Cell::new((minx + maxx) * 0.5, (miny + maxy) * 0.5, 0.0, poly);
    }

    while let Some(cell) = heap.pop() {
        if cell.d > best.d {
            best = cell;
        }
        if cell.max - best.d <= precision {
            continue;
        }
        let h = cell.h * 0.5;
        heap.push(Cell::new(cell.x - h, cell.y - h, h, poly));
        heap.push(Cell::new(cell.x + h, cell.y - h, h, poly));
        heap.push(Cell::new(cell.x - h, cell.y + h, h, poly));
        heap.push(Cell::new(cell.x + h, cell.y + h, h, poly));
    }

    (Point::new(best.x, best.y), best.d.max(0.0))
}

fn shuffle(points: &mut [Point]) {
    let mut seed = 0x9E37_79B9_7F4A_7C15u64 ^ (points.len() as u64);
    for i in (1..points.len()).rev() {
        seed ^= seed << 7;
        seed ^= seed >> 9;
        seed ^= seed << 8;
        let j = (seed % ((i + 1) as u64)) as usize;
        points.swap(i, j);
    }
}

fn circle_from2(a: Point, b: Point) -> Circle {
    let c = Point::new((a.x + b.x) * 0.5, (a.y + b.y) * 0.5);
    Circle { c, r: dist(a, b) * 0.5 }
}

fn circle_from3(a: Point, b: Point, c: Point) -> Circle {
    let d = 2.0 * (a.x * (b.y - c.y) + b.x * (c.y - a.y) + c.x * (a.y - b.y));
    if d.abs() <= 1e-12 {
        let ab = dist2(a, b);
        let ac = dist2(a, c);
        let bc = dist2(b, c);
        if ab >= ac && ab >= bc {
            return circle_from2(a, b);
        }
        if ac >= ab && ac >= bc {
            return circle_from2(a, c);
        }
        return circle_from2(b, c);
    }

    let a2 = a.x * a.x + a.y * a.y;
    let b2 = b.x * b.x + b.y * b.y;
    let c2 = c.x * c.x + c.y * c.y;
    let ux = (a2 * (b.y - c.y) + b2 * (c.y - a.y) + c2 * (a.y - b.y)) / d;
    let uy = (a2 * (c.x - b.x) + b2 * (a.x - c.x) + c2 * (b.x - a.x)) / d;
    let center = Point::new(ux, uy);
    Circle { c: center, r: dist(center, a) }
}

fn min_enclosing_circle(points: &[Point]) -> Circle {
    if points.is_empty() {
        return Circle { c: Point::new(0.0, 0.0), r: 0.0 };
    }
    if points.len() == 1 {
        return Circle { c: points[0], r: 0.0 };
    }
    let mut pts = points.to_vec();
    shuffle(&mut pts);

    let mut c = Circle { c: pts[0], r: 0.0 };
    for i in 1..pts.len() {
        if !c.contains(pts[i]) {
            c = Circle { c: pts[i], r: 0.0 };
            for j in 0..i {
                if !c.contains(pts[j]) {
                    c = circle_from2(pts[i], pts[j]);
                    for k in 0..j {
                        if !c.contains(pts[k]) {
                            c = circle_from3(pts[i], pts[j], pts[k]);
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
    c
}

fn rotate_about(p: Point, anchor: Point, angle: f64) -> Point {
    let s = angle.sin();
    let c = angle.cos();
    let dx = p.x - anchor.x;
    let dy = p.y - anchor.y;
    Point::new(anchor.x + dx * c - dy * s, anchor.y + dx * s + dy * c)
}

#[inline]
fn orient(a: Point, b: Point, c: Point) -> f64 {
    cross(a, b, c)
}

#[inline]
fn proper_segment_intersection(a: Point, b: Point, c: Point, d: Point) -> bool {
    let o1 = orient(a, b, c);
    let o2 = orient(a, b, d);
    let o3 = orient(c, d, a);
    let o4 = orient(c, d, b);

    let s1 = o1 > EPS;
    let s2 = o2 > EPS;
    let s3 = o3 > EPS;
    let s4 = o4 > EPS;
    (s1 != s2) && (s3 != s4)
}

fn fits_in_stock(
    piece: &[Point],
    stock: &[Point],
    stock_edges: &[(Point, Point)],
    translation: Point,
    angle: f64,
    scratch: &mut Vec<Point>,
) -> bool {
    scratch.clear();
    let s = angle.sin();
    let c = angle.cos();
    for &p in piece {
        let q = Point::new(p.x * c - p.y * s + translation.x, p.x * s + p.y * c + translation.y);
        if !point_in_polygon(q, stock) {
            return false;
        }
        scratch.push(q);
    }

    let n = scratch.len();
    for i in 0..n {
        let a = scratch[i];
        let b = scratch[(i + 1) % n];
        for &(c1, c2) in stock_edges {
            if proper_segment_intersection(a, b, c1, c2) {
                return false;
            }
        }
    }
    true
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
struct Candidate {
    p: Point,
    clearance: f64,
}

fn stock_candidates(stock: &[Point]) -> Vec<Candidate> {
    let (minx, maxx, miny, maxy) = polygon_bbox(stock);
    let (best_p, best_d) = polylabel(stock, 1e-6);
    let mut candidates = Vec::new();
    candidates.push(Candidate {
        p: best_p,
        clearance: best_d,
    });

    let steps = 64usize;
    let dx = if (maxx - minx).abs() <= EPS {
        0.0
    } else {
        (maxx - minx) / steps as f64
    };
    let dy = if (maxy - miny).abs() <= EPS {
        0.0
    } else {
        (maxy - miny) / steps as f64
    };
    for ix in 0..=steps {
        let x = minx + dx * ix as f64;
        for iy in 0..=steps {
            let y = miny + dy * iy as f64;
            let p = Point::new(x, y);
            let d = signed_distance_to_polygon(p, stock);
            if d > 0.0 {
                candidates.push(Candidate { p, clearance: d });
            }
        }
    }

    candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {
        b.clearance
            .partial_cmp(&a.clearance)
            .unwrap_or(Ordering::Equal)
            .then_with(|| a.p.x.partial_cmp(&b.p.x).unwrap_or(Ordering::Equal))
            .then_with(|| a.p.y.partial_cmp(&b.p.y).unwrap_or(Ordering::Equal))
    });
    let mut dedup = Vec::new();
    for c in candidates {
        if dedup.iter().all(|d: &Candidate| {
            (d.p.x - c.p.x).abs() > 1e-9 || (d.p.y - c.p.y).abs() > 1e-9
        }) {
            dedup.push(c);
        }
        if dedup.len() >= 256 {
            break;
        }
    }
    dedup
}

fn parse_input() -> (Vec<Point>, Vec<Vec<Point>>) {
    let mut input = String::new();
    std::io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
    let mut it = input.split_whitespace();
    let sv: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let np: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    let mut stock = Vec::with_capacity(sv);
    for _ in 0..sv {
        let x: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let y: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        stock.push(Point::new(x, y));
    }

    let mut pieces = Vec::with_capacity(np);
    for _ in 0..np {
        let pv: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let mut piece = Vec::with_capacity(pv);
        for _ in 0..pv {
            let x: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
            let y: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
            piece.push(Point::new(x, y));
        }
        pieces.push(piece);
    }
    (stock, pieces)
}

fn main() {
    let (stock, pieces) = parse_input();
    let num_stocks = pieces.len();
    let candidates = stock_candidates(&stock);
    let stock_edges: Vec<(Point, Point)> = (0..stock.len())
        .map(|i| (stock[i], stock[(i + 1) % stock.len()]))
        .collect();

    let fallback_angles = [
        0.0,
        PI / 6.0,
        PI / 3.0,
        PI / 2.0,
        2.0 * PI / 3.0,
        5.0 * PI / 6.0,
        PI,
        7.0 * PI / 6.0,
        4.0 * PI / 3.0,
        3.0 * PI / 2.0,
        5.0 * PI / 3.0,
        11.0 * PI / 6.0,
    ];

    let mut outputs: Vec<(usize, f64, f64, f64)> = Vec::with_capacity(num_stocks);
    let mut scratch = Vec::new();

    for (i, piece) in pieces.iter().enumerate() {
        let mec = min_enclosing_circle(piece);
        let piece_anchor = polygon_centroid(piece);

        let mut placed = false;

        for cand in &candidates {
            if cand.clearance + 1e-8 >= mec.r {
                let angle = 0.0;
                let rot_anchor = mec.c;
                let rot_c = rotate_about(rot_anchor, Point::new(0.0, 0.0), angle);
                let translation = Point::new(cand.p.x - rot_c.x, cand.p.y - rot_c.y);
                outputs.push((i, translation.x, translation.y, angle));
                placed = true;
                break;
            }
        }
        if placed {
            continue;
        }

        'search: for cand in &candidates {
            for &angle in &fallback_angles {
                let translation = Point::new(
                    cand.p.x - (piece_anchor.x * angle.cos() - piece_anchor.y * angle.sin()),
                    cand.p.y - (piece_anchor.x * angle.sin() + piece_anchor.y * angle.cos()),
                );
                if fits_in_stock(
                    piece,
                    &stock,
                    &stock_edges,
                    translation,
                    angle,
                    &mut scratch,
                ) {
                    outputs.push((i, translation.x, translation.y, angle));
                    placed = true;
                    break 'search;
                }
            }
        }

        if !placed {
            let cand = candidates.first().copied().unwrap_or(Candidate {
                p: Point::new(0.0, 0.0),
                clearance: 0.0,
            });
            let angle = 0.0;
            let translation = Point::new(cand.p.x - piece_anchor.x, cand.p.y - piece_anchor.y);
            outputs.push((i, translation.x, translation.y, angle));
        }
    }

    let mut out = String::new();
    out.push_str(&format!("{}\n", num_stocks));
    for (idx, x, y, rot) in outputs {
        out.push_str(&format!("{} {:.12} {:.12} {:.12}\n", idx, x, y, rot));
    }
    print!("{}", out);
}
Layout

4 rectangles from 100x100 square

Stock 1/4
Stock 2/4
Stock 3/4
Stock 4/4
