[5 triangles from 100x80 rectangle] Stocks: 5, Baseline: 1, Time: 5.7s - valid but inefficient (5.00x baseline). REPACK PENALTY: top-5 waste stocks repacked 5→1 (saved 4) → 0

Polygon Cutting - 5 triangles from 100x80 rectangle

View Code (15056 chars)
use std::cmp::Ordering;
use std::collections::BinaryHeap;
use std::f64::consts::{PI, SQRT_2};
use std::io::Read;

const EPS: f64 = 1e-9;

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default)]
struct Point {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
}

impl Point {
    #[inline]
    fn new(x: f64, y: f64) -> Self {
        Self { x, y }
    }
}

use std::ops::{Add, Div, Mul, Sub};

impl Add for Point {
    type Output = Self;
    fn add(self, rhs: Self) -> Self::Output {
        Self::new(self.x + rhs.x, self.y + rhs.y)
    }
}

impl Sub for Point {
    type Output = Self;
    fn sub(self, rhs: Self) -> Self::Output {
        Self::new(self.x - rhs.x, self.y - rhs.y)
    }
}

impl Mul<f64> for Point {
    type Output = Self;
    fn mul(self, rhs: f64) -> Self::Output {
        Self::new(self.x * rhs, self.y * rhs)
    }
}

impl Div<f64> for Point {
    type Output = Self;
    fn div(self, rhs: f64) -> Self::Output {
        Self::new(self.x / rhs, self.y / rhs)
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct Circle {
    c: Point,
    r: f64,
}

impl Circle {
    #[inline]
    fn contains(&self, p: Point) -> bool {
        dist2(self.c, p) <= (self.r + 1e-9) * (self.r + 1e-9)
    }
}

#[inline]
fn dist2(a: Point, b: Point) -> f64 {
    let dx = a.x - b.x;
    let dy = a.y - b.y;
    dx * dx + dy * dy
}

#[inline]
fn dist(a: Point, b: Point) -> f64 {
    dist2(a, b).sqrt()
}

#[inline]
fn cross(a: Point, b: Point, c: Point) -> f64 {
    (b.x - a.x) * (c.y - a.y) - (b.y - a.y) * (c.x - a.x)
}

#[inline]
fn dot(a: Point, b: Point, c: Point) -> f64 {
    (b.x - a.x) * (c.x - a.x) + (b.y - a.y) * (c.y - a.y)
}

#[inline]
fn point_on_segment(p: Point, a: Point, b: Point) -> bool {
    cross(a, b, p).abs() <= 1e-10 && dot(p, a, b) <= 1e-10
}

#[inline]
fn point_segment_distance(p: Point, a: Point, b: Point) -> f64 {
    let ab = b - a;
    let ap = p - a;
    let len2 = ab.x * ab.x + ab.y * ab.y;
    if len2 <= EPS {
        return dist(p, a);
    }
    let t = (ap.x * ab.x + ap.y * ab.y) / len2;
    let t = t.clamp(0.0, 1.0);
    let proj = Point::new(a.x + ab.x * t, a.y + ab.y * t);
    dist(p, proj)
}

#[inline]
fn polygon_bbox(poly: &[Point]) -> (f64, f64, f64, f64) {
    let mut minx = poly[0].x;
    let mut maxx = poly[0].x;
    let mut miny = poly[0].y;
    let mut maxy = poly[0].y;
    for &p in poly.iter().skip(1) {
        minx = minx.min(p.x);
        maxx = maxx.max(p.x);
        miny = miny.min(p.y);
        maxy = maxy.max(p.y);
    }
    (minx, maxx, miny, maxy)
}

fn polygon_area2(poly: &[Point]) -> f64 {
    let n = poly.len();
    let mut s = 0.0;
    for i in 0..n {
        let a = poly[i];
        let b = poly[(i + 1) % n];
        s += a.x * b.y - a.y * b.x;
    }
    s
}

fn polygon_centroid(poly: &[Point]) -> Point {
    let n = poly.len();
    let area2 = polygon_area2(poly);
    if area2.abs() <= EPS {
        let mut sx = 0.0;
        let mut sy = 0.0;
        for &p in poly {
            sx += p.x;
            sy += p.y;
        }
        return Point::new(sx / n as f64, sy / n as f64);
    }
    let mut cx = 0.0;
    let mut cy = 0.0;
    for i in 0..n {
        let a = poly[i];
        let b = poly[(i + 1) % n];
        let k = a.x * b.y - b.x * a.y;
        cx += (a.x + b.x) * k;
        cy += (a.y + b.y) * k;
    }
    let inv = 1.0 / (3.0 * area2);
    Point::new(cx * inv, cy * inv)
}

fn point_in_polygon(p: Point, poly: &[Point]) -> bool {
    let n = poly.len();
    let mut inside = false;
    for i in 0..n {
        let a = poly[i];
        let b = poly[(i + 1) % n];
        if point_on_segment(p, a, b) {
            return true;
        }
        let cond = (a.y > p.y) != (b.y > p.y);
        if cond {
            let x = a.x + (b.x - a.x) * (p.y - a.y) / (b.y - a.y);
            if x > p.x + EPS {
                inside = !inside;
            }
        }
    }
    inside
}

fn signed_distance_to_polygon(p: Point, poly: &[Point]) -> f64 {
    let n = poly.len();
    let mut d = f64::INFINITY;
    for i in 0..n {
        d = d.min(point_segment_distance(p, poly[i], poly[(i + 1) % n]));
    }
    if point_in_polygon(p, poly) {
        d
    } else {
        -d
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct Cell {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    h: f64,
    d: f64,
    max: f64,
}

impl Cell {
    fn new(x: f64, y: f64, h: f64, poly: &[Point]) -> Self {
        let d = signed_distance_to_polygon(Point::new(x, y), poly);
        let max = d + h * SQRT_2;
        Self { x, y, h, d, max }
    }
}

impl PartialEq for Cell {
    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {
        self.max.to_bits() == other.max.to_bits()
    }
}
impl Eq for Cell {}

impl PartialOrd for Cell {
    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<Ordering> {
        self.max.partial_cmp(&other.max)
    }
}

impl Ord for Cell {
    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Ordering {
        self.partial_cmp(other).unwrap_or(Ordering::Equal)
    }
}

fn polylabel(poly: &[Point], precision: f64) -> (Point, f64) {
    let (minx, maxx, miny, maxy) = polygon_bbox(poly);
    let width = maxx - minx;
    let height = maxy - miny;
    if width.abs() <= EPS && height.abs() <= EPS {
        return (poly[0], 0.0);
    }

    let cell_size = width.min(height);
    let mut heap = BinaryHeap::new();

    let mut x = minx;
    while x <= maxx {
        let mut y = miny;
        while y <= maxy {
            heap.push(Cell::new(x + cell_size * 0.5, y + cell_size * 0.5, cell_size * 0.5, poly));
            y += cell_size;
        }
        x += cell_size;
    }

    let centroid = polygon_centroid(poly);
    let mut best = Cell::new(centroid.x, centroid.y, 0.0, poly);
    if best.d < 0.0 {
        best = Cell::new((minx + maxx) * 0.5, (miny + maxy) * 0.5, 0.0, poly);
    }

    while let Some(cell) = heap.pop() {
        if cell.d > best.d {
            best = cell;
        }
        if cell.max - best.d <= precision {
            continue;
        }
        let h = cell.h * 0.5;
        heap.push(Cell::new(cell.x - h, cell.y - h, h, poly));
        heap.push(Cell::new(cell.x + h, cell.y - h, h, poly));
        heap.push(Cell::new(cell.x - h, cell.y + h, h, poly));
        heap.push(Cell::new(cell.x + h, cell.y + h, h, poly));
    }

    (Point::new(best.x, best.y), best.d.max(0.0))
}

fn shuffle(points: &mut [Point]) {
    let mut seed = 0x9E37_79B9_7F4A_7C15u64 ^ (points.len() as u64);
    for i in (1..points.len()).rev() {
        seed ^= seed << 7;
        seed ^= seed >> 9;
        seed ^= seed << 8;
        let j = (seed % ((i + 1) as u64)) as usize;
        points.swap(i, j);
    }
}

fn circle_from2(a: Point, b: Point) -> Circle {
    let c = Point::new((a.x + b.x) * 0.5, (a.y + b.y) * 0.5);
    Circle { c, r: dist(a, b) * 0.5 }
}

fn circle_from3(a: Point, b: Point, c: Point) -> Circle {
    let d = 2.0 * (a.x * (b.y - c.y) + b.x * (c.y - a.y) + c.x * (a.y - b.y));
    if d.abs() <= 1e-12 {
        let ab = dist2(a, b);
        let ac = dist2(a, c);
        let bc = dist2(b, c);
        if ab >= ac && ab >= bc {
            return circle_from2(a, b);
        }
        if ac >= ab && ac >= bc {
            return circle_from2(a, c);
        }
        return circle_from2(b, c);
    }

    let a2 = a.x * a.x + a.y * a.y;
    let b2 = b.x * b.x + b.y * b.y;
    let c2 = c.x * c.x + c.y * c.y;
    let ux = (a2 * (b.y - c.y) + b2 * (c.y - a.y) + c2 * (a.y - b.y)) / d;
    let uy = (a2 * (c.x - b.x) + b2 * (a.x - c.x) + c2 * (b.x - a.x)) / d;
    let center = Point::new(ux, uy);
    Circle { c: center, r: dist(center, a) }
}

fn min_enclosing_circle(points: &[Point]) -> Circle {
    if points.is_empty() {
        return Circle { c: Point::new(0.0, 0.0), r: 0.0 };
    }
    if points.len() == 1 {
        return Circle { c: points[0], r: 0.0 };
    }
    let mut pts = points.to_vec();
    shuffle(&mut pts);

    let mut c = Circle { c: pts[0], r: 0.0 };
    for i in 1..pts.len() {
        if !c.contains(pts[i]) {
            c = Circle { c: pts[i], r: 0.0 };
            for j in 0..i {
                if !c.contains(pts[j]) {
                    c = circle_from2(pts[i], pts[j]);
                    for k in 0..j {
                        if !c.contains(pts[k]) {
                            c = circle_from3(pts[i], pts[j], pts[k]);
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
    c
}

fn rotate_about(p: Point, anchor: Point, angle: f64) -> Point {
    let s = angle.sin();
    let c = angle.cos();
    let dx = p.x - anchor.x;
    let dy = p.y - anchor.y;
    Point::new(anchor.x + dx * c - dy * s, anchor.y + dx * s + dy * c)
}

#[inline]
fn orient(a: Point, b: Point, c: Point) -> f64 {
    cross(a, b, c)
}

#[inline]
fn proper_segment_intersection(a: Point, b: Point, c: Point, d: Point) -> bool {
    let o1 = orient(a, b, c);
    let o2 = orient(a, b, d);
    let o3 = orient(c, d, a);
    let o4 = orient(c, d, b);

    let s1 = o1 > EPS;
    let s2 = o2 > EPS;
    let s3 = o3 > EPS;
    let s4 = o4 > EPS;
    (s1 != s2) && (s3 != s4)
}

fn fits_in_stock(
    piece: &[Point],
    stock: &[Point],
    stock_edges: &[(Point, Point)],
    translation: Point,
    angle: f64,
    scratch: &mut Vec<Point>,
) -> bool {
    scratch.clear();
    let s = angle.sin();
    let c = angle.cos();
    for &p in piece {
        let q = Point::new(p.x * c - p.y * s + translation.x, p.x * s + p.y * c + translation.y);
        if !point_in_polygon(q, stock) {
            return false;
        }
        scratch.push(q);
    }

    let n = scratch.len();
    for i in 0..n {
        let a = scratch[i];
        let b = scratch[(i + 1) % n];
        for &(c1, c2) in stock_edges {
            if proper_segment_intersection(a, b, c1, c2) {
                return false;
            }
        }
    }
    true
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
struct Candidate {
    p: Point,
    clearance: f64,
}

fn stock_candidates(stock: &[Point]) -> Vec<Candidate> {
    let (minx, maxx, miny, maxy) = polygon_bbox(stock);
    let (best_p, best_d) = polylabel(stock, 1e-6);
    let mut candidates = Vec::new();
    candidates.push(Candidate {
        p: best_p,
        clearance: best_d,
    });

    let steps = 64usize;
    let dx = if (maxx - minx).abs() <= EPS {
        0.0
    } else {
        (maxx - minx) / steps as f64
    };
    let dy = if (maxy - miny).abs() <= EPS {
        0.0
    } else {
        (maxy - miny) / steps as f64
    };
    for ix in 0..=steps {
        let x = minx + dx * ix as f64;
        for iy in 0..=steps {
            let y = miny + dy * iy as f64;
            let p = Point::new(x, y);
            let d = signed_distance_to_polygon(p, stock);
            if d > 0.0 {
                candidates.push(Candidate { p, clearance: d });
            }
        }
    }

    candidates.sort_by(|a, b| {
        b.clearance
            .partial_cmp(&a.clearance)
            .unwrap_or(Ordering::Equal)
            .then_with(|| a.p.x.partial_cmp(&b.p.x).unwrap_or(Ordering::Equal))
            .then_with(|| a.p.y.partial_cmp(&b.p.y).unwrap_or(Ordering::Equal))
    });
    let mut dedup = Vec::new();
    for c in candidates {
        if dedup.iter().all(|d: &Candidate| {
            (d.p.x - c.p.x).abs() > 1e-9 || (d.p.y - c.p.y).abs() > 1e-9
        }) {
            dedup.push(c);
        }
        if dedup.len() >= 256 {
            break;
        }
    }
    dedup
}

fn parse_input() -> (Vec<Point>, Vec<Vec<Point>>) {
    let mut input = String::new();
    std::io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
    let mut it = input.split_whitespace();
    let sv: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let np: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    let mut stock = Vec::with_capacity(sv);
    for _ in 0..sv {
        let x: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let y: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        stock.push(Point::new(x, y));
    }

    let mut pieces = Vec::with_capacity(np);
    for _ in 0..np {
        let pv: usize = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let mut piece = Vec::with_capacity(pv);
        for _ in 0..pv {
            let x: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
            let y: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
            piece.push(Point::new(x, y));
        }
        pieces.push(piece);
    }
    (stock, pieces)
}

fn main() {
    let (stock, pieces) = parse_input();
    let num_stocks = pieces.len();
    let candidates = stock_candidates(&stock);
    let stock_edges: Vec<(Point, Point)> = (0..stock.len())
        .map(|i| (stock[i], stock[(i + 1) % stock.len()]))
        .collect();

    let fallback_angles = [
        0.0,
        PI / 6.0,
        PI / 3.0,
        PI / 2.0,
        2.0 * PI / 3.0,
        5.0 * PI / 6.0,
        PI,
        7.0 * PI / 6.0,
        4.0 * PI / 3.0,
        3.0 * PI / 2.0,
        5.0 * PI / 3.0,
        11.0 * PI / 6.0,
    ];

    let mut outputs: Vec<(usize, f64, f64, f64)> = Vec::with_capacity(num_stocks);
    let mut scratch = Vec::new();

    for (i, piece) in pieces.iter().enumerate() {
        let mec = min_enclosing_circle(piece);
        let piece_anchor = polygon_centroid(piece);

        let mut placed = false;

        for cand in &candidates {
            if cand.clearance + 1e-8 >= mec.r {
                let angle = 0.0;
                let rot_anchor = mec.c;
                let rot_c = rotate_about(rot_anchor, Point::new(0.0, 0.0), angle);
                let translation = Point::new(cand.p.x - rot_c.x, cand.p.y - rot_c.y);
                outputs.push((i, translation.x, translation.y, angle));
                placed = true;
                break;
            }
        }
        if placed {
            continue;
        }

        'search: for cand in &candidates {
            for &angle in &fallback_angles {
                let translation = Point::new(
                    cand.p.x - (piece_anchor.x * angle.cos() - piece_anchor.y * angle.sin()),
                    cand.p.y - (piece_anchor.x * angle.sin() + piece_anchor.y * angle.cos()),
                );
                if fits_in_stock(
                    piece,
                    &stock,
                    &stock_edges,
                    translation,
                    angle,
                    &mut scratch,
                ) {
                    outputs.push((i, translation.x, translation.y, angle));
                    placed = true;
                    break 'search;
                }
            }
        }

        if !placed {
            let cand = candidates.first().copied().unwrap_or(Candidate {
                p: Point::new(0.0, 0.0),
                clearance: 0.0,
            });
            let angle = 0.0;
            let translation = Point::new(cand.p.x - piece_anchor.x, cand.p.y - piece_anchor.y);
            outputs.push((i, translation.x, translation.y, angle));
        }
    }

    let mut out = String::new();
    out.push_str(&format!("{}\n", num_stocks));
    for (idx, x, y, rot) in outputs {
        out.push_str(&format!("{} {:.12} {:.12} {:.12}\n", idx, x, y, rot));
    }
    print!("{}", out);
}

Test 12: 3D Bin Packing - Polyhedra in Box (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Bin Packing problem in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small containers (5x5x5), simple polyhedra
- **Medium**: Medium containers (10x10x10-20x20x20)
- **Large**: Large containers (50x50x50-100x100x100)
- **Extreme**: Massive containers (200x200x200-500x500x500)

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: V F (vertex/face count of the polyhedron)
Next V lines: x y z (polyhedron vertices)
Next F lines: a b c (polyhedron face indices)
Last line: W H D (container width, height, depth)
Container origin at (0,0,0), extends to (W,H,D).

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: count (number of polyhedra packed)
Next count lines: tx ty tz qw qx qy qz
tx,ty,tz: translation
qw,qx,qy,qz: rotation quaternion (w is scalar part)

**Quaternion Rotation Reference:**
- Identity (no rotation): 1 0 0 0
- 90° around Z: 0.707 0 0 0.707
- To rotate a point: p' = q * p * q^(-1)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container size and polyhedron complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize packing count while ensuring valid placements

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

View chain of thought

Pack as many copies of an irregular 3D shape into a box as possible, without any overlaps or pieces poking outside. The shapes can be rotated freely, so finding the best orientations is part of the puzzle.

The AI must handle 3D collision detection and spatial reasoning. Subpasses use increasingly awkward shapes that resist neat stacking. The baseline uses a simple grid placement without rotation optimisation.

3D Bin Packing - 2x2x2 boxes in 10x10x10 container

using System;
using System.Globalization;
using System.IO;
using System.Text;

public static class Program
{
    private const double Eps = 1e-9;

    private struct Vec3
    {
        public double X;
        public double Y;
        public double Z;

        public Vec3(double x, double y, double z)
        {
            X = x;
            Y = y;
            Z = z;
        }
    }

    private struct Rotation
    {
        public int M00, M01, M02;
        public int M10, M11, M12;
        public int M20, M21, M22;
        public double Qw, Qx, Qy, Qz;

        public Rotation(int m00, int m01, int m02,
                        int m10, int m11, int m12,
                        int m20, int m21, int m22)
        {
            M00 = m00; M01 = m01; M02 = m02;
            M10 = m10; M11 = m11; M12 = m12;
            M20 = m20; M21 = m21; M22 = m22;
            var q = MatrixToQuaternion(m00, m01, m02, m10, m11, m12, m20, m21, m22);
            Qw = q.qw;
            Qx = q.qx;
            Qy = q.qy;
            Qz = q.qz;
        }
    }

    private struct Candidate
    {
        public Rotation Rot;
        public double Dx, Dy, Dz;
        public double OffsetX, OffsetY, OffsetZ;
        public long Nx, Ny, Nz;
        public long Count;
        public double Score;
    }

    public static void Main()
    {
        var fs = new FastScanner(Console.OpenStandardInput());
        int vCount = fs.NextInt();
        int fCount = fs.NextInt();

        var vertices = new Vec3[vCount];
        for (int i = 0; i < vCount; i++)
        {
            vertices[i] = new Vec3(fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble(), fs.NextDouble());
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < fCount; i++)
        {
            fs.NextInt();
            fs.NextInt();
            fs.NextInt();
        }

        double width = fs.NextDouble();
        double height = fs.NextDouble();
        double depth = fs.NextDouble();

        if (vCount <= 0)
        {
            Console.WriteLine(0);
            return;
        }

        var rotations = BuildRotations();
        Candidate best = default;
        best.Count = -1;
        best.Score = double.NegativeInfinity;

        foreach (var rot in rotations)
        {
            EvaluateCandidate(vertices, width, height, depth, rot, out Candidate cand);
            if (cand.Count > best.Count || (cand.Count == best.Count && cand.Score > best.Score))
            {
                best = cand;
            }
        }

        if (best.Count <= 0 || best.Nx <= 0 || best.Ny <= 0 || best.Nz <= 0)
        {
            Console.WriteLine(0);
            return;
        }

        var stdout = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput(), new UTF8Encoding(false), 1 << 20);
        stdout.NewLine = "\n";

        stdout.WriteLine(best.Count);

        // Stream the placements directly to stdout to avoid large memory spikes.
        for (long iz = 0; iz < best.Nz; iz++)
        {
            double baseZ = iz * best.Dz;
            for (long iy = 0; iy < best.Ny; iy++)
            {
                double baseY = iy * best.Dy;
                for (long ix = 0; ix < best.Nx; ix++)
                {
                    double tx = ix * best.Dx + best.OffsetX;
                    double ty = baseY + best.OffsetY;
                    double tz = baseZ + best.OffsetZ;
                    WritePlacement(stdout, tx, ty, tz, best.Rot.Qw, best.Rot.Qx, best.Rot.Qy, best.Rot.Qz);
                }
            }
        }

        stdout.Flush();
    }

    private static void EvaluateCandidate(Vec3[] vertices, double width, double height, double depth, Rotation rot, out Candidate cand)
    {
        double minX = double.PositiveInfinity, minY = double.PositiveInfinity, minZ = double.PositiveInfinity;
        double maxX = double.NegativeInfinity, maxY = double.NegativeInfinity, maxZ = double.NegativeInfinity;

        for (int i = 0; i < vertices.Length; i++)
        {
            Vec3 v = vertices[i];
            double x = rot.M00 * v.X + rot.M01 * v.Y + rot.M02 * v.Z;
            double y = rot.M10 * v.X + rot.M11 * v.Y + rot.M12 * v.Z;
            double z = rot.M20 * v.X + rot.M21 * v.Y + rot.M22 * v.Z;

            if (x < minX) minX = x;
            if (y < minY) minY = y;
            if (z < minZ) minZ = z;
            if (x > maxX) maxX = x;
            if (y > maxY) maxY = y;
            if (z > maxZ) maxZ = z;
        }

        double dx = Math.Max(maxX - minX, Eps);
        double dy = Math.Max(maxY - minY, Eps);
        double dz = Math.Max(maxZ - minZ, Eps);

        long nx = SafeFloorDiv(width, dx);
        long ny = SafeFloorDiv(height, dy);
        long nz = SafeFloorDiv(depth, dz);

        long count = SafeMul(SafeMul(nx, ny), nz);
        double score = count;
        score -= 1e-12 * (dx + dy + dz);
        score -= 1e-15 * (dx * dy * dz);

        cand = new Candidate
        {
            Rot = rot,
            Dx = dx,
            Dy = dy,
            Dz = dz,
            OffsetX = -minX,
            OffsetY = -minY,
            OffsetZ = -minZ,
            Nx = nx,
            Ny = ny,
            Nz = nz,
            Count = count,
            Score = score
        };
    }

    private static long SafeFloorDiv(double a, double b)
    {
        if (!(a > 0) || !(b > 0))
        {
            return 0;
        }

        double q = (a + 1e-10) / b;
        if (q <= 0) return 0;
        if (q >= long.MaxValue) return long.MaxValue;
        return (long)Math.Floor(q);
    }

    private static long SafeMul(long a, long b)
    {
        if (a == 0 || b == 0) return 0;
        if (a > 0 && b > 0 && a > long.MaxValue / b) return long.MaxValue;
        return a * b;
    }

    private static void WritePlacement(StreamWriter stdout, double tx, double ty, double tz, double qw, double qx, double qy, double qz)
    {
        stdout.Write(tx.ToString("G17", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
        stdout.Write(' ');
        stdout.Write(ty.ToString("G17", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
        stdout.Write(' ');
        stdout.Write(tz.ToString("G17", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
        stdout.Write(' ');
        stdout.Write(qw.ToString("G17", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
        stdout.Write(' ');
        stdout.Write(qx.ToString("G17", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
        stdout.Write(' ');
        stdout.Write(qy.ToString("G17", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
        stdout.Write(' ');
        stdout.WriteLine(qz.ToString("G17", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
    }

    private static Rotation[] BuildRotations()
    {
        int[][] perms =
        {
            new[] { 0, 1, 2 },
            new[] { 0, 2, 1 },
            new[] { 1, 0, 2 },
            new[] { 1, 2, 0 },
            new[] { 2, 0, 1 },
            new[] { 2, 1, 0 }
        };

        int[] signs = { -1, 1 };
        var rotations = new Rotation[24];
        int idx = 0;

        foreach (var perm in perms)
        {
            for (int sx = 0; sx < 2; sx++)
            {
                for (int sy = 0; sy < 2; sy++)
                {
                    for (int sz = 0; sz < 2; sz++)
                    {
                        int[] col0 = BasisVector(perm[0], signs[sx]);
                        int[] col1 = BasisVector(perm[1], signs[sy]);
                        int[] col2 = BasisVector(perm[2], signs[sz]);

                        int m00 = col0[0], m01 = col1[0], m02 = col2[0];
                        int m10 = col0[1], m11 = col1[1], m12 = col2[1];
                        int m20 = col0[2], m21 = col1[2], m22 = col2[2];

                        int det = Determinant(m00, m01, m02, m10, m11, m12, m20, m21, m22);
                        if (det == 1)
                        {
                            rotations[idx++] = new Rotation(m00, m01, m02, m10, m11, m12, m20, m21, m22);
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        if (idx != 24)
        {
            Array.Resize(ref rotations, idx);
        }
        return rotations;
    }

    private static int[] BasisVector(int axis, int sign)
    {
        switch (axis)
        {
            case 0: return new[] { sign, 0, 0 };
            case 1: return new[] { 0, sign, 0 };
            default: return new[] { 0, 0, sign };
        }
    }

    private static int Determinant(int m00, int m01, int m02, int m10, int m11, int m12, int m20, int m21, int m22)
    {
        return m00 * (m11 * m22 - m12 * m21)
             - m01 * (m10 * m22 - m12 * m20)
             + m02 * (m10 * m21 - m11 * m20);
    }

    private static (double qw, double qx, double qy, double qz) MatrixToQuaternion(
        int m00, int m01, int m02,
        int m10, int m11, int m12,
        int m20, int m21, int m22)
    {
        double qw, qx, qy, qz;
        double trace = m00 + m11 + m22;
        if (trace > 0)
        {
            double s = Math.Sqrt(trace + 1.0) * 2.0;
            qw = 0.25 * s;
            qx = (m21 - m12) / s;
            qy = (m02 - m20) / s;
            qz = (m10 - m01) / s;
        }
        else if (m00 > m11 && m00 > m22)
        {
            double s = Math.Sqrt(1.0 + m00 - m11 - m22) * 2.0;
            qw = (m21 - m12) / s;
            qx = 0.25 * s;
            qy = (m01 + m10) / s;
            qz = (m02 + m20) / s;
        }
        else if (m11 > m22)
        {
            double s = Math.Sqrt(1.0 + m11 - m00 - m22) * 2.0;
            qw = (m02 - m20) / s;
            qx = (m01 + m10) / s;
            qy = 0.25 * s;
            qz = (m12 + m21) / s;
        }
        else
        {
            double s = Math.Sqrt(1.0 + m22 - m00 - m11) * 2.0;
            qw = (m10 - m01) / s;
            qx = (m02 + m20) / s;
            qy = (m12 + m21) / s;
            qz = 0.25 * s;
        }

        double norm = Math.Sqrt(qw * qw + qx * qx + qy * qy + qz * qz);
        if (norm > 0)
        {
            qw /= norm;
            qx /= norm;
            qy /= norm;
            qz /= norm;
        }
        return (qw, qx, qy, qz);
    }

    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream;
        private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _len;
        private int _ptr;

        public FastScanner(Stream stream)
        {
            _stream = stream;
        }

        private int Read()
        {
            if (_ptr >= _len)
            {
                _len = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _ptr = 0;
                if (_len <= 0) return -1;
            }
            return _buffer[_ptr++];
        }

        public string Next()
        {
            var sb = new StringBuilder();
            int c;
            do
            {
                c = Read();
            } while (c != -1 && c <= 32);

            while (c != -1 && c > 32)
            {
                sb.Append((char)c);
                c = Read();
            }
            return sb.ToString();
        }

        public int NextInt()
        {
            return int.Parse(Next(), CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);
        }

        public double NextDouble()
        {
            return double.Parse(Next(), CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);
        }
    }
}

Test 13: Longest Common Substring (C++) Score: 0.00 / 12
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Longest Common Substring problem in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-3 short strings (10-50 chars each)
- **Medium**: 3-5 medium strings (100-500 chars each)
- **Large**: 5-10 long strings (1000-5000 chars each)
- **Extreme**: 100 very long strings (10MB+ each), massive search space

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N (number of strings)
Next N lines: one string per line

**Output format (stdout):**
One line: the longest common substring
If no common substring exists, output an empty line.

**Example:**
Input:
3
abcdef
xbcdey
zbcdew

Output:
bcde

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number and length of strings
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 300 seconds even for very long strings
3. **Correctness**: Must find the actual longest common substring

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given several strings, find the longest chunk of text that appears identically in every one of them. For example, if the strings are “abcdef” and “xbcdey”, the answer is “bcde”.

Naïve approaches check every possible substring and become far too slow on large inputs. Subpasses increase the string lengths into the millions, so the AI must use efficient data structures or hashing. The baseline uses a simple library call that times out on the bigger cases.

 0.0000
[3 short strings (7 chars)] Invalid: Result must be string, got dict

Longest Common Substring - 3 short strings (7 chars)

Algorithm: Longest Common Substring solver Adaptive: 1) Generalized suffix automaton on the shortest string for smaller inputs. 2) Rolling-hash intersection with binary search for larger inputs.

View Code (12965 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <cstdint>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <string>
#include <utility>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

static inline void strip_cr(string& s) {
    if (!s.empty() && s.back() == '\r') s.pop_back();
}

struct HashPair {
    uint64_t a = 0;
    uint64_t b = 0;

    bool operator==(const HashPair& other) const noexcept {
        return a == other.a && b == other.b;
    }
};

static inline uint64_t mix64(uint64_t x) noexcept {
    x ^= x >> 33;
    x *= 0xff51afd7ed558ccdULL;
    x ^= x >> 33;
    x *= 0xc4ceb9fe1a85ec53ULL;
    x ^= x >> 33;
    return x;
}

struct HashPairHasher {
    size_t operator()(const HashPair& p) const noexcept {
        return static_cast<size_t>(mix64(p.a ^ (p.b + 0x9e3779b97f4a7c15ULL + (p.a << 6) + (p.a >> 2))));
    }
};

class PairSet {
public:
    void init(size_t expected) {
        size_t cap = 1;
        size_t need = expected + expected / 2 + 8;
        while (cap < need) cap <<= 1;
        keys.assign(cap, HashPair{});
        used.assign(cap, 0);
        mask = cap - 1;
        filled = 0;
    }

    bool insert(const HashPair& x) {
        size_t pos = HashPairHasher{}(x) & mask;
        while (used[pos]) {
            if (keys[pos] == x) return false;
            pos = (pos + 1) & mask;
        }
        used[pos] = 1;
        keys[pos] = x;
        ++filled;
        return true;
    }

    bool contains(const HashPair& x) const {
        size_t pos = HashPairHasher{}(x) & mask;
        while (used[pos]) {
            if (keys[pos] == x) return true;
            pos = (pos + 1) & mask;
        }
        return false;
    }

    vector<HashPair> values() const {
        vector<HashPair> out;
        out.reserve(filled);
        for (size_t i = 0; i < used.size(); ++i) {
            if (used[i]) out.push_back(keys[i]);
        }
        return out;
    }

    size_t size() const { return filled; }

private:
    vector<HashPair> keys;
    vector<uint8_t> used;
    size_t mask = 0;
    size_t filled = 0;
};

struct RollingHash {
    static constexpr uint64_t B1 = 911382323ULL;
    static constexpr uint64_t B2 = 972663749ULL;

    uint64_t pow1 = 1;
    uint64_t pow2 = 1;

    explicit RollingHash(int len = 0) {
        pow1 = ipow(B1, len);
        pow2 = ipow(B2, len);
    }

    static uint64_t ipow(uint64_t base, int exp) {
        uint64_t result = 1;
        uint64_t cur = base;
        while (exp > 0) {
            if (exp & 1) result *= cur;
            cur *= cur;
            exp >>= 1;
        }
        return result;
    }

    HashPair hash_window(const string& s, int start, int len) const {
        uint64_t h1 = 0, h2 = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < len; ++i) {
            unsigned char c = static_cast<unsigned char>(s[start + i]);
            uint64_t v = static_cast<uint64_t>(c) + 1;
            h1 = h1 * B1 + v;
            h2 = h2 * B2 + v;
        }
        return {h1, h2};
    }

    void init_first(const string& s, int len, uint64_t& h1, uint64_t& h2) const {
        h1 = 0;
        h2 = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < len; ++i) {
            unsigned char c = static_cast<unsigned char>(s[i]);
            uint64_t v = static_cast<uint64_t>(c) + 1;
            h1 = h1 * B1 + v;
            h2 = h2 * B2 + v;
        }
    }

    HashPair next_hash(uint64_t h1, uint64_t h2, unsigned char out, unsigned char in) const {
        uint64_t ov = static_cast<uint64_t>(out) + 1;
        uint64_t iv = static_cast<uint64_t>(in) + 1;
        h1 = h1 * B1 + iv - ov * pow1;
        h2 = h2 * B2 + iv - ov * pow2;
        return {h1, h2};
    }
};

static bool build_hash_set(const string& s, int L, PairSet& set) {
    if (L > static_cast<int>(s.size())) return false;
    size_t windows = s.size() - L + 1;
    set.init(windows);
    RollingHash rh(L);
    uint64_t h1 = 0, h2 = 0;
    rh.init_first(s, L, h1, h2);
    set.insert({h1, h2});
    for (size_t i = L; i < s.size(); ++i) {
        unsigned char out = static_cast<unsigned char>(s[i - L]);
        unsigned char in = static_cast<unsigned char>(s[i]);
        HashPair hp = rh.next_hash(h1, h2, out, in);
        h1 = hp.a;
        h2 = hp.b;
        set.insert(hp);
    }
    return true;
}

static bool contains_hash(const string& s, int L, const HashPair& target) {
    if (L > static_cast<int>(s.size())) return false;
    RollingHash rh(L);
    uint64_t h1 = 0, h2 = 0;
    rh.init_first(s, L, h1, h2);
    if (HashPair{h1, h2} == target) return true;
    for (size_t i = L; i < s.size(); ++i) {
        unsigned char out = static_cast<unsigned char>(s[i - L]);
        unsigned char in = static_cast<unsigned char>(s[i]);
        HashPair hp = rh.next_hash(h1, h2, out, in);
        h1 = hp.a;
        h2 = hp.b;
        if (hp == target) return true;
    }
    return false;
}

static size_t locate_hash(const string& s, int L, const HashPair& target) {
    if (L > static_cast<int>(s.size())) return string::npos;
    RollingHash rh(L);
    uint64_t h1 = 0, h2 = 0;
    rh.init_first(s, L, h1, h2);
    if (HashPair{h1, h2} == target) return 0;
    for (size_t i = L; i < s.size(); ++i) {
        unsigned char out = static_cast<unsigned char>(s[i - L]);
        unsigned char in = static_cast<unsigned char>(s[i]);
        HashPair hp = rh.next_hash(h1, h2, out, in);
        h1 = hp.a;
        h2 = hp.b;
        if (hp == target) return i - L + 1;
    }
    return string::npos;
}

static bool check_length(const vector<string>& strs, const vector<int>& order, int L, vector<HashPair>* survivors) {
    if (L == 0) {
        if (survivors) survivors->clear();
        return true;
    }
    const string& base = strs[order[0]];
    if (L > static_cast<int>(base.size())) {
        if (survivors) survivors->clear();
        return false;
    }

    PairSet current;
    if (!build_hash_set(base, L, current)) {
        if (survivors) survivors->clear();
        return false;
    }
    vector<HashPair> cand = current.values();
    if (cand.empty()) {
        if (survivors) survivors->clear();
        return false;
    }

    for (size_t k = 1; k < order.size() && !cand.empty(); ++k) {
        const string& s = strs[order[k]];
        if (L > static_cast<int>(s.size())) {
            cand.clear();
            break;
        }

        if (cand.size() == 1) {
            if (!contains_hash(s, L, cand[0])) {
                cand.clear();
            }
            continue;
        }

        PairSet cur;
        if (!build_hash_set(s, L, cur)) {
            cand.clear();
            break;
        }

        vector<HashPair> next;
        next.reserve(cand.size());
        for (const auto& h : cand) {
            if (cur.contains(h)) next.push_back(h);
        }
        cand.swap(next);
    }

    if (survivors) *survivors = cand;
    return !cand.empty();
}

struct SuffixAutomaton {
    struct State {
        int link = -1;
        int len = 0;
        int firstpos = -1;
        vector<pair<unsigned char, int>> next;
    };

    vector<State> st;
    int last = 0;

    explicit SuffixAutomaton(size_t reserve_states = 0) {
        st.reserve(reserve_states);
        st.push_back(State{});
    }

    int get(int v, unsigned char c) const {
        const auto& nxt = st[v].next;
        for (const auto& e : nxt) {
            if (e.first == c) return e.second;
        }
        return -1;
    }

    void set_edge(int v, unsigned char c, int to) {
        auto& nxt = st[v].next;
        for (auto& e : nxt) {
            if (e.first == c) {
                e.second = to;
                return;
            }
        }
        nxt.push_back({c, to});
    }

    void extend(unsigned char c, int pos) {
        int cur = static_cast<int>(st.size());
        st.push_back(State{});
        st[cur].len = st[last].len + 1;
        st[cur].firstpos = pos;

        int p = last;
        while (p != -1 && get(p, c) == -1) {
            set_edge(p, c, cur);
            p = st[p].link;
        }

        if (p == -1) {
            st[cur].link = 0;
        } else {
            int q = get(p, c);
            if (st[p].len + 1 == st[q].len) {
                st[cur].link = q;
            } else {
                int clone = static_cast<int>(st.size());
                st.push_back(st[q]);
                st[clone].len = st[p].len + 1;
                while (p != -1 && get(p, c) == q) {
                    set_edge(p, c, clone);
                    p = st[p].link;
                }
                st[q].link = st[cur].link = clone;
            }
        }
        last = cur;
    }
};

static string solve_sam(const vector<string>& strs, int refIdx) {
    const string& ref = strs[refIdx];
    if (ref.empty()) return string();

    SuffixAutomaton sam(ref.size() * 2 + 1);
    for (int i = 0; i < static_cast<int>(ref.size()); ++i) {
        sam.extend(static_cast<unsigned char>(ref[i]), i);
    }

    int sz = static_cast<int>(sam.st.size());
    vector<int> order(sz);
    vector<int> cnt(ref.size() + 1, 0);
    for (int i = 0; i < sz; ++i) ++cnt[sam.st[i].len];
    for (size_t i = 1; i < cnt.size(); ++i) cnt[i] += cnt[i - 1];
    for (int i = 0; i < sz; ++i) order[--cnt[sam.st[i].len]] = i;

    vector<int> common(sz, 0);
    for (int i = 0; i < sz; ++i) common[i] = sam.st[i].len;

    vector<int> best(sz, 0);
    for (int idx = 0; idx < static_cast<int>(strs.size()); ++idx) {
        if (idx == refIdx) continue;
        fill(best.begin(), best.end(), 0);
        int v = 0, l = 0;
        const string& s = strs[idx];
        for (char chRaw : s) {
            unsigned char ch = static_cast<unsigned char>(chRaw);
            int nxt = sam.get(v, ch);
            while (v != 0 && nxt == -1) {
                v = sam.st[v].link;
                l = sam.st[v].len;
                nxt = sam.get(v, ch);
            }
            if (nxt != -1) {
                v = nxt;
                ++l;
            } else {
                v = 0;
                l = 0;
            }
            if (l > best[v]) best[v] = l;
        }

        for (int oi = sz - 1; oi > 0; --oi) {
            int vtx = order[oi];
            int p = sam.st[vtx].link;
            if (p >= 0) {
                int val = best[vtx];
                if (val > sam.st[p].len) val = sam.st[p].len;
                if (val > best[p]) best[p] = val;
            }
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < sz; ++i) {
            if (best[i] < common[i]) common[i] = best[i];
        }
    }

    int bestState = 0;
    int bestLen = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < sz; ++i) {
        if (common[i] > bestLen) {
            bestLen = common[i];
            bestState = i;
        }
    }
    if (bestLen <= 0) return string();
    int endPos = sam.st[bestState].firstpos;
    int startPos = endPos - bestLen + 1;
    if (startPos < 0 || startPos + bestLen > static_cast<int>(ref.size())) return string();
    return ref.substr(startPos, bestLen);
}

static string solve_hash(const vector<string>& strs, int refIdx, const vector<int>& order) {
    int low = 0;
    int high = static_cast<int>(strs[order[0]].size());
    for (int idx : order) high = min(high, static_cast<int>(strs[idx].size()));

    while (low < high) {
        int mid = (low + high + 1) >> 1;
        if (check_length(strs, order, mid, nullptr)) low = mid;
        else high = mid - 1;
    }

    if (low == 0) return string();

    vector<HashPair> survivors;
    if (!check_length(strs, order, low, &survivors) || survivors.empty()) return string();

    const string& base = strs[order[0]];
    for (const auto& h : survivors) {
        size_t pos = locate_hash(base, low, h);
        if (pos == string::npos) continue;
        string candidate = base.substr(pos, low);
        bool ok = true;
        for (int idx : order) {
            if (strs[idx].find(candidate) == string::npos) {
                ok = false;
                break;
            }
        }
        if (ok) return candidate;
    }

    return string();
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int N;
    if (!(cin >> N)) return 0;
    string dummy;
    getline(cin, dummy);

    vector<string> strs;
    strs.reserve(max(0, N));
    for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
        string s;
        getline(cin, s);
        strip_cr(s);
        strs.push_back(std::move(s));
    }

    if (strs.empty()) {
        cout << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    vector<int> order(strs.size());
    for (int i = 0; i < static_cast<int>(strs.size()); ++i) order[i] = i;
    sort(order.begin(), order.end(), [&](int a, int b) {
        if (strs[a].size() != strs[b].size()) return strs[a].size() < strs[b].size();
        return a < b;
    });

    if (strs[order[0]].empty()) {
        cout << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    const size_t SAM_LIMIT = 300000;
    string ans;
    if (strs[order[0]].size() <= SAM_LIMIT) {
        ans = solve_sam(strs, order[0]);
    } else {
        ans = solve_hash(strs, order[0], order);
    }

    cout << ans << '\n';
    return 0;
}

Test 14: Minesweeper Solver (C#) Score: 0.00 / 10
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a Minesweeper solver in C# that must handle ANY board complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale.

- **Trivial**: Small boards (5x5-8x8), simple patterns, few mines
- **Extreme**: Massive boards (10000x10000), very complex constraint satisfaction problems

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: H W (height and width)
Next H lines: the board row strings (each W characters)

**Cell meanings:**
- ' ' (space): Unknown/unrevealed cell - could be mine or safe
- '0'-'8': Revealed safe cell showing count of adjacent mines (8 neighbors)
- '*': Flagged mine (known mine location)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: N (number of safe cells found)
Next N lines: x y (column row, 0-indexed)
Only output cells that are currently unknown (' ') and provably safe.
Do NOT guess - only return cells you can prove are safe.

**Example:**
For a board where cell (0,0) shows '1' and has one adjacent mine at (1,0):
Neighbors (0,1) and (0,2) are guaranteed safe.

**Constraints:**
- Return as many provably safe cells as possible
- Never return a cell that could be a mine
- Must handle varying board sizes and mine densities efficiently

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on board complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a partially-revealed Minesweeper board, deduce which hidden cells are safe to click and which contain mines. Each visible number tells you exactly how many of its neighbours are mined — the AI must combine these clues to make guaranteed-safe moves without guessing.

Larger boards and denser mine layouts make the logic harder. The problem is NP-complete in general, but clever constraint reasoning can solve most practical boards. The baseline uses only the simplest single-cell deduction rules.

Minesweeper Solver - 8x8 board, 10 mines

View Code (18380 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;

public static class Program
{
    private const byte Unknown = 0;
    private const byte Mine = 1;
    private const byte Safe = 2;

    private sealed class Constraint
    {
        public int Target;
        public int[] Vars = Array.Empty<int>();
    }

    public static void Main()
    {
        string? first = Console.ReadLine();
        if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(first))
        {
            Console.WriteLine(0);
            return;
        }

        int[] hw = ParseInts(first);
        int h = hw[0];
        int w = hw[1];

        string[] board = new string[h];
        for (int r = 0; r < h; r++)
        {
            string? line = Console.ReadLine();
            if (line == null) line = string.Empty;
            if (line.Length < w) line = line.PadRight(w, ' ');
            else if (line.Length > w) line = line.Substring(0, w);
            board[r] = line;
        }

        int[] dr = { -1, -1, -1, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1 };
        int[] dc = { -1, 0, 1, -1, 1, -1, 0, 1 };

        var varIndex = new Dictionary<int, int>();
        var varRows = new List<int>();
        var varCols = new List<int>();
        var constraints = new List<Constraint>();

        for (int r = 0; r < h; r++)
        {
            string row = board[r];
            for (int c = 0; c < w; c++)
            {
                char ch = row[c];
                if (ch < '0' || ch > '8') continue;

                int clue = ch - '0';
                int flags = 0;
                var vars = new List<int>(8);

                for (int k = 0; k < 8; k++)
                {
                    int nr = r + dr[k];
                    int nc = c + dc[k];
                    if ((uint)nr >= (uint)h || (uint)nc >= (uint)w) continue;
                    char adj = board[nr][nc];
                    if (adj == '*')
                    {
                        flags++;
                    }
                    else if (adj == ' ')
                    {
                        int key = nr * w + nc;
                        if (!varIndex.TryGetValue(key, out int id))
                        {
                            id = varRows.Count;
                            varIndex[key] = id;
                            varRows.Add(nr);
                            varCols.Add(nc);
                        }
                        vars.Add(id);
                    }
                }

                if (vars.Count == 0) continue;

                int target = clue - flags;
                if (target < 0 || target > vars.Count)
                {
                    continue;
                }

                constraints.Add(new Constraint
                {
                    Target = target,
                    Vars = vars.ToArray()
                });
            }
        }

        int varCount = varRows.Count;
        int constraintCount = constraints.Count;
        var varToConstraints = new List<int>[varCount];
        for (int i = 0; i < varCount; i++) varToConstraints[i] = new List<int>();
        for (int i = 0; i < constraintCount; i++)
        {
            foreach (int v in constraints[i].Vars)
            {
                varToConstraints[v].Add(i);
            }
        }

        byte[] state = new byte[varCount];
        int[] knownMines = new int[constraintCount];
        int[] unknownCounts = new int[constraintCount];
        for (int i = 0; i < constraintCount; i++) unknownCounts[i] = constraints[i].Vars.Length;

        var queue = new Queue<int>();
        bool conflict = false;

        void EnqueueAssign(int v, byte newState)
        {
            if (conflict) return;
            byte cur = state[v];
            if (cur == newState) return;
            if (cur != Unknown)
            {
                conflict = true;
                return;
            }

            state[v] = newState;
            queue.Enqueue(v);

            foreach (int c in varToConstraints[v])
            {
                unknownCounts[c]--;
                if (newState == Mine) knownMines[c]++;
                if (knownMines[c] > constraints[c].Target || knownMines[c] + unknownCounts[c] < constraints[c].Target)
                {
                    conflict = true;
                    return;
                }
            }
        }

        void Propagate()
        {
            while (queue.Count > 0 && !conflict)
            {
                int v = queue.Dequeue();
                foreach (int c in varToConstraints[v])
                {
                    if (conflict) return;
                    int target = constraints[c].Target;
                    if (knownMines[c] == target)
                    {
                        foreach (int u in constraints[c].Vars)
                        {
                            if (state[u] == Unknown) EnqueueAssign(u, Safe);
                            if (conflict) return;
                        }
                    }
                    else if (knownMines[c] + unknownCounts[c] == target)
                    {
                        foreach (int u in constraints[c].Vars)
                        {
                            if (state[u] == Unknown) EnqueueAssign(u, Mine);
                            if (conflict) return;
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < constraintCount; i++)
        {
            if (constraints[i].Target == 0)
            {
                foreach (int v in constraints[i].Vars) EnqueueAssign(v, Safe);
            }
            else if (constraints[i].Target == constraints[i].Vars.Length)
            {
                foreach (int v in constraints[i].Vars) EnqueueAssign(v, Mine);
            }
        }
        Propagate();

        if (!conflict && varCount > 0)
        {
            var visitedVar = new bool[varCount];
            var visitedConstraint = new bool[constraintCount];
            var components = new List<(List<int> Vars, List<int> Constraints)>();

            for (int start = 0; start < varCount; start++)
            {
                if (state[start] != Unknown || visitedVar[start]) continue;

                var compVars = new List<int>();
                var compConstraints = new List<int>();
                var stack = new Stack<(bool IsVar, int Id)>();
                stack.Push((true, start));
                visitedVar[start] = true;

                while (stack.Count > 0)
                {
                    var item = stack.Pop();
                    if (item.IsVar)
                    {
                        int v = item.Id;
                        compVars.Add(v);
                        foreach (int c in varToConstraints[v])
                        {
                            if (unknownCounts[c] == 0 || visitedConstraint[c]) continue;
                            visitedConstraint[c] = true;
                            stack.Push((false, c));
                        }
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        int c = item.Id;
                        compConstraints.Add(c);
                        foreach (int v in constraints[c].Vars)
                        {
                            if (state[v] != Unknown || visitedVar[v]) continue;
                            visitedVar[v] = true;
                            stack.Push((true, v));
                        }
                    }
                }

                components.Add((compVars, compConstraints));
            }

            const int ExactThreshold = 20;
            const int SubsetThreshold = 80;

            foreach (var comp in components)
            {
                if (comp.Vars.Count == 0 || comp.Constraints.Count == 0) continue;

                if (comp.Vars.Count <= ExactThreshold)
                {
                    RunExactComponent(comp.Vars, comp.Constraints, constraints, state, EnqueueAssign);
                    Propagate();
                    if (conflict) break;
                }
                else if (comp.Vars.Count <= SubsetThreshold)
                {
                    RunSubsetComponent(comp.Vars, comp.Constraints, constraints, state, EnqueueAssign);
                    Propagate();
                    if (conflict) break;
                }
            }
        }

        var output = new List<(int X, int Y)>();
        for (int i = 0; i < varCount; i++)
        {
            if (state[i] == Safe)
            {
                output.Add((varCols[i], varRows[i]));
            }
        }

        output.Sort((a, b) => a.Y != b.Y ? a.Y.CompareTo(b.Y) : a.X.CompareTo(b.X));

        using var writer = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput());
        writer.WriteLine(output.Count);
        foreach (var p in output)
        {
            writer.WriteLine($"{p.X} {p.Y}");
        }
    }

    private static void RunSubsetComponent(
        List<int> compVars,
        List<int> compConstraints,
        List<Constraint> constraints,
        byte[] state,
        Action<int, byte> enqueueAssign)
    {
        int n = compVars.Count;
        int m = compConstraints.Count;
        var localOfGlobal = new Dictionary<int, int>(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) localOfGlobal[compVars[i]] = i;

        var localConstraints = new int[m][];
        var localTargets = new int[m];
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            Constraint c = constraints[compConstraints[i]];
            var arr = new int[c.Vars.Length];
            int idx = 0;
            foreach (int gv in c.Vars)
            {
                if (state[gv] != Unknown) continue;
                arr[idx++] = localOfGlobal[gv];
            }
            if (idx != arr.Length)
            {
                Array.Resize(ref arr, idx);
            }
            Array.Sort(arr);
            localConstraints[i] = arr;
            localTargets[i] = c.Target;
            foreach (int gv in c.Vars)
            {
                if (state[gv] == Mine) localTargets[i]--;
            }
        }

        var localSafe = new bool[n];
        var localMine = new bool[n];

        int[] order = new int[m];
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) order[i] = i;
        Array.Sort(order, (a, b) => localConstraints[a].Length.CompareTo(localConstraints[b].Length));

        for (int oi = 0; oi < m; oi++)
        {
            int i = order[oi];
            int[] a = localConstraints[i];
            for (int oj = oi + 1; oj < m; oj++)
            {
                int j = order[oj];
                int[] b = localConstraints[j];
                if (a.Length > b.Length) continue;

                if (TrySubset(a, b, out int[] diff))
                {
                    int dt = localTargets[j] - localTargets[i];
                    int ds = diff.Length;
                    if (dt == 0)
                    {
                        foreach (int v in diff) localSafe[v] = true;
                    }
                    else if (dt == ds)
                    {
                        foreach (int v in diff) localMine[v] = true;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            if (localSafe[i] && !localMine[i]) enqueueAssign(compVars[i], Safe);
            else if (localMine[i] && !localSafe[i]) enqueueAssign(compVars[i], Mine);
        }
    }

    private static bool TrySubset(int[] small, int[] large, out int[] diff)
    {
        var temp = new List<int>(large.Length - small.Length);
        int i = 0, j = 0;
        while (i < small.Length && j < large.Length)
        {
            if (small[i] == large[j])
            {
                i++;
                j++;
            }
            else if (small[i] > large[j])
            {
                temp.Add(large[j]);
                j++;
            }
            else
            {
                diff = Array.Empty<int>();
                return false;
            }
        }

        if (i < small.Length)
        {
            diff = Array.Empty<int>();
            return false;
        }

        while (j < large.Length)
        {
            temp.Add(large[j]);
            j++;
        }

        diff = temp.ToArray();
        return true;
    }

    private static void RunExactComponent(
        List<int> compVars,
        List<int> compConstraints,
        List<Constraint> constraints,
        byte[] state,
        Action<int, byte> enqueueAssign)
    {
        int n = compVars.Count;
        int m = compConstraints.Count;
        var localOfGlobal = new Dictionary<int, int>(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) localOfGlobal[compVars[i]] = i;

        var localToGlobal = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) localToGlobal[i] = compVars[i];

        var localConstraints = new int[m][];
        var localTargets = new int[m];
        var varToConstraints = new List<int>[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) varToConstraints[i] = new List<int>();

        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            Constraint c = constraints[compConstraints[i]];
            var arr = new int[c.Vars.Length];
            int idx = 0;
            foreach (int gv in c.Vars)
            {
                if (state[gv] != Unknown) continue;
                int lv = localOfGlobal[gv];
                arr[idx++] = lv;
            }
            if (idx != arr.Length) Array.Resize(ref arr, idx);
            Array.Sort(arr);
            localConstraints[i] = arr;
            int target = c.Target;
            foreach (int gv in c.Vars)
            {
                if (state[gv] == Mine) target--;
            }
            localTargets[i] = target;
            foreach (int lv in arr) varToConstraints[lv].Add(i);
        }

        byte[] assign = new byte[n];
        int[] knownMines = new int[m];
        int[] unknownCounts = new int[m];
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) unknownCounts[i] = localConstraints[i].Length;
        bool[] canBeSafe = new bool[n];
        bool[] canBeMine = new bool[n];

        bool Assign(int v, byte newState, Queue<int> q, byte[] a, int[] km, int[] uc)
        {
            byte cur = a[v];
            if (cur == newState) return true;
            if (cur != Unknown)
            {
                return false;
            }

            a[v] = newState;
            q.Enqueue(v);
            foreach (int c in varToConstraints[v])
            {
                uc[c]--;
                if (newState == Mine) km[c]++;
                if (km[c] > localTargets[c] || km[c] + uc[c] < localTargets[c])
                {
                    return false;
                }
            }

            return true;
        }

        bool Propagate(byte[] a, int[] km, int[] uc, Queue<int> q)
        {
            while (q.Count > 0)
            {
                int v = q.Dequeue();
                foreach (int c in varToConstraints[v])
                {
                    if (km[c] == localTargets[c])
                    {
                        foreach (int u in localConstraints[c])
                        {
                            if (a[u] == Unknown && !Assign(u, Safe, q, a, km, uc)) return false;
                        }
                    }
                    else if (km[c] + uc[c] == localTargets[c])
                    {
                        foreach (int u in localConstraints[c])
                        {
                            if (a[u] == Unknown && !Assign(u, Mine, q, a, km, uc)) return false;
                        }
                    }
                }
            }

            return true;
        }

        void Search(byte[] a, int[] km, int[] uc, Queue<int> q)
        {
            if (!Propagate(a, km, uc, q)) return;

            int next = -1;
            int bestDegree = -1;
            for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
            {
                if (a[i] != Unknown) continue;
                int degree = varToConstraints[i].Count;
                if (degree > bestDegree)
                {
                    bestDegree = degree;
                    next = i;
                }
            }

            if (next == -1)
            {
                for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
                {
                    if (a[i] == Safe) canBeSafe[i] = true;
                    else if (a[i] == Mine) canBeMine[i] = true;
                }
                return;
            }

            {
                var a0 = (byte[])a.Clone();
                var km0 = (int[])km.Clone();
                var uc0 = (int[])uc.Clone();
                var q0 = new Queue<int>();
                if (Assign(next, Safe, q0, a0, km0, uc0)) Search(a0, km0, uc0, q0);
            }

            {
                var a1 = (byte[])a.Clone();
                var km1 = (int[])km.Clone();
                var uc1 = (int[])uc.Clone();
                var q1 = new Queue<int>();
                if (Assign(next, Mine, q1, a1, km1, uc1)) Search(a1, km1, uc1, q1);
            }
        }

        {
            var a = (byte[])assign.Clone();
            var km = (int[])knownMines.Clone();
            var uc = (int[])unknownCounts.Clone();
            var q = new Queue<int>();
            for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
            {
                if (localTargets[i] == 0)
                {
                    foreach (int v in localConstraints[i]) if (!Assign(v, Safe, q, a, km, uc)) return;
                }
                else if (localTargets[i] == localConstraints[i].Length)
                {
                    foreach (int v in localConstraints[i]) if (!Assign(v, Mine, q, a, km, uc)) return;
                }
            }

            Search(a, km, uc, q);
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            if (!canBeMine[i] && canBeSafe[i])
            {
                enqueueAssign(localToGlobal[i], Safe);
            }
            else if (canBeMine[i] && !canBeSafe[i])
            {
                enqueueAssign(localToGlobal[i], Mine);
            }
        }
    }

    private static int[] ParseInts(string s)
    {
        var parts = s.Split(new[] { ' ', '\t' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        int[] values = new int[parts.Length];
        for (int i = 0; i < parts.Length; i++) values[i] = int.Parse(parts[i]);
        return values;
    }
}

Test 15: Tetrahedron Shadow Covering (Rust) Score: 11.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Shadow Covering problem with tetrahedrons in Rust.

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with polygons ranging from simple shapes to very complex geometries.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: NV (number of polygon vertices)
Next NV lines: x y (polygon vertices on z=0 plane)
Last line: sx sy sz (sun direction vector, points from sun toward ground)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: count (number of tetrahedrons)
Next count lines: px py pz qw qx qy qz scale
px,py,pz: center position
qw,qx,qy,qz: rotation quaternion (w is scalar)
scale: uniform scale factor

**Standard Tetrahedron Reference:**
Vertices (before transform):
[1.0, 0.0, -0.707]
[-1.0, 0.0, -0.707]
[0.0, 1.0, 0.707]
[0.0, -1.0, 0.707]

**Constraints:**
- Combined shadows must completely cover target polygon
- Tetrahedrons must not intersect each other in 3D
- Maximum scale factor per tetrahedron: 10.0
- Minimize number of tetrahedrons

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Be aware that default warnings are enabled and will cause a compilation failure,
so ensure that you write warning-free code.

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.

Place 3D tetrahedra (triangular pyramids) above a flat surface so that their shadows, cast by a directional light, completely cover a given 2D target shape. Use as few tetrahedra as possible while covering every part of the target.

The AI must reason about 3D-to-2D projection, rotation, and coverage geometry. Subpasses use more complex target shapes and trickier light angles. The baseline uses a simple grid of tetrahedra above the target.

 1.0000
[3x3 square, sun straight down] 100% coverage with 1 tetrahedrons, Time: 0.0s

Shadow Covering - 3x3 square, sun straight down

View Code (2642 chars)
use std::fmt::Write as _;
use std::io::{self, Read};

fn main() {
    let mut input = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
    let mut it = input.split_whitespace();

    let nv: usize = match it.next() {
        Some(v) => v.parse().unwrap(),
        None => return,
    };

    let mut min_x = f64::INFINITY;
    let mut min_y = f64::INFINITY;
    let mut max_x = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
    let mut max_y = f64::NEG_INFINITY;

    for _ in 0..nv {
        let x: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let y: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        min_x = min_x.min(x);
        min_y = min_y.min(y);
        max_x = max_x.max(x);
        max_y = max_y.max(y);
    }

    let sx: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let sy: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let sz: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    let width = max_x - min_x;
    let height = max_y - min_y;
    let span = width.max(height);

    let mut out = String::new();
    let scale = 10.0_f64;
    let qw = 1.0_f64;
    let qx = 0.0_f64;
    let qy = 0.0_f64;
    let qz = 0.0_f64;

    if sz.abs() < 1e-9 || (nv <= 8 && span <= 18.0) {
        let cx = (min_x + max_x) * 0.5;
        let cy = (min_y + max_y) * 0.5;
        let cz = 0.0_f64;
        writeln!(&mut out, "1").unwrap();
        writeln!(
            &mut out,
            "{:.10} {:.10} {:.10} {:.10} {:.10} {:.10} {:.10} {:.10}",
            cx, cy, cz, qw, qx, qy, qz, scale
        )
        .unwrap();
        print!("{out}");
        return;
    }

    let inv_sz = 1.0 / sz;
    let alpha = sx * inv_sz;
    let beta = sy * inv_sz;

    let step = if span <= 60.0 { 8.0_f64 } else { 10.0_f64 };
    let margin = 20.0_f64;
    let height_step = 50.0_f64;

    let cols = ((width + 2.0 * margin) / step).ceil().max(1.0) as usize;
    let rows = ((height + 2.0 * margin) / step).ceil().max(1.0) as usize;
    let count = cols * rows;

    writeln!(&mut out, "{count}").unwrap();

    let start_x = min_x - margin;
    let start_y = min_y - margin;
    let mut idx = 0usize;
    for r in 0..rows {
        let gy = start_y + (r as f64 + 0.5) * step;
        for c in 0..cols {
            let gx = start_x + (c as f64 + 0.5) * step;
            let pz = idx as f64 * height_step;
            let px = gx + alpha * pz;
            let py = gy + beta * pz;
            writeln!(
                &mut out,
                "{:.10} {:.10} {:.10} {:.10} {:.10} {:.10} {:.10} {:.10}",
                px, py, pz, qw, qx, qy, qz, scale
            )
            .unwrap();
            idx += 1;
        }
    }

    print!("{out}");
}

Test 16: Job-Shop Scheduling (C++) Score: 0.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Job-Shop Scheduling problem in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to extremely large scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-3 jobs, 2-3 machines, 5-10 tasks total
- **Extreme**: 1000+ jobs, 50+ machines, 50k+ tasks total

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: J M (number of jobs, number of machines)
For each job:
Line: T (number of tasks in this job)
Next T lines: machine duration

**Output format (stdout):**
For each job (J lines total):
T pairs per line: start_time machine start_time machine ... (all tasks for that job)

**Example:**
Input:
2 2
2
0 3
1 2
2
1 2
0 4

Output:
0 0 3 1
0 1 5 0

(Job 0: task 0 starts at t=0 on M0, task 1 at t=3 on M1)
(Job 1: task 0 starts at t=0 on M1, task 1 at t=5 on M0)

**Key Constraints:**
1. Each task in a job must run on its specified machine
2. Tasks within a job must execute in order (task i before task i+1)
3. A machine can only run one task at a time
4. Tasks cannot be interrupted once started

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.
 Prompt Changes:

Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Schedule a set of jobs across several machines to finish everything as quickly as possible. Each job is a sequence of tasks that must run in order, and each task needs a specific machine — but a machine can only do one thing at a time.

This classic factory-scheduling problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number of jobs and machines. The baseline greedily starts whichever task can begin earliest.

 0.0000
[3 jobs, 2 machines] Compilation failed:
tmpsrix0z0n.cpp
C:\Users\Ash\AppData\Local\Temp\tmpsrix0z0n.cpp(1): fatal error C1083: Cannot open include file: 'bits/stdc++.h': No such file or directory

Job-Shop Scheduling - 3 jobs, 2 machines

View Code (2310 chars)
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

struct Task {
    int machine;
    int duration;
};

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int J, M;
    if (!(cin >> J >> M)) return 0;

    vector<vector<Task>> jobs(J);
    long long totalTasks = 0;
    for (int j = 0; j < J; ++j) {
        int T;
        cin >> T;
        totalTasks += T;
        jobs[j].resize(T);
        for (int i = 0; i < T; ++i) {
            cin >> jobs[j][i].machine >> jobs[j][i].duration;
        }
    }

    // Adaptive choice:
    // - For small instances, keep the code path simple and compact.
    // - For large instances, use the same O(T) schedule construction, which
    //   is already streaming-friendly and memory efficient.
    const bool smallInstance = totalTasks <= 2000;

    vector<vector<long long>> starts(J);
    for (int j = 0; j < J; ++j) starts[j].resize(jobs[j].size());

    vector<long long> jobReady(J, 0);
    vector<long long> machineReady(max(0, M), 0);

    if (smallInstance) {
        // Job-major greedy schedule:
        // schedule each job in input order, task by task.
        for (int j = 0; j < J; ++j) {
            for (size_t i = 0; i < jobs[j].size(); ++i) {
                const Task& t = jobs[j][i];
                long long s = max(jobReady[j], machineReady[t.machine]);
                starts[j][i] = s;
                long long e = s + t.duration;
                jobReady[j] = e;
                machineReady[t.machine] = e;
            }
        }
    } else {
        // Same schedule, but kept in the fastest possible tight loop for
        // large task counts.
        for (int j = 0; j < J; ++j) {
            long long jr = 0;
            for (size_t i = 0; i < jobs[j].size(); ++i) {
                const Task& t = jobs[j][i];
                long long s = jr > machineReady[t.machine] ? jr : machineReady[t.machine];
                starts[j][i] = s;
                jr = s + t.duration;
                machineReady[t.machine] = jr;
            }
            jobReady[j] = jr;
        }
    }

    for (int j = 0; j < J; ++j) {
        for (size_t i = 0; i < jobs[j].size(); ++i) {
            if (i) cout << ' ';
            cout << starts[j][i] << ' ' << jobs[j][i].machine;
        }
        cout << '\n';
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 17: 3D AABB Bin Packing (C#) Score: 0.00 / 8
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Bin Packing problem with axis-aligned boxes in C#.

Your program must work on all scales, from 10,000 boxes in 1,000,000 cubic units
down to 4 boxes in a 10x10x10 container.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N W H D (number of boxes, container width, height, depth)
Next N lines: w h d (box dimensions)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: packed_count
Next packed_count lines: box_index x y z (0-indexed box index and corner position)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container size and number of boxes
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for very large containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize number of boxes packed while ensuring valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Boxes must be entirely within container bounds
- No two boxes may overlap
- Boxes are axis-aligned (no arbitrary rotation, but you may reorder w/h/d)
- Maximize number of boxes packed

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**

Pack a collection of rectangular boxes into a shipping container, fitting as much total volume in as possible. Boxes may be rotated to any axis-aligned orientation, but cannot overlap or stick out of the container.

This 3D packing problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number and variety of box sizes. The baseline uses a greedy first-fit approach.

 0.0000

Determining projects to restore...
